answer: 3-nitrobenzylthioinosine (3H-NBI) is a potent adenosine uptake blocker whose binding to brain and cardiac membranes can be inhibited by dihydropyridine calcium antagonists in a non-competitive manner.

The purine disulfide analog of ATP, 6,6'-dithiobis(inosinyl imidodiphosphate), inactivates bovine cardiac myosin ATPase activity with a Ki of 130 µM and a half-life of 8.2 min at 0°C when used at saturating inhibitor concentrations.
Inosinyl imidodiphosphate can form mixed disulfide bonds with key cysteines on myosin, heavy meromyosin, and subfragment one, leading to inactivation of EDTA ATPase activities.
Binding of [8-3H]adenylyl imidodiphosphate to heavy meromyosin or subfragment one results in tight binding with an average dissociation constant of 5 × 10⁻⁷ M.
Inosinyl imidodiphosphate (S2P-PNP) is a site-specific analog of ATP that inactivates myosin's ATPase activities by forming mixed disulfides with key cysteine residues.
N6-benzyladenosine dialdehyde inhibits L1210 cell proliferation, with the degree of inhibition dependent on its concentration (1.0 µM).

In chorda tympani and glossopharyngeal fibres, responses to the mixture of MSG and GMP are positively correlated with responses to IMP (r = 0.627 for chorda tympani, r = 0.586 for glossopharyngeal, P < 0.05).
Inosine 5'-methylphosphonate is a simple noncompetitive inhibitor of IMP dehydrogenase and adenylosuccinate synthetase.

Phenylaminoadenosine, an adenosine A2 receptor agonist at 0.25 µM, does not improve postischemic left ventricular developed pressure (LVDP) in isolated rat hearts after global no-flow ischemia, with values at 52 ± 5% of preischemic.
Phenylaminoadenosine, an adenosine A1 receptor agonist, does not improve postischemic left ventricular developed pressure (LVDP) in isolated rat hearts after global no-flow ischemia.
Substitution of Mn2+ for Mg2+ and ITP for ATP in myosin assays decreases the rate of oxygen exchange (kcat) by 10-fold.

Using MnITP as a substrate for myosin allows both myosin heads to operate at the same rate of oxygen exchange (kcat) regardless of actin activation, overcoming inhibition of Head 2 even in the absence of actin.
6-[(4-nitrobenzyl)thio]-9-beta-D-ribofuranosylpurine is a ligand for T. gondii adenosine kinase and can be selectively metabolized to toxic nucleotides within the parasite.
9-(3-nitrobenzyl)adenine is NI-oxidised significantly slower than benzyladenine by a hamster hepatic microsomal system.
9-(3-nitrobenzyl)adenine undergoes N1-oxidation in normal hamster hepatic microsomes, but at a lower rate compared to 9-(2-nitrobenzyl)adenine and 9-benzyladenine.
9-(3-nitrobenzyl)adenine is metabolized by animal hepatic microsomes to 9-(3-nitrobenzyl)adenine-N1-oxide.
TNP-dGMP is used as an adsorbent for antibody purification, with 0.1 M TNP-dGMP eluting 162 pg antibody per ml antiserum from pooled hyperimmune anti-TNP-sDNA antisera.
Among N 6 -phenyladenosine derivatives, 4-chloro- and 4-bromo-N 6 phenyladenosines exhibit EC 50 values of about 0.5 µM at the A 2B receptor in a cyclic AMP production assay with CHO-A 2B cells.

Ribose diinosine monophosphate forms a single N-(7)-N(7)-chelated complex with cis-[Pt(NH3)9] when reacted, as demonstrated by CD spectral changes including increased ellipticity in the 275-nm region.
2'-C-hydroxymethyl adenosine exhibits potent anti-HCV activity.

Stilbene isothiocyanate can be used to label the N-terminus of thymopentin analogues, resulting in a mono-labeled analogue (SITS-TP-5) that retains the ability to induce Thy 1.2 surface marker expression on nude mouse prothymocytes and inhibit radiolabeled thymopentin binding to human lymphocytes.
Chlorostyrene is efficiently epoxidized to the corresponding epoxide with about 90% enantiomeric excess using catalyst 4b and iodosylbenzene in dichloromethane.

(E)-2-oxo-4-phenyl-3-butenoic acid type inhibitors/alternate substrates enable detection of an enzyme-bound enamine intermediate at 440 nm in both pdc1 and pdc1-6 fusion enzymes of brewers' yeast pyruvate decarboxylase.
4-Hydroxystilbene is an effective antioxidant against both AAPH- and iron-induced peroxidation of rat liver microsomes.

(Z)-cinnamyl bromide serves as a substrate in palladium-catalyzed allylic substitution reactions with organogold(I) reagents, resulting in α-coupling products with high yield and complete isomerization to the most stable trans isomer.
(Z)-cinnamyl bromide reacts with arylgold(I) phosphanes in a palladium-catalyzed cross-coupling at 80 °C to form α-substituted products.

The cinnamylthio- 4-substituted pyrazolo[3,4-d]pyrimidine ribonucleoside serves as a substrate for adenosine kinases from Eimeria tenella and Eimeria acervulina, but is not detectably phosphorylated by the Eimeria brunetti enzyme.

Cinnamylzinc chloride is a racemic allylic zinc reagent that adds smoothly to aromatic, heteroaromatic, and alkyl imines, providing homoallylic amines in excellent yields (93–95%) and with high diastereoselectivity (g98:2).
The hydroxystyryl-acrylonitrile compound LS104 inhibits proliferation and induces potent cytotoxic effects in FLT3-expressing leukemic cells in vitro.
Combination of LS104 with chemotherapeutic agents markedly enhances cytotoxic effects in FLT3-mutant leukemic cells.
LS104 inhibits phosphorylation of FLT3-ITD and its downstream targets in FLT3-mutant leukemic cells.
Benzylidenethiazolidinedione is a compound with antimicrobial activity.
Benzylidene thiazolidinedione exhibits improved antibacterial activity compared to benzyl thiazolidinedione.
Upon acylation by papain, peptides containing styryldehydroalanine exhibit a large red shift in absorption maxima, with a shift of 59 nm (from 318 to 377 nm) for SDA peptides.
Diphenylphosphinylallenes serve as substrates in Rh-catalyzed intermolecular hydroalkoxylation with phenols to produce vinyl ethers in high yields and enantioselectivities.
Ethyl trans-cinnamate is an antifeedant compound isolated from lodgepole pine bark that significantly reduces pine weevil feeding in laboratory bioassays.
Analogues of 2-styryl-3,5-dihydro-4H-imidazol-4-one act as reactive oxygen species (ROS) inducers, leading to increased oxidative stress and apoptosis in cancer cells.

Iodoxybenzene is formed as a side-product during alkene epoxidation when the oxoiron centre in the porphyrin catalyst is not readily accessible to the substrate, leading to competition with epoxidation.

Methylbenzylidene camphor demonstrates good photostability as a sunscreen agent, with a linear HPLC assay detection limit of 0.1 µg/ml and a calculated half-life in the studied concentration range (25–60 µM).

1, 3-diphenyl-3-acetoxy-1-propene undergoes palladium(II)-catalyzed allylic alkylation with dimethyl malonate in the presence of optically active N-tosylsulfimides and sulfimides as chiral ligands, yielding the alkylation product quantitatively and with up to 90% enantiomeric excess.
8-ethoxy-9-ethyladenine is a ligand with the smallest 8-substituent and exhibits one of the highest affinities for the A 2A adenosine receptor among tested adenine derivatives.
8-ethoxy- 9-ethyladenine reverses haloperidol-induced catalepsy in rat models of Parkinson's disease.
8-ethoxy- 9-ethyladenine is an 8-substituted 9-ethyladenine derivative characterized as an adenosine receptor antagonist in vitro.
8-ethoxy-9-ethyladenine is a high-affinity antagonist of the human A2A adenosine receptor, with selectivity versus other receptor subtypes in the range of 10 to 600.

In unilaterally 6-hydroxydopamine-lesioned rats, 8-ethoxy- 9-ethyladenine potentiates the effect of l-dihydroxy-phenylalanine (l-DOPA) on turning behaviour and induces contralateral turning behaviour in l-DOPA-sensitised rats.
Substitution of adenosine with 8-methyladenosine in the 2-5A trimer results in a product that is more active than parent 2-5A in activating RNase L.
1-(6-amino-9H-purin-9-yl) propan-2-one can be enzymatically reduced by (R)-selective Codexis KRED P1B02 to produce a chiral intermediate for tenofovir synthesis.
A basic or neutral group at the 6-position of the purine nucleus is essential for the inhibitory activity of 6-amino-9-(3-hydroxypropyl)purine against adenosine deaminase.
8-pentylaminoadenosine may act as a partial agonist in vitro for the adenosine A2A receptor, as it causes GTP shift and maximal inhibition of adenylate cyclase even at high concentrations.

8-phenethyladenosine is an 8-alkoxyadenosine derivative with a medium-sized alkyl group, which is suggested to efficiently serve both duplex stability and immunostimulation prevention in siRNA guide strands.

9-benzyl-8-azaadenine is an adenosine receptor ligand that is inactive when unsubstituted at the 2 position but shows improved activity when an unsubstituted phenyl group is present at the 2 position.
2'-Me-CPA is a selective A1 adenosine receptor agonist that has been evaluated for affinity and selectivity at all cloned human adenosine receptor subtypes.
ApbA stimulates [35S]GTvS binding to human and rat lung membranes in a manner similar to other diadenosine polyphosphates.
9-(2-phosphonoethoxypropyl)adenine (PMPA) is an acyclic nucleotide analogue investigated for its activity in experimental allergic encephalomyelitis and rheumatoid arthritis models.
9-(6-deoxy-β-D-galactopyranosyl)adenine was synthesized from D-fucose and its biological activity confirmed.

9-(6-deoxy-β-D-galactopyranosyl)adenine inhibits the growth of L1210 leukemia cells in culture by about 75% at 48 hours, with 50% inhibition at a concentration 6 times higher than the inhibitory concentration (M).
9-β-D-Fucopyranosyladenine is a hexopyranosyl nucleoside that has been reported to inhibit the development of leukemia cells L 1210.
Adenosine 5'-monophosphoramidate (NH2pA) reacts with adenosine 5'-phosphorimidazolide (ImpA) in the presence of montmorillonite to form dinucleotides.
Adenosine diphosphorothioate (ADP-a-S) acts as a modulator of bovine heart NAD-isocitrate dehydrogenase with kinetic constants similar to ADP, indicating similar allosteric activation activity.
Ap7A confers catalytic activity to otherwise inactive CBS-PPase at low Mg2+ concentrations.
Melophlin C is a tetramic acid derivative that can exist as a mixture of 5(S)- and 5(R)-enantiomers at the C-5 chiral center, as determined by ESI LC/MS analysis.

Melophlin C displays pronounced antibacterial activity against Bacillus subtilis and Staphylococcus aureus, as well as antifungal activity against Candida albicans, and is inactive against HL60 and TF-1 cells.
Mycolipanolic acid is present in both the wild type and msl3 mutant polyacylated trehalose fractions, though in much smaller amounts in the msl3 mutant.
In the pks2 mutant lacking hydroxyphthioceranic acids, a small amount of 14C label from propionic acid incorporation is found in mycolipanolic acids, which are shorter than phthioceranic acids.
Chrysotrione B is a shorter-chain homologue of the cyclic b,b'-triketone intermediate formed during the biosynthesis of hygrophorone B.
A biogenetic route for hygrophorone B(12) biosynthesis involves a chrysotrione B homologue as part of the metabolic pathway.
Chrysotrinone B is an acylcyclopentenedione isolated from Hygrophorus chrysodon that exhibits antifungal activity against Fusarium verticillioides.
9-methylnonadecane is a branched-chain alkane with biological activity against young larvae of Culex p. quinquefusciutus.

9-methylnonadecane is a branched-chain alkane with strong biological activity against Culex pipiens quinquefusciutus mosquito larvae, showing LC50 values of 1–2 ppm for acute toxicity and LCw values of about or less than 10 ppm.
3-methylhexadecanoate serves as a substrate for ϩ CO 2 (carbon dioxide) β-oxidation in peroxisomes.
3-methylhexadecanoic acid cannot undergo ␤-oxidation due to the presence of a methyl group in the 3-position and is first shortened by one carbon atom via ␣-oxidation.
The subcellular localization of ␣-oxidation for both R- and S-isomers of 3-methylhexadecanoic acid is peroxisomal in rat liver.
3-methylhexadecanoic acid undergoes ␣-oxidation in rat liver and hepatocytes, with both R- and S-isomers showing comparable rates of ␣-oxidation in intact hepatocytes and rat liver homogenates.
Verticilide B1 is a cyclodepsipeptide compound isolated from the fungal strain FKI-2679 and serves as an inhibitor of both ACAT1 and ACAT2 isozymes in cell-based assays.
For isoamyl stearate synthesis, enzyme loading beyond 10 mg has no effect on conversion when using 10 mg acid, 6 µL alcohol, and 50°C for 8 hours.
Isoamyl stearate can be synthesized via lipase-catalyzed esterification in supercritical carbon dioxide, achieving a maximum conversion of 53% when Lipolase 100T is used as the catalyst.
Isoamyl stearate is an ester produced by the enzymatic reaction of stearic acid and isoamyl alcohol, catalyzed by lipases from Candida Antarctica, T. langinosus, and Candida rugosa.
L-Ascorbyl laurate has antioxidative activity as potent as ascorbyl stearate.
(R)-10-hydroxystearic acid is produced from oleic acid via enzymatic conversion catalyzed by oleate hydratase.
6-hydroxystearic acid is an unusual fatty acid detected in small amounts in the faeces of patients with steatorrhoea.
VO(6mpa)2 is a vanadium(IV)-picolinate complex that enhances Akt-dependent GSK3b phosphorylation in diabetic mice.
2-((4,6-dimethylpyrimidin-2-yl)thio)-N-phenylacetamide derivatives demonstrate high selectivity for SIRT2 over SIRT1 and SIRT3.
2-((4,6-Dimethylpyrimidin-2-yl)thio)-N-phenylacetamide derivatives are novel inhibitors of SIRT2.
The inhibitory effect of 3,4,5-trihydroxy-N-(8-hydroxyquinolin-2-yl)benzamide (SG-HQ2) on mast cell activation is mediated by reduction of nuclear factor-kappaB.

3,4,5-trihydroxy-N-(8-hydroxyquinolin-2-yl)benzamide (SG-HQ2) inhibits the expression of pro-inflammatory cytokines tumor necrosis factor-alpha, interleukin-1beta, interleukin-4, and interleukin-6 in activated mast cells at concentrations 100 times lower than gallic acid.

3,4,5-trihydroxy-N-(8-hydroxyquinolin-2-yl)benzamide (SG-HQ2) is a synthetic analogue of gallic acid that attenuates histamine release by reducing intracellular calcium in human and primary peritoneal mast cells.

[(1S,2S)-2-methyl-1-(2-pyridylcarbamoyl)-1-butyl]amide (GW3333) is a TACE/MMP inhibitor that induces human cytochrome P450 3A (CYP3A) expression in vitro, with CYP3A levels comparable to rifampicin at approximately 10 µM in human hepatocytes.
1-butanoyl-3-(2, 6-dimethylphenyl) thiourea (compound 3b) is an acetylcholinesterase (AChE) inhibitor with an IC50 of 8.92 ± 1.03 μM and shows 2.5-fold greater inhibition than neostigmine.
1-butanoyl-3-(2, 6-dimethylphenyl) thiourea (compound 3b) exhibits high selectivity for acetylcholinesterase (AChE) over butyrylcholinesterase (BuChE) with a selectivity index (SI) of 0.073.

2-(2-methylquinolin-4-ylamino)-N-phenylacetamide exhibits significantly greater antileishmanial activity than sodium antimony gluconate in reducing parasite load in the spleen and liver of hamster models at much lower concentrations.
2-(2-methylquinolin-4-ylamino)-N-phenylacetamide exhibits strong antileishmanial activity against L. donovani in both in vitro and in vivo assays.
2-(2-methylquinolin-4-ylamino)-N-phenylacetamide is a quinoline derivative that demonstrates significantly higher in vitro and in vivo antileishmanial activity than sodium antimony gluconate.

2-(2-methylquinolin-4-ylamino)-N-phenylacetamide inhibits the growth of Leishmania donovani promastigotes in vitro in a dose-dependent manner, with 66% inhibition on the second day and 92% and 95% inhibition on the fourth and seventh days at a concentration of 5.0 mg/mL.
4-bromopyridyl acetamide at the C7 position of the chromone scaffold is optimal for activity in a series of potent flavone-based inhibitors targeting ABL and ABL T315I.

2-benzoyl-6-methylpyridine thiosemicarbazone (6-MeBpT) is a novel thiosemicarbazone iron chelator whose analogues with a single methyl substituent on the pyridine ring exhibit the highest anti-proliferative activity among tested compounds.
Norleucine-naphthylamid is a substrate for arylaminopeptidases in bovine dental pulp and serum, exhibiting high enzymatic activity in both tissues.
Methionine-naphthylamid is a substrate for arylaminopeptidases in bovine dental pulp and serum, showing high enzymatic activity in both tissues.
In human skin fibroblasts, pyrene-C8:0 is a major β-oxidation product of pyrene-C12:0, with its fluorescent intensity increasing linearly up to 3 hours and 50 μM substrate concentration.

Pyrene octanoate, a long chain pyrenyl fatty acid, incorporates efficiently into phospholipids, mainly phosphatidylcholine and phosphatidylethanolamine, and neutral lipids such as di- and triglycerides and cholesterol esters in BHK fibroblasts.
N,N-diethylsalicylamide has been used as a ligand to promote copper-catalyzed N-arylation of aliphatic amines.

1-nitropyrene-9,10-quinone is a K-region derivative of 1-nitropyrene that is less mutagenic than 1-nitropyrene in the absence of $9 mix in S. typhimurium TA98 and TA100, but its mutagenic activity is increased in the presence of $9 mix.
4-nitropyrene 9,10-quinone is mutagenic in the bacterial strain TA98, both with and without S9 metabolic activation, but is much less mutagenic in TA98NR and TA98/1,8-DNP6 strains.

The inhibition of HIV-1 reverse transcriptase by nitrophenyl phenyl sulfone requires a protein segment between residues 158 and 190, and possibly an additional segment between residues 192 and 202, for sensitivity.
Erythrocytes from all three species metabolize o-DNB to S-(2-nitrophenyl)glutathione conjugates.
Nitrobenzamide derivatives, such as CB 1954, are substrates for DT diaphorase, and all tested co-factors for menadione reduction are equally effective co-factors for their reduction.

Nitrobenzamide CB1954 is a cytotoxic compound that interacts with DT-diaphorase, with its aziridinyl group pointing toward Tyr155 and its amide group near a hydrophobic pocket next to Tyr128 in the active site.
Nitrobenzamide derivatives function as photonucleases that can be conjugated to acridine derivatives to enhance DNA interaction and activity.
Nitrobenzamide derivatives, such as CB 1954, are substrates for DT diaphorase, and all tested co-factors for menadione reduction are equally effective co-factors for their reduction.

Nitrobenzamide CB1954 is a cytotoxic compound that interacts with DT-diaphorase, with its aziridinyl group pointing toward Tyr155 and its amide group near a hydrophobic pocket next to Tyr128 in the active site.
Nitrobenzamide derivatives function as photonucleases that can be conjugated to acridine derivatives to enhance DNA interaction and activity.

A dinitroazidophenol analog of cytochrome c, containing one to two dinitroazidophenyl groups per cytochrome c molecule, can be synthesized and used to covalently label cytochrome c oxidase in mitochondrial membranes upon irradiation.
Ac-ONp undergoes one-proton catalysis in deacylation reactions with chymotrypsin and elastase, exhibiting an overall solvent isotope effect of 2.45.
Lanthanum picrate acts as a neutral mononuclear ligand in the complex La(C6H2N3O7)3(C2H6O)3, coordinating to lanthanum(III).
Nitrobenzoylcoumarin derivatives can lower parasitaemia in mice infected with the Y strain of T. cruzi.
Modification of the allyl group on the C6 carbon of nitrobenzoylcoumarin to an n-propyl chain increases its activity against T. cruzi epimastigote, making it as active as benznidazole.
Nitrobenzoylcoumarin derivatives exhibit low toxicity and are effective against T. cruzi amastigote forms.
Livers of rabbits, dogs, and monkeys produce more 2,4-dinitrobenzyl alcohol under aerobic conditions during in vitro incubation with 2,4-DNT.
Methanol droplets containing 2% m-nitrobenzyl alcohol evaporate down to a fixed size and charge that remains constant with no observable discharges during Rayleigh discharge.

2,5-dinitrophenolate is an organic anion that, when bound to the periphery of cation-filled G-quadruplexes, does not effectively chelate separate G(8).M(2+) octamers via anion-nucleobase hydrogen bonds, resulting in higher lability and isomerization half-lives of approximately 30 minutes under the tested conditions.
Hydroxymethoxynitrobenzaldehyde derivatives, except for one, do not inhibit catechol-O-methyltransferase (COMT) at a concentration of 5 × 10⁻¹ M in vitro.
2-fluoropyridine can be synthesized from 2-chloropyridine or 2-bromopyridine using fluoride ion in dimethylsulfone at 200°C for more than 1 week, yielding 49% and 42% respectively.
2-fluoropyridine can be prepared from 2-nitropyridine in 60% yield by fluorodenitration using fluoride ion in HMPT at 160°C for 24 hours.
[18F]fluoropyridine derivatives can be efficiently eluted from C18 Sep-pak cartridges using CH2Cl2 as the solvent.
2-fluoropyridine is not suitable as a carbon source for growth in a minimal synthetic medium.

The cytotoxicity of platinum(II) complexes with 2-F-C5H4N as the pyridine ligand is not significantly affected by the nature of the platinum(II) complex, indicating that the substituent on the pyridine ligand has a relatively small effect on cytotoxicity under the tested conditions.

2-bromomethylpyridine reacts with Ni 0 (bpy) at a rate similar to that of 2-bromopyridine, indicating that the presence of a methyl group on the pyridine ring does not significantly affect reactivity toward Ni 0 (bpy).
The dissociation of tris-(2,2-bipyridyl) iron(II) is very slow at pH 6.96 in aqueous medium but is accelerated in Triton X-100/hexanol/cyclohexane reverse micellar medium.

Iron(II) tris-bipyridine complexes can exhibit diastereoselectivity in their solid-state structure, influenced by noncovalent interactions and solvent polarity, due to the chiral nature of the L-amino acid ligands.

Tris-(2,2'-bipyridyl) iron(II) dissociates in reverse micellar media with a first-order rate constant that decreases with increasing water activity (W) and is accelerated by Cl- and SCN- ions, forming ion pairs with these anions.
Os(bpy)2Cl2 acts as a mediator attached to polystyrene polymer, which is electrocatalytically reduced at 0.1 V to convert H2O2 to H2O in amperometric detection.

Osmium bis 2,2′-bipyridine chloride can extract photogenerated holes from the excited P700 site of photosystem I and transfer them to polymer-bound or diffuse-through Os(bpy)2Cl2, or directly to the backing electrode.

Os(bpy)2Cl-modified poly(vinyl)imidazole redox polymer hydrogels, when electrically contacting PSII in Thermosynechococcus elongatus, yield a 10-fold increase in photocurrent at 45 µA cm−2 and improve system lifetimes due to superior stability.
BPT is used at a final concentration of 6.2 mM in the optimized ultrafiltration assay for measuring TGA and BPT.
2,2'-bipyridyl iron is an iron chelator used to create iron-deficient conditions in bacterial cultures by binding iron ions.
On a millisecond time scale, RuBpy-mediated photoreduction of cytochrome oxidase is primarily confined to reduction of heme a, with much slower electron leak to Cu B (over minutes).
RuBpy is an electron donor that mediates single-electron photoreduction of the ferric cyanide complex of cytochrome oxidase, which is associated with a 45-second electrogenic phase.

The crystal structure of Ruthenium(II) tris (2,2'-bipyridyl) monochloride nonahydrate reveals that the chlorine anion forms a halogen hydrogen-bonded organic framework (XHOF) that contributes to the complex's stability.

Ruthenium(II) tris (2,2'-bipyridyl) monochloride nonahydrate is a ruthenium complex that acts as a photosensitizer, exhibiting cytotoxic activity against L6 cells and the ability to generate reactive oxygen species upon irradiation.

Currently, only two methods exist for selectively synthesizing fluoropyridazine: (1) displacement of a chlorine atom by a metal fluoride (requires harsh conditions and prior synthesis of chlorinated pyridazine) and (2) AgF2-mediated C-H fluorination at the C3/C6 position.
Fluoropyridazine can be obtained as a single regioisomer in 77% yield from the reaction of 1-phenyl-2,2-difluorocyclopropene with ethyl diazoacetate under basic conditions.
Dichlorocarbene adds to the formal 'anti-Bredt' type double bond of an aromatic ring in [5]metacyclophane in a 1,2-fashion, leading to the formation of a norcaradiene intermediate.

Polymeric aluminum ferric chloride flocculants, manufactured from leaching solutions of aluminum and iron from coal fly ash, can achieve 96.1% removal efficiency of suspended solids and 91.5% removal efficiency of oil in oil recovery wastewater from polymer flooding when the Al/Fe molar ratio is 20:1 and basicity is 70%.

Aluminium ferric chloride (PAFC) coagulation produces lower dissolved organic matter and microcystin levels and reduced C- and N-disinfection by-product formation in dewatering water from cyanobacteria-containing sludge compared to AlCl3 coagulation, even when sludge is stored for the same amount of time.

2-chloroiodosylbenzene is a meta-substituted iodosylbenzene derivative that acts as a more efficient catalyst for liver microsomal androstenedione hydroxylation than its meta- or para-substituted analogues.
1,2,4-trioxolane can be used as a reagent to selectively cleave the methyl glycoside linkage during acetolysis of ozonides derived from methyl glycosides.

In the shallow water-bearing zone of TCE-contaminated groundwater with added formate, trichlorofluoroethene (TCFE) is reductively dechlorinated to cis-dichloroethene, trans-dichloroethene, and (E)-1-chloro-2-fluoroethene, with transformation rates ranging from 0.053 to 0.30 µmol/L-day.

In the shallow water-bearing zone of TCE-contaminated groundwater with added formate, trichlorofluoroethene (TCFE) is reductively dechlorinated to cis-dichloroethene, trans-dichloroethene, and (E)-1-chloro-2-fluoroethene, with transformation rates ranging from 0.053 to 0.30 µmol/L-day.

DCMU inhibits oxygen evolution in chloroplasts, but does not significantly affect flash-induced luminescence at 0 °C, indicating it does not accelerate deactivation of S2 and S3 states in this context.

Ferric hydrogensulfate acts as a catalyst in the multi-component, one-pot condensation reaction for synthesizing amidoalkyl naphthols from beta-naphthol, aromatic aldehydes, and acetamide under solvent, solvent-free, and microwave conditions.

Ferric hydrogensulfate can be used as a catalyst in a one-pot multi-component condensation reaction to synthesize amidoalkyl naphthol derivatives from aryl aldehydes, 2-naphthol, and acetamide or acetonitrile.
Chlorodiazirine reagents enable a versatile ring expansion reaction of pyrrole and indole substrates by generating aryl carbynyl cation equivalents.
Dichloroacetonitrile is a water disinfectant by-product that acts as a direct-acting mutagen and induces DNA strand breaks in cultured human lymphoblastic cells.

Poly(chlorotrifluoroethylene) (PCTFE) can be surface-modified with alternating patterns of cell-adhesive peptides and nonadhesive polyethylene glycol (PEG) to mimic guidance signals for neuron adhesion and neurite outgrowth in vitro.
A dose of 500 pmol/kg 1,3-dichloro-2-bromopropane is required to induce an equivalent degree of testicular DNA damage as 170 pmol/kg l-bromo-2,3-dichloropropane in rats.

Tetrahydroxybenzene serves as a cosubstrate in the molybdenum-catalyzed transhydroxylation of pyrogallol to phloroglucinol, with the enzyme transferring a hydroxyl group from tetrahydroxybenzene to pyrogallol.
2-Benzyl-1,2,3,4-tetrahydroisoquinoline compounds are structurally similar to ticlopidine and exhibit antiplatelet activity.

N-(2-chloroethyl)-N-2-bromobenzyl-amine hydrochloride (DSP-4) is a noradrenergic-specific neurotoxin that, when administered to female canaries or starlings, causes a significant reduction in their behavioral responses to both masked and unmasked songs.
Mrp2 mediates the ATP-dependent transport of Ac-DCVC in the renal proximal tubule.

Piperidine-1-yloxyl derivatives substituted at the 4-position with hydrogen bonding hydroxy groups or ionic substituents can influence the viscosity and rotational properties of 1-alkyl-3-methylimidazolium bis(trifluoromethylsulfonylimide) ionic liquids.

Piperidinyloxyl is a synthetic spin label compound that can be injected into the carotid artery of anesthetized rats, and its ability to cross the blood-brain barrier and bind to brain tissue components can be evaluated using ESR spectroscopy.
Cyano-aci-nitroacetate, in its dianionic form, acts as a safe synthetic equivalent for nitroacetonitrile in Michael addition reactions to produce 5-acylated 3-cyanoisoxazoles.
Cyano-aci-nitroacetate can undergo intramolecular nucleophilic substitution of the chloro group by nitronate ion after Michael addition with alpha-chloro-alpha,beta-unsaturated ketone.

Cyano-aci-nitroacetate can undergo intramolecular nucleophilic substitution of the chloro group by nitronate ion after Michael addition with alpha-chloro-alpha,beta-unsaturated ketone.
Cyano-aci-nitroacetate can undergo intramolecular nucleophilic substitution of a chloro group by a nitronate ion after Michael addition with α-chloro-α,β-unsaturated ketone.
Ce(NH4)2(NO3)3 is used in equimolar amounts with EDTA to prepare a homogeneous Ce(IV)/EDTA complex for DNA modification at pH 7.0 and 37°C.
Strontium nitrate suppresses sensory irritation caused by structurally and biologically unrelated chemical irritants over a pH range of 0.6–12.
Inhalation exposure of mice to 241 Am(N03)3 at doses of 550 Bq results in a dose-dependent reduction in the total number of pulmonary alveolar macrophages (PAM).
The mechanism of WhiB3 4Fe-4S cluster degradation is similar to that of the fumarate nitrate regulator.
Fumarate nitrate regulation, mediated by the Fnr protein, is involved in the iron-dependent regulation of the MnSOD gene (sodA) in Escherichia coli.

FeNAc is associated with a first-order kinetic process in iron uptake by serum-transferrin, as observed by fluorescence and characterized by a reciprocal relaxation time independent of species concentration.
Nitrooxyalkyl moieties are incorporated into NO-donor antioxidants to provide vasodilator properties.

Nitroxy derivatives of lamtidine analogues act as histamine H2 receptor antagonists but are 10-fold less potent than analogues lacking NO-donor properties in gastroprotective activity against 0.6 N HCl-induced gastric lesions.
Nitrooxy functions as a nitric oxide (NO) donor moiety in the synthesis of alpha-tocopherol analogs with vasodilating and antiplatelet properties.
Nitrooxy serves as a nitric oxide-releasing group in alpha-tocopherol mimetics evaluated for their biological effects.

Nitromalic acid inhibits spontaneous and oxidant-induced lipid peroxidation (LPO) in rat brain homogenate, but its antioxidant activity is less pronounced than that of hypocard.
Nitromalic acid has been analyzed for antioxidant activity in the Fe(II)-induced lipid peroxidation reaction in rat brain homogenates at concentrations of 0.5–1.0 mM.

Cyclopropyl p-nitrophenyl ketone is used as a substrate in the nucleophilic aromatic substitution synthesis of no-carrier-added fluorine-18-labeled N-methylspiroperidol, providing a 10–15% radiochemical yield (based on [18F-] with mass <2 nmol and specific activity >10 Ci/mumol (EOB)).
2-amino-5-benzoylbenzimidazole is a metabolite of mebendazole and can be analyzed in poultry muscle by HPLC-MS/MS.
6-(2-amino-1H-benzo[d]imidazole-6-yl)quinazolin-4(3H)-one derivatives have been designed and synthesized as inhibitors of Aurora A kinase.
6-(2-amino-1H-benzo[d]imidazole-6-yl)-quinazolin-4(3H)-one derivatives have been identified as potential inhibitors of AURKA kinase activity.

6-(2-amino-1H-benzo[d]imidazole-6-yl)-quinazolin-4(3H) -one derivatives have been optimized as multi-targeted inhibitors for Aurora A, PI3Kalpha, and BRD4, showing anticancer activity against non-small cell lung cancer (NSCLC) cells.
6-(2-amino-1H-benzo[d] imidazole-6-yl)quinazolin-4(3H)-one derivatives have been evaluated for anti-proliferative activity against MDA-MB-231 TNBC cells.
Benzophenone disulfamate compounds exhibit high inhibitory potency against steroid sulfatase (estrone sulfatase, E.C. 3.1.6.2.).
Benzophenone bis-sulfamate derivatives are potent steroid sulfatase inhibitors with submicromolar IC50 values (e.g., 2.0 nM for 29f) against recombinant, purified steroid sulfatase.

3-(hydroxymethyl)benzophenone undergoes a highly efficient (Φ ∼ 0.6) intramolecular redox reaction in acidic (pH < 3) aqueous solution to form 3-formylbenzhydrol (95%) with trace amounts of the fully oxidized 3-formylbenzophenone (5%).
Benzimidazolecarboxylic thiophenol esters are cleaved by n-butylamine at a rate 45 times faster than aromatic thiophenol esters and 3 times faster than alkyl thiophenol esters.
3-benzoylphenylacetic acid undergoes efficient photodecarboxylation via its carboxylate form in aqueous solution, yielding the corresponding methyl-substituted aromatic ketone product.

Manganese tetrakis benzoic acid porphyrin at 10 μM improves endothelium-dependent relaxation in human saphenous vein endothelial function assays compared to control (57.0% vs 37.8%, P < 0.01) in an ex vivo model.

4-chloro-3-nitrophenyl(phenyl)methanone functions as a non-inhibitory pharmacological chaperone that binds near the HMBS active site, enhances HMBS enzyme levels and activity in liver lysates, and reduces urinary ALA and PBG excretion in Hmbs-deficient mice.
The 8-amino modification of 3-deazaguanine is considered an important synthetic target for developing 3-deazaguanine as an antitumor agent.
3,4,5-trimethoxybenzophenone arrangement confers the highest cytotoxicity activity among tested analogues in MDA-MB-231 triple-negative breast cancer cells.
Zoligratinib resistance can be promoted by certain FGFR2 mutations.
2-amino-5-benzoyl-4-(2-furyl)thiazole analogs with arylalkyl or heteroarylalkyl substituents at the 2-amino position can achieve over 500-fold selectivity for A2A over A1 adenosine receptors.
2-amino-5-benzoyl-4-(2-furyl)thiazoles are antagonists of the human A2A adenosine receptor, with potency measured by Ki values typically less than 100 nM for hydrophobic 2-amino substituents.

2-amino-5-benzoyl-4-(2-furyl)thiazole derivatives have been developed as selective AdoA2A receptor antagonists, with one substituted derivative showing a Ki of 12 nM at human AdoA2A and over 500-fold selectivity versus AdoA1R.

2-amino-5-benzoyl-4-(2-furyl) thiazole derivatives act as antagonists of the human A2A adenosine receptor, with the most active compound showing a Ki of 12 nM and 580-fold selectivity over the A1 adenosine receptor.

3-benzoyl-4-hydroxyisoquinolin-1(2H)-one is a scaffold with potential as an anti-Chagas agent, exhibiting trypanocidal activity against Trypanosoma cruzi that can surpass the reference drug benznidazole at certain concentrations.
Ile-His-Pro-Phe-His-Leu (Ang 5-10) is a fragment generated from Angiotensin I by the action of mMCP-4, but is present in much lower amounts than other fragments after 40 minutes of reaction.
Bz-Gly-His-Pro-Phe is a tripeptidyl substrate that is hydrolyzed by negligible amounts by hog lung and kidney kininase II.
The memory-enhancing effect of rubiscolin-6 is blocked by the ␦ opioid antagonist naltrindole.
The memory-enhancing effect of rubiscolin-6 is mediated via the opioid receptor, as it is blocked by the opioid antagonist naltrindole.

Rubiscolin-6 is a Rubisco-derived opioid peptide that enhances memory consolidation after intracerebroventricular administration, with the optimal dose for memory consolidation being 3 nmol/mouse and being dose-dependent and bell-shaped.
Rubiscolin-6 enhances memory consolidation in mice as assessed by a one-trial step-through passive avoidance test.
The ACE2 enzyme has potent cleaving activity on neocasomorphin.
ACE2 is capable of complete hydrolysis of Neocasomorphin.

SQNYPIVQ is a peptide substrate for HIV-1 protease, containing two phenylalanine residues added to create two cleavage segments (FFSQNY and PIVQ) of different lengths for nanopore-based enzymatic assays.
Neurotensin (8-13) is the minimal fragment of neurotensin required for high affinity binding to neurotensin receptors.
Neurotensin (8-13) can displace 18F-labeled peptides that bind to the neurotensin receptor 1 (NTR1) in HT-29 and WiDr cells in the nanomolar range (5–14 nM) in vitro.
Neurotensin does not inhibit cyclic AMP levels stimulated by forskolin in CHO-rNTR-10 cells.
Neurotensin induces a dose-dependent release of inositol phosphates in intact CHO-rNTR-10 cells, with an EC50 of 3 nM, indicating functional activation of the expressed neurotensin receptor.
Incorporation of non-natural analogues of arginine or lysine into neurotensin can increase receptor binding affinities and enhance partitioning through the blood-brain barrier.
Phe-Thr-Tyr-Val-Pro-Gly-Ala is an ACE inhibitory peptide isolated from Rhopilema esculentum protein hydrolysate, with an IC50 value of 21.15 µmol/L for ACE inhibitory activity.
Phe-Thr-Tyr-Val-Pro-Gly-Ala is a novel ACE inhibitory peptide produced from jellyfish (Rhopilema esculentum) using papain and other proteases, with an IC50 value of 21.15 µM for ACE inhibition.
Glu-Glu-Glu-Glu-Tyr-Pro-Met-Glu (MT peptide) inhibits chemotaxis of rabbit peritoneal leucocytes stimulated by the synthetic chemoattractant fMet-Leu-Phe.

Glu-Glu-Glu-Glu-Tyr-Pro-Met-Glu (MT peptide) inhibits chemotaxis of rabbit peritoneal leucocytes stimulated by fMet-Leu-Phe and blocks the release of arachidonic acid from phospholipids in neutrophils, indicating inhibition distal to chemotactic receptors.

Thr-Thr-His-Tyr-Gly-Ser-Leu-Pro-Gln-Lys is a 10-residue peptide derived from the basic protein (BP) that serves as a major encephalitogenic site in rabbits, capable of eliciting the autoimmune disease experimental autoimmune encephalomyelitis (EAE) at doses below 1 µg.
Synthetic peptides containing the sequence Thr-Thr-His-Tyr-Gly-Ser-Leu-Pro-Gln-Lys are moderately encephalitogenic.

N-(chloro-3-[11C]methoxyphenyl)-2-picolinamide is a carbon-11 labeled PET radioligand that selectively binds to mGlu4 receptors in the brain, showing fast brain uptake and accumulation in mGlu4-rich regions.
N-(chloro-3-[11C]methoxyphenyl)-2-picolinamide exhibits a shorter retention time in the brain compared to the mGlu5 PET tracer [18F]FPEB, which may affect imaging quality.
N-(3-bromophenyl)picolinamide acts as a bidentate ligand in rhodium(III) complexes, and its complexes exhibit cytotoxicity against cancer cell lines.
Substitution with a methoxy group at the 2-position of the benzoyl nucleus in 2-benzoylamino-3,5-dichloropyridine derivatives enhances HSV-1(MP) growth inhibition activity.

Picolinohydrazide-naphthol (HL) is a Schiff base reagent used for selective trace-level detection of CN- anions, producing a visual color change from colorless to yellow in DMSO/H2O (9:1, v/v) at pH 7.2 (HEPES buffer).

4i, [N-(3-chloro-2 methylphenyl) quinoxalin-2-carboxamide], is a carboxamide derivative synthesized as a 5-HT 3 receptor antagonist and was selected for its strong antidepressant-like effects in preliminary screening.

4i exhibits 5-HT 3 receptor antagonistic potential, as evaluated in a guinea pig ileum longitudinal muscle myenteric plexus preparation against the 5-HT 3 agonist 2-methyl-5-HT, and its pA2 value was determined.

N-(3-chloro-2-methylphenyl) quinoxalin-2-carboxamide modulates the serotonergic system via 5-HT3 receptor antagonism, as shown by synergistic effects with fluoxetine and potentiation of 5-hydroxytryptophan-induced head twitch response.

N-(3-chloro-2-methylphenyl) quinoxalin-2-carboxamide exhibits antidepressant-like and anti-anxiety-like effects in rodent models, including decreased immobility in the forced swim and tail suspension tests, increased exploratory behavior in the hole board test, and reversal of depressive anhedonia and anxious hyperactive behavior in chronic depression models.
N-(3-chloro-2-methylphenyl) quinoxalin-2-carboxamide is a novel 5-HT3 receptor antagonist with a pA2 of 7.6 in isolated guinea pig ileal longitudinal muscle-myenteric plexus in vitro.

Pyridine-2-carboxylate gold(III) acts as a catalyst for the cycloisomerization of optically active 1-cyclopropyl propargyl acetate, affording cyclopentanone with up to 61% enantiomeric excess under standard conditions.
Calcium dipicolinate (DPA-Ca) in Bacillus subtilis spores undergoes significant changes in amount during germination, as monitored by ATR/FTIR spectroscopy.
Iron(III) picolinate catalyzes the production of hydroxyl radicals in the presence of superoxide radical and hydrogen peroxide.
Iron(III) picolinate (Fe III (PA) 3) can serve as a catalyst for stereoselective allylic hydroxylation of steroids in the presence of hydrogen peroxide and acetonitrile.

Pyridine-2-carboxylate gold(III) acts as a catalyst for the cycloisomerization of optically active 1-cyclopropyl propargyl acetate, affording cyclopentanone with up to 61% enantiomeric excess under standard conditions.
Pyridine carboxylic acid phenyl ester exhibits superior skin permeation when delivered with liposomes.
di-2-pyridylketone dithiocarbamate (DpdtC) is an iron chelator that mobilizes iron from ferritin and exhibits antitumor effects against hepatoma cell lines HepG2 and Bel-7402.

Ferritinophagy mediated by Di-2-pyridylketone dithiocarbamate (DpdtC) triggers an iron-driven intralysosomal oxidative reaction, leading to lysosomal reactive oxygen species (ROS) generation, LMP change, and lipid peroxidation.
The anticancer activity of di-2-pyridylketone dithiocarbamate (DpdtC) against hepatoma cell lines is partly attributed to ferritinophagy-mediated lysosomal reactive oxygen species (ROS) generation.

Di-2-pyridylketone dithiocarbamate (DpdtC) is a novel iron chelator that can mobilize iron from ferritin and displays antitumor activity against hepatoma carcinoma cell lines, with IC50 values of 0.4 ± 0.2 µM for HepG2 and 3.5 ± 0.3 µM for Bel-7402.
5-hydroxyuracil is an oxidative DNA base modification that is recognized by the repair enzyme uracil DNA N-glycosylase.

5-hydroxyuracil is a substrate for endo III exonuclease, and its endo III processing kinetics show a Km value about 8-fold higher than that of thymine glycol (DHT) and a Vm value in the same range as thymine glycol (DHT).
The Michaelis-Menten kinetic constants (Km and Vm) for E. coli endonuclease III have been determined for 5-hydroxyuracil as a substrate.
5-hydroxyuracil is a radical oxidation product of cytosine in calf thymus DNA formed upon exposure to radiation-induced Ø OH radicals in aerated aqueous solution.

Cyclobutane thymine dimers are formed as photoproducts when benzophenone reacts with thymidine, but this process is thermodynamically disfavored and does not appear to be a major DNA damage mechanism.
Cyclobutane thymine can undergo photocycloreversion in the presence of oxidants or reductants in their excited states, leading to cleavage of the thymine dimer.
Cyclobutane thymine-containing dimers are DNA photoproducts that can be detected and quantified in DNA fractions using reverse phase HPLC and 'H radioactivity assays.
Cyclobutane thymine dimers are formed in DNA as a result of photosensitization by BP and its derivatives.
5-trichloroethyl uracil is a tentative reaction product identified when thymine reacts with chemically generated ·CCl3.
Bromoacetamidouracil liberates bromide ion after being shaken for 5 minutes in 3 N NaOH at room temperature.
5-bromoacetamidouracil does not inhibit the growth of Neurospora.
5-bromoacetamidouracil completely inhibits the growth of E. coli K-12 at various minimal concentrations.
Bromoacetamidouracil causes severe damage to Ehrlich ascites tumor cells when administered intraperitoneally at single doses of 2 or 3 mg in Swiss mice.
Dihydroxy-isobutyl-thymine is used as a substrate in the HSV1 thymidine kinase (PDB id 1E2P) structure for studying substrate binding in TK2.
Anilinouracil derivatives represent a novel class of antimicrobials with activity against gram-positive bacteria, including strains resistant to currently available agents.
Anilinouracil derivatives represent a unique class of inhibitors that target the DNA polymerase polC in gram-positive bacteria.
Anilinouracil derivatives are potent inhibitors of PolC activity, as detected by sensitive filter-binding and scintillation proximity assay methods.

Anilinouracil analogs inhibit the activity of the recombinant polC polymerase from Streptococcus pyogenes, as measured by inhibition of DNA synthesis in a filter-binding and homogeneous scintillation proximity assay formats.

Anilinouracil derivatives are a class of compounds that inhibit low G-C gram-positive DNA polymerase β (PolC) activity by competing with dGTP for binding to deoxycytidine residues on the DNA template at the enzyme's active site.
5-fluorouracil is a fluoropyrimidine chemotherapy drug that irreversibly inhibits thymidylate synthase, leading to cytotoxicity in normal cells.
7-dehydrobrefeldin A is a known metabolite isolated from the EtOAc extract of Penicillium sp. DT-F29.
7-dehydrobrefeldin A is a more potent form of Brefeldin A that induces loss of cis-Golgi cisternae and promotes the formation of ER-Golgi hybrid compartments in plant cells.
Pestalotioprolide E is a potent macrolide secondary metabolite derived from the endophytic fungus Pestalotiopsis microspora.
Macrosphelide L is a compound isolated from a strain of Periconia byssoides originally separated from the sea hare Aplysia kurodai.
Macrosphelide L is a cell-adhesion inhibitor isolated from the marine microorganism Periconia byssoides OUPS-N133.

Macrosphelide L is a cell-adhesion inhibitor isolated from the marine-derived fungus Periconia byssoides OUPS-N133 that inhibits the adhesion of human-leukemia HL-60 cells to human-umbilical-vein endothelial cells (HUVEC) more potently than herbimycin A.
Bicyclohept-2-en-6-one can undergo enantioselective conversion by CHMO to yield both 2-oxabicyclo[3.3.0]oct-6-en-3-one and 3-oxabicyclo[3.3.0]oct-6-en-2-one with high enantiomeric excess.
The estrogenic activity of β-trans-zearalenol, a reduction product of trans-zearalenone, exceeds that of trans-zearalenone by approximately a factor of 80.
β-trans-Zearalenol reaches maximum cis-Zearalenol formation after 48 hours of UV irradiation at 350 nm.

Zearalenone 14-glucuronide is a di-glucuronide binding metabolite of zearalenone, with a higher yield in swine compared to goats and cows, indicating the C-14 hydroxyl group is the principal site for glucuronide binding.

Zearalenone 14-glucuronide is a glucuronidated metabolite of Zearalenone formed in humans and animals, and has been quantified in naturally contaminated human urine samples from Cameroon and South Africa.

Zearalenone 14-glucuronide (ZEN-14-GlcA) is the predominant glucuronide metabolite of zearalenone formed in the presence of uridine 5'-diphosphate glucuronic acid (UDPGA) by hepatic microsomes from various species.
Zearalenone 14-glucuronide is a glucuronidated metabolite of zearalenone formed by Caco-2 cell metabolism.
Zearalenone 14-glucuronide is detected in urine at concentrations up to 81 ng/mL 24 hours after oral administration of ZEN-14-S.
Cnidilide, a component of Cnidii Rhizoma (Senkyu) fraction 5, contributes to the inhibition of late phase and very late phase skin reactions in mice after dinitrophenol challenge.
Cnidilide, as the main component of fraction 5 from Cnidii Rhizoma extract, inhibits both late-phase primary (LPR) and very late-phase primary (vLPR) reactions in a triphasic skin assay in mice.
Cnidilide is a component of fraction 5 of Cnidii Rhizoma (Senkyu) extract, which contributes to the inhibitory effect on triphasic skin reaction in Shimotsu-to.
3-amino-6-methyltetrahydro-2H-pyran-2-one, a cyclized derivative of 2-amino-5-hydroxyhexanoic acid, has a superior lipid lowering and antioxidant activity profile compared to the open chain compound.
25CN-NBOH is a highly selective 5-HT2A receptor agonist, exhibiting over 100-fold selectivity for 5-HT2AR over 5-HT2CR and a 46-fold selectivity for 5-HT2AR over 5-HT2BR.
25CN-NBOH does not substitute for M100907 in mice, eliciting only saline-like responding across tested cumulative doses up to 30.0 mg/kg.
Feeding DCA increases GCA concentration by about 0.8 nmol/g in colon tissue of Oatp1a1-null mice, but has little effect in wild-type mice.
Feeding DCA increases GCA concentration by about 74 nmol/g in ileum tissue of Oatp1a1-null mice, but not in wild-type mice.
Chromium glycinate is a new organic complex of chromium investigated for its biological activity and toxicity.

Chromium glycinate supplementation can reverse adverse metabolic and cognitive changes induced by high-fat feeding and insulin resistance in a dose-dependent manner, likely due to its higher bioavailability.
Z-3-fluoro-2-(4-methoxybenzyl)allylamine (LJP 1586) is an orally active compound with reasonable inhibition of SSAO, exhibiting an IC50 of 4 nM.

Ethylene bis(chloroformate) is used to introduce biscarbamate linkages into low molecular weight PEI, resulting in a degradable PEI with higher transfection activity and lower cytotoxicity compared to PEI 25 kDa.
Carbamyl chloride, a potent muscarinic agonist, displays a preferred antiplanar-anticlinal conformation in the solid state, stabilized by several hydrogen bonds.
Glycine phosphoramidate derivatives (3a-b) exhibit antiviral activity against HIV-1 and HIV-2 replication in vitro, with alanine derivatives being about 10 times more potent than glycine derivatives.
N-methylglycinamide derivatives of 5H-benzopyrano[4,3-d]pyrimidine, specifically compounds 1b-g, have shown interesting pharmacological activities.
Ethyl chlorothioformate solvolyzes by an ionization mechanism with assistance from nucleophilic solvation, with calculated N T values of -1.86 (0.06 in 60% TFE-40% ethanol).
Ethyl chlorothioformate shows two-term correlation parameters (l = 0.43–0.69, m = 0.82–1.19) in its ionization reactions, similar to conventional ionization at an sp^3 hybridized carbon.
Ethyl chlorothioformate solvolyzes by an ionization mechanism with assistance from nucleophilic solvation, with calculated N T values of -1.86 (0.06 in 60% TFE-40% ethanol).
Ethyl chlorothioformate shows two-term correlation parameters (l = 0.43–0.69, m = 0.82–1.19) in its ionization reactions, similar to conventional ionization at an sp^3 hybridized carbon.
N-methylglycinamide derivatives of 5H-benzopyrano[4,3-d]pyrimidine, specifically compounds 1b-g, have shown interesting pharmacological activities.
EPA-EA is a non-oxidatively converted endogenous omega-3 eCB that acts as a precursor to more potent bioactive molecules through CB-independent and CB-dependent pathways.
Eicosapentaenoic acid-ethanolamine is an omega-3 polyunsaturated fatty acid-ethanolamine derivative identified as an excellent anti-inflammatory agent that targets Nur77.
The slow binding of (E)-4-amino-3-methylbut-2-en-1-yl diphosphate to IspH is best described by a one-step mechanism involving formation of the enzyme-inhibitor complex.
(E)-4-amino-3-methylbut-2-en-1-yl diphosphate is a reversible slow-binding inhibitor of E. coli IspH with a Ki of 54 ± 19 nM.

(E)-4-amino-3-methylbut-2-enyl 1-diphosphate (AMBPP) is a potent, reversible, competitive inhibitor of E. coli IspH, with a Ki of 54 nM, and acts by binding to the fourth iron site of the [4Fe-4S] cluster.

(E)-4-amino-3-methylbut-2-enyl 1-diphosphate (AMBPP) is substantially more potent than previously reported pyridine phosphates (IC50 = 38 μM for BPH-293 with Aquifex aeolicus IspH) and alkynyl diphosphates (IC50 = 0.45 μM for 3-butynyl diphosphate with Aquifex aeolicus IspH) as IspH inhibitors, with an IC50 of 150 nM.
2-(4'-fluorophenyl)-5-(4-phenyl-1-piperazinyl)pyridine (11C-FMZ) uptake in the auditory cortex decreases relative to 18F-FDG uptake during the recovery period of tinnitus.
N'-(4-methylbenzylidene)-5-phenylisoxazole-3-carbohydrazide is a potent, reversible, and competitive inhibitor of MAO-B, with a docking score of -10.98 kcal/mol for MAO-B binding.

N'-(4-methylbenzylidene)-5-phenylisoxazole-3-carbohydrazide significantly prevents MPTP-induced neurotoxicity in a mouse model of Parkinson's disease, as shown by improved gait behavior in the footprint test and increased grip strength in the horizontal wire test.
4-(3-phenylisoxazole-5-carboxamido)cyclohexanecarboxylic acid is a DGAT1 inhibitor with an inhibition concentration of 1 mM (¼73%) in the tested assay.

In galectins-1 and -3, the 4-fluorophenyl-triazole moiety binds to both subsite B and subsite E, with the up-field shifted 19F NMR resonances for subsite B attributed to multiple orthogonal fluorine bondings and dense packing by surrounding amino acid residues.
4-fluorophenyl-1,2,3-triazole derivatives inhibit RNase A with potency ranging from 1.6 to 94.8 µM or higher, with the uracil-containing analogue being more potent than the thymine analogue.
4-fluorophenyl-triazole forms a π-arginine interaction at subsite E of galectins-1, -3, and -7, as revealed by NMR spectroscopy and structural studies.
Nucleosides bearing a p-fluorophenyltriazole residue, such as compounds 17 and 26, show moderate activity against HepG2, A549, Hela, and LAC tumor cell lines.
6-(4-fluorophenyl)-4-hydroxypyridazin-3(2H)-one is a 6-aza analogue of 5-(4-fluorophenyl)-3-hydroxypyridin-2(1H)-one that retains modest inhibitory activity against influenza endonuclease.

(E)-3-((2-(4-(trifluoromethyl)phenyl)hydrazono)methyl)-4H-chromen-4-one is a pharmaceutically active hydrazine derivative that forms stable ligand-protein interactions with threonine tyrosine kinase (TTK) in molecular dynamics simulations (100 ns) and exhibits predicted antitumor activity higher than reference compounds.
7-fluoro 4-anilinoquinoline is a chemical scaffold designed to be as potent as quinazoline analogs in anticancer activity.
7-fluoro-4-anilinoquinoline derivatives display cytotoxic activity against HeLa and BGC823 cell lines.
6-(4-Fluorophenyl)pyrimidine displays a structure-based docking score of 2,588 kcal/mol and an inhibition constant (Ki) of 1.9 M against dihydrofolate reductase (DHFR).

(E)-4-(2-(pyridin-4-ylmethylene)hydrazinyl)benzonitrile functions as a mixed-type corrosion inhibitor for mild steel in 1 M HCl, with lower inhibition efficiency than (E)-4-((2-(2,4-dinitrophenyl)hydrazono)methyl)phenol but higher than (E)-4-((2-(2,4-dinitrophenyl)hydrazono)methyl)pyridine.

4-(4-Fluorobenzylideneamino)-5-(3-fluorophenyl)-2,4-dihydro-3H-1,2,4-triazole-3-thione exhibits good antibacterial activity with MIC 16–31.25 μg/mL and excellent antifungal activity with MIC 4–8 μg/mL against Gram-positive bacteria and fungal strains.
5-(4-fluorophenyl) salicylic acid acts as a cyclooxygenase inhibitor and exhibits differential effects on platelet function.
Hydronitroxide radical is generated from nitroso forms of arylamines in the presence of NADH and requires Cu(II) to yield Cu(I), which then reacts with O2 to produce H2O2.
The hydronitroxide radical (NH2O.) is a paramagnetic intermediate formed during the reaction between oxyhemoglobin and hydroxylamine, as detected by ESR spectroscopy.
The hydronitroxide radical (R-NHO') is a theoretically possible free radical generated during one-electron reduction of nitro-substituted compounds in vivo.

1-(4-Fluorophenyl)-2-(4-phenyl-1H-1,2,3-triazol-1-yl) ethanone is a compound characterized by IR absorption maxima at 3148, 2980, 2942, 1690 cm⁻¹ (C=O), and 1508, 1464, 1412, 1350, 1228, 1162, 1052, 994, 834, 814, 768, 700 cm⁻¹, and displays 1H NMR signals in DMSO-d6 at δ (ppm) = 6.28 (s, 2H, CH2), 7.35 (m, 1H, 4-H), 7.47 (m, 4H, 3,5-H, 3,5-H), 7.89 (d, J = 7.2 Hz, 2H, 2,6-H), 8.20 (m, 2H, 2,6-H), 8.54 (s, 1H, 5-H).
The selectivity of N-(2-sulfoethyl)chitosan for silver(I) ions over copper(II) ions increases with higher percentage of N-2-sulfoethyl groups.
Glucosamine sulfate is available in combination products with chondroitin sulfate.
Adverse effects of glucosamine sulfate include gastrointestinal complaints (most frequently), headache, leg pain and edema, and itching.
Glucosamine sulfate is a natural product classified as a slow-acting drug for osteoarthritis (SADOA) and is commonly sold in capsules containing 500 mg.
Glucosamine sulfate is administered orally at a dosage of 500 mg three times daily in most studies evaluating its efficacy for osteoarthritis.
Two relatively well-designed studies found that glucosamine sulfate 500 mg three times daily is superior to placebo and as effective as ibuprofen 400 mg three times daily in treating osteoarthritis.
6-sulfo lactosamine is a terminal sulfated carbohydrate determinant that is not recognized by the antibody MECA-79.
The sulfation of the 6-sulfolactosamine epitope in human colorectal carcinoma cells involves the activity of PAPST1 (SLC35B2) and PAPST2 (SLC35B3) PAPS transporters.
6-sulfo lactosamine is a core1 glycan structure found in the sera of certain cancer patients and is considered a reliable marker for cancer.
Gal(β1–4)GlcNAc(β1–2)Man is a glycan structure present on the outer terminal of certain glycans on purified TgAb IgG, as detected by binding specificity PHA-L.
Galactose-alpha-1,4-N-acetylglucosamine is specifically bound by the PHA-L lectin on purified TgAb IgG.
TgAb IgG from the hHT group contains a greater amount of Gal(β1–4)GlcNAc(β1–2)Man glycans on its surface compared to TgAb IgG from the mHT group.

The amount of Gal(β1–4)GlcNAc(β1–2)Man glycans on purified TgAb IgG is increased in Huntington's disease patients compared to healthy controls, as shown by increased binding to Phaseolus vulgaris Lectin.
Gal(β1–4)GlcNAc(β1–2)Man is a glycan structure associated with increased levels on TgAb IgG in HT patients compared to controls, as detected by lectin microarray analysis.
ManGlcNAc2 is a disaccharide unit that, when incorporated into oligomannose-type sugar chains (such as ManGlcNAc2-Man5GlcNAc2), serves as a substrate for alpha-mannosidases.
Man6GlcNAc2 accumulates in noninfected cells and is trimmed by α-D-mannosidase activity to form Man5GlcNAc, which is the preferred substrate for GlcNAc transferase I.
Incubation of Spodoptera frugiperda IPLB-SF-21AE cell extracts with Man9GlcNAc2 results in trimming to Man6GlcNAc2, which is relatively stable to further alpha-D-mannosidase action in these cells.
In hybridoma cell lines such as Lec9.12A, failure to complement within the Lec9 group leads to accumulation of ManGlcNAc2 in lipid-linked oligosaccharides.

ManGlcNAc2 is a truncated oligosaccharide that can be transferred to invertase in dpml yeast cells at 37°, serving as an alternative acceptor for outer chain extension, although most of these chains are not extended and the resulting protein remains Endo-H-resistant.
Galactosyl-(1→4)-N-acetylglucosamine is a product of partial acid hydrolysis of [14C]Gal-keratan, with 10% of the 14C content identified as this structure by paper chromatography.

(5S,12S)-dihydroxy-6,8,10,14-eicosatetraenoic acid inhibits leukotriene B4-induced degranulation and superoxide anion generation in human leukocytes in a dose-dependent manner without affecting cyclic AMP levels.
(5S,12S)-dihydroxy-6,8,10,14-eicosatetraenoic acid modulates the action of leukotriene B4 in human leukocytes.
Dimethyl-5,8-eicosadienoic acid acts as a phospholipase A2 inhibitor and suppresses exocytosis of catecholamine in bovine chromaffin cells.

5,8,11,12,14-eicosapentaenoic acid reduces exudate volume and protein concentration substantially in the acute inflammatory response, but does not influence the phagocytic function of polymorphonuclear neutrophils (PMN).
Dietary enrichment with 5,8,11,12,14-eicosapentaenoic acid suppresses MSU crystal-induced acute inflammation in the rat subcutaneous air pouch model.
trans-LCP is formed by desaturation and chain-elongation of cis,trans-18:2 isomers, accounting for only 3% of dietary intake.
trans-C 18:1 is present at lower proportions in milk fat from Jersey cows compared to Holstein cows.
Copper depletion increases the proportion of trans-C 18:1 in milk fat.

Triglycerides containing palmitic-palmitic-linoleic (PPL) fragments in processed soybean oil and deodorizer distillate show a reduction of up to 37.69% during neutralization, bleaching, and deodorization processes.
Cis-13,16-docosadienoic acid (C22:2) is the strongest inhibitor of both mammalian DNA polymerases and DNA topoisomerases among C22-fatty acids tested.
PGO has a stronger protective effect against colon carcinogenesis than conjugated linoleic acid (CLA).
2-AG (2-arachidonyl-glycerol) is an endocannabinoid that can be derivatized and analyzed in mouse small intestine tissue extracts using isotope dilution gas chromatography-mass spectrometry.
8-Hydroxy-5,9,11,14-eicosatetraenoic acid (8-HETE) can be generated by non-enzymatic lipid peroxidation or by P450 monooxygenase-catalyzed arachidonic acid metabolism.
8-Hydroxy-5,9,11,14-eicosatetraenoic acid (8-HETE) is a major arachidonic acid metabolite detected in phorbol ester-treated mouse skin.
8-hydroxy-5,9,11,14-eicosatetraenoic acid is produced from arachidonic acid by the action of recombinant mouse skin 8-lipoxygenase.
8-Hydroxy-5,9,11,14-eicosatetraenoic acid (8-HETE) is a minor product found in human neutrophils, psoriatic skin, human tracheal epithelial cells, and squamous head and neck carcinomas.
5-hydroxy-6,8,11-eicosatrienoic acid is the main metabolite identified from Mead acid in neutrophils.
5-oxo-20:3 is a metabolite that can be formed from Mead acid by neutrophils in the presence of calcium ionophore A23187 and PMA.

11-Hydroxyhexadecatrienoic acid [16:3(11-OH)] is generated in the medium from 15-Hydroxyeicosatetraenoic acid after two cycles of β-oxidation in cultured bovine aorta endothelial cells and human umbilical vein endothelial cells.
The 22:6(n-3) level is increased in both platelet membranes and RBC membranes in rats fed the EPAX diet compared to controls.

1-naphthyloxyacetylarginine (SKF 63649) inhibits C3a receptor internalization in human neutrophils at concentrations ranging from 100 nM to 10 µM, with inhibition observed after 3 min incubation at 37°C with 10 nM C3a.
BzArgNHNaph stock solutions of 2 mM strength are prepared by dissolving the anilide in dimethylsulfoxide, with a 1% final concentration of dimethylsulfoxide in the solution.
BzArgNHNaph is hydrolyzed to produce p-nitroaniline, which can be quantified spectrophotometrically at 410 nm.
BzArgNap is a substrate used to measure enzyme activity in enzymatic assays by coupling with p-dimethylaminocinnamaldehyde and detecting at 540 nm.
BzArgNHNaph is an arginine ester substrate used for spectrophotometric measurement of esterase activity, with hydrolysis monitored at 410 nm and product formation quantified as p-nitroaniline.
Bz-Arg-NOAn is a substrate for human enterokinase, and its hydrolysis is catalyzed by trypsin, with a reported Km value of 12.7 mM at pH 8.
Benzoxycarbonyl-L-arginine 4-methoxy-2-naphthylamide is a substrate used to assay human cathepsin B activity in enzymatic assays.
N-benzoyl-DL-arginyl-naphthylamide is used as a substrate in the calorimetric assay for measuring trypsin activity.
Benzoyl-DL-argininyl-naphthylamide is used as a substrate to measure trypsin activity in biochemical assays.

Benzoyl-DL-argininyl-8-naphthylamide is a substrate for trypsin whose hydrolysis is inhibited by human and ox-brain homogenates and cell fractions, with the greatest inhibition in the microsomal (P3) fraction of human brain and the membranous (Ss) fraction of ox-brain.
α-N-benzoyl-DL-arginyl-2-naphthylamide (BANA) is a substrate commonly used to assay cathepsin H activity, as it exhibits activity characteristic of an endopeptidase.

BGEE (glycine ethyl ester) is a substrate used to assess the enzymatic hydrolysis activity of modified trypsin, and the extent of modification affects the enzyme's reactivity with BGEE less than with BAEE.
D-MMA is a chemical isomer of L-MMA and does not enhance IL-6 production in IFN-g-activated RAW 264.7 macrophages when L-MMA is used.
D-NMMA is a nitric oxide synthase (NOS) inhibitor that is ineffective at restoring proteoglycan synthesis in chondrocytes and patellae under the tested conditions.
The Km for CAME hydrolysis by trypsin is approximately 7.8 × 10⁻⁰ M.
Nalpha-benzoyl-L-argininamide hydrochloride monohydrate serves as a substrate for measuring proteolytic activity of crude protease extracts in the pH range of 2–8.
Binding of PADPR to pig heart isocitrate dehydrogenase increases the 350 nm protein fluorescence by about 50%.
Z-arginylarginyl-naphthylamide is a substrate used to measure cathepsin B-like activity in gingival crevicular fluid (GCF) and other cysteine proteinase systems.
N-amino-L-arginine is a non-specific inhibitor of nitric oxide synthase (NOS), affecting both cNOS and iNOS isoforms by direct enzyme inhibition and/or indirect mechanisms.
N-amino-L-arginine is a relatively selective inhibitor of the constitutive isoforms of nitric oxide synthase (NOS).

Cyclopropyl p-nitrophenyl ketone is used as a substrate in the nucleophilic aromatic substitution synthesis of no-carrier-added fluorine-18-labeled N-methylspiroperidol, providing a 10–15% radiochemical yield (based on [18F-] with mass <2 nmol and specific activity >10 Ci/mumol (EOB)).

FeNAc is associated with a first-order kinetic process in iron uptake by serum-transferrin, as observed by fluorescence and characterized by a reciprocal relaxation time independent of species concentration.
Epoxide hydrolase enzymes from A. niger strains can catalyze the hydrolysis of nitrostyrene oxides.
The hydrolysis of nitrostyrene oxide by Rhodotorula glutinis and Beauveria densa occurs with a reaction rate that decreases in the order para > meta > ortho.
RpEH from R. paludigena JNU001 demonstrates high enantioselectivity and catalytic activity for nitrostyrene oxides 2a, 3a, and 4a.
The p-nitroperoxybenzoate anion, the negatively charged form of p-nitroperoxybenzoic acid, reacts rapidly with wild-type CcP but reacts about 10 times slower than the neutral acid.

The apparent bimolecular rate constant for the reaction between p-nitroperoxybenzoate and cytochrome c peroxidase (CcP) is independent of ionic strength in the range of 0.01–1.0 M, indicating that electrostatic repulsion is not the primary cause of its lower reactivity.
The ionization of pNPBA to the peroxybenzoate anion causes an inflection in the pH dependence of its reaction with CcP and CcP(H52L) near pH 7.
HQNO (10 µM), a mitochondrial complex III inhibitor, substantially inhibits the reduction of ALA, NQSA, and ferricyanide, as well as oxygen uptake by Entamoeba histolytica trophozoites.
N-phenyl-4-nitroaniline is produced in 93% yield from the reaction of 1-chloro-4-nitrobenzene and aniline using Pd[P(t-Bu)3]2 and KOH under nitrogen atmosphere.

Aryl substituted 2-(4-nitrophenyl)-4-oxo-4-phenylbutanoates generally exhibit poor to moderate inhibitory potency (4–73%) towards rat liver microsomal retinoic acid metabolising enzymes compared to ketoconazole (80%).
2-(4-nitrophenyl)-4-oxo-4-phenylbutanoate is an aryl substituted 1,2-diphenylpropane compound evaluated as an inhibitor of rat microsomal CYP1A2 (RA-metabolising) enzyme.
p-nitrophenyldiazoacetate acts as a bifunctional quasi-substrate for chymotrypsin, acylating the seryl residue in the enzyme's active site.
p-nitrophenyldiazoacetate is a photoaffinity label that acylates the active site of subtilisin, converting it into diazoacetylsubtilisin, which can be photolyzed to yield inactivated derivatives.
p-nitrophenyldiazoacetate is used as a reagent to introduce a diazo moiety covalently linked to the serine hydroxyl at the active site of chymotrypsin.
p-nitrophenyldiazoacetate reacts with chymotrypsin to form an acyl-chymotrypsin derivative that is stable to deacylation.
4-NMPB binding in the Torpedo presynaptic preparation can be inhibited by agonists that bind to both high- and low-affinity sites, as modeled by a two-site binding model.

The aminolysis of p-nitrophenyl N-methyl-N-phenylcarbamate is at least 10,000 times slower than the aminolysis of p-nitrophenyl N-phenylcarbamate due to the unfeasibility of the isocyanate mechanism when N-H is replaced by N-CH3.
ZPCK, a serine protease inhibitor, does not inhibit detachment of serum-treated A. viscosus 14V by PMN at 10 mg/ml.
4-nitrobenzylcarbamate can be used as a prodrug moiety to link with cytotoxins, enabling nitro-triggered drug release for gene delivery applications.
Gadolinium(III) phytanate molecules can form a hexagonal columnar structure in which Gd ions are located at the center of the cylinders and hydrocarbon chains radiate outward.
Gadolinium(III) phytanate forms colloidal particles with relaxivity values comparable to the commercial contrast agent Magnevist.
Gadolinium phytanate forms a less-ordered hexagonal phase in water, as shown by broadened hexagonal peaks.
Gadolinium phytanate forms a hexagonal phase, as indicated by a single broad diffraction peak in SAXS measurements that aligns with the first-order reflection of more ordered hexagonal phases.
4,8-dimethyldecanoate (C12D) is a deuterium-labeled precursor that is incorporated into 4,8-DMD by Tribolium castaneum.
3,7,11,15-tetramethylhexadec-15-monoenoic acid is a mono-unsaturated analogue of phytanic acid that has been identified in the serum and urine of patients with Refsum's disease.
[123I]16-iodo-3-methyl-hexadecanoic acid (MIHA) is a beta-methyl-branched free fatty acid developed for myocardial single-photon emission tomography (SPET) imaging.

[123I]16-iodo-3-methyl-hexadecanoic acid (MIHA) is a labelled fatty acid with a β-methyl group that prevents βoxidation, resulting in its main cardiac metabolic pathway being intracellular trapping in the endogenous triglyceride pool.
The myocardial clearance of [123I]16-iodo-3-methyl-hexadecanoic acid between two consecutive SPET studies is not affected by ischaemia in either animal or patient models.

In patient SPET studies, [123I]16-iodo-3-methyl-hexadecanoic acid uptake in ischaemic and viable myocardium (74% ± 9% of maximal left ventricular value) is higher than in necrotic (59% ± 7%) or normal (88% ± 6%) areas.

In a blood-perfused isolated heart model subjected to moderate ischaemia (50% flow reduction), the early retention fraction of [123I]16-iodo-3-methyl-hexadecanoic acid in the myocardium increases from 0.14 ± 0.04 (normoxic) to 0.24 ± 0.10 (ischaemic), indicating enhanced myocardial uptake under ischaemic conditions.
Dietary inulin supplementation decreases the abundance of 8-methylnonenoate in the rumen of dairy cows.
The subcellular localization of ␣-oxidation for both R- and S-isomers of 3-methylhexadecanoic acid is peroxisomal in rat liver.
3-methylhexadecanoic acid undergoes ␣-oxidation in rat liver and hepatocytes, with both R- and S-isomers showing comparable rates of ␣-oxidation in intact hepatocytes and rat liver homogenates.
3MeGlu transport in Swiss 3T3 mouse fibroblasts is enhanced by TPA and can be inhibited by glycyrrhetic acid.
2,3-dihydroxy-12-methyltridecanoic acid is a dihydroxy fatty acid detected in the lipopolysaccharide of Legionella pneumophila.
1,1-dihydroxy-4-methyl-2 pentanone acts as a temporary redox sink during the oxidative catabolism of KIC, a branched-chain ␣-keto acid.

1, 1-dihydroxy-4-methyl-2-pentanone is an intermediate detected extracellularly during the conversion of alpha-ketoisocaproic acid by the bkd gene cluster from Enterococcus faecalis, and serves as a temporary redox sink for the reduced form of alpha-ketoisocaproic acid.
1,1-dihydroxy-4-methyl-2 pentanone acts as a temporary redox sink during the oxidative catabolism of KIC, a branched-chain ␣-keto acid.

1, 1-dihydroxy-4-methyl-2-pentanone is an intermediate detected extracellularly during the conversion of alpha-ketoisocaproic acid by the bkd gene cluster from Enterococcus faecalis, and serves as a temporary redox sink for the reduced form of alpha-ketoisocaproic acid.

The protein-ligand complex of 5,6-dihydroxy-2-(4-hydroxyphenyl)-7-[3,4,5-trihydroxy-6-(hydroxymethyl)oxane-2-yl]oxychromen-4-one with DENV-2 NS5 methyl transferase exhibits a binding free energy change of -38.01 ± 7.53 to -17.75 ± 11.03 kcal/mol (MM/PBSA model), indicating sustained binding spontaneity and product formation.

5,6-dihydroxy-2-(4-hydroxyphenyl)-7-[3,4,5-trihydroxy-6-(hydroxymethyl)oxane-2-yl]oxychromen-4-one (FLD3) binds to the dengue virus NS5 methyl transferase with a binding affinity of -10.1 kcal/mol, showing better binding than the native ligand (-7.6 kcal/mol) and diclofenac sodium (-7.3 kcal/mol) in molecular docking studies.
In rats administered G-Hesperidin, the serum concentration of hesperetin conjugate is higher than that of hesperidin throughout the experimental period, except at 27 hours.
Ghesperidin down-regulates the assembly of apo B-containing lipoproteins by reducing cellular cholesterol ester synthesis augmented with oleate and suppresses excess apo B secretion from HepG2 cells.

After administration of G-Hesperidin to rats, hesperetin-glucuronide reaches a maximum serum concentration of 6.3 mM at 6 hours and has an area under the concentration-time curve (AUC) approximately 3.7-fold higher than that of hesperidin.
Gericudranin E, isolated from the root bark of Cudrania tricuspidata, exhibits hepatoprotective effects.
5,7,3'-trihydroxy-4'-methoxydihydroflavonol is a flavanone identified as an inducer of the nod gene in sterile root exudate of Vicia sativa.

6,8-di-C-methylkaempferol 7-O-beta-d-glucopyranoside exhibits significant neuroprotective effects against hydrogen peroxide (H2O2)-induced and amyloid beta peptide (Abeta)-induced apoptosis in human neuroblastoma SH-SY5Y cells, with a 30% improvement in cell viability at 10 µM concentration.

Apigenin 5-O-α-L-rhamnopyranosyl-(1 → 3)-β-D-glucopyranoside is a newly identified flavone glycoside isolated from the water soluble fraction of Cephalotaxus koreana leaf extract and is identified as an active antioxidant principle.
6,8-diprenyl-7,4′-dihydroxyflavanone suppresses activation of nuclear factor-kappaB (NF-kappaB) and extracellular signal-regulated kinases (ERKs) in lipopolysaccharide-stimulated macrophages.
6,8-diprenyl-7,4′-dihydroxyflavanone demonstrates anti-inflammatory effects by blocking NF-kappaB and ERK activation in macrophages.

6,8-diprenyl-7,4′-dihydroxyflavanone inhibits the production of nitric oxide and the expression of tumor necrosis factor alpha, interleukin-1beta, and interleukin-6 in macrophage-mediated inflammation in vitro.

7-beta-d-glucopyranosyloxy-4'-hydroxyflavylium (P3) exhibits a high cis-trans isomerization barrier in its chalcone form, resulting in a pseudo-equilibrium between all species except the trans-chalcone.

2-(2-hydroxyphenyl)ethanol-O-beta-d-glucopyranoside is a phenolic glycoside isolated from Brassica rapa flowers that exhibits inhibitory activity against intestinal alpha-glucosidase with an IC50 value in the range of 14.43 to 50.20 µM.
Hesperetin 7-O-glucuronide at 30 mM concentration provides 25% protection against UV-A-induced necrotic cell death in human foreskin-derived fibroblasts (FEK4).

Hesperetin 7-O-glucuronide is a bioavailable form of hesperetin that protects against UV-A-induced oxidative stress in cultured human foreskin-derived fibroblasts (FEK4) by interacting with the external plasma membrane and evoking an adaptive response.

4-[4-(beta-d-glucopyranosyloxy)phenyl]-2-butanone is a food ingredient that can be biotransformed by Phytolacca americana suspension cells to produce beta-glycosides, including 4-[(3S)-3-hydroxybutyl]phenyl-beta-d-glucopyranoside and 2-hydroxy-4-[(3S)-3-hydroxybutyl]phenyl-beta-d-glucopyranoside.
Lysidiside O is a type II stilbene glycoside with a fragmentation pattern that is markedly different from type I stilbene glycosides, influenced by its structural arrangement.
Lupinifolin, a prenylated flavonoid isolated from Erythrina fusca stem bark extract, does not show antimalarial activity against Plasmodium falciparum K1 strain.
5,7-dihydroxy-8-geranylflavanone is a prenylated phenolic compound isolated from the fruits of Amorpha fruticosa and obtained in pure form (95.2%) by high-speed counter-current chromatography.
Nigerapyrone E is a cytotoxic secondary metabolite produced by marine-derived Aspergillus niger.
Nigerapyrone E, isolated from the endophytic fungus A. niger MA-132, exhibits cytotoxicity against the SW1990, MDA-MB-231, and A549 cell lines with IC50 values of 38, 48, and 43 μM, respectively.
Nigerapyrone B is a cytotoxic secondary metabolite produced by marine-derived Aspergillus niger.
Nigerapyrone B, isolated from the endophytic fungus A. niger MA-132, shows moderate cytotoxicity against the HepG2 cell line with an IC50 of 62.0 μM.
Nigerapyrone B is a natural product that exhibits cytotoxic activity against many cancer cells and acts as a potent and selective COX-2 inhibitor.
2,4,5-trimethoxycinnamic acid can be reduced with PdCl2-HCOOH-aq. NaOH to yield 3-(2,4,5-trimethoxy)phenyl propionic acid in 88% yield within 3 minutes.

2,4,5-trimethoxycinnamic acid is a metabolite of Alpinia flabellate and can be synthesized from asaronaldehyde and malonic acid using piperidine and acetic acid as condensing agents under microwave irradiation.
2,4,5-trimethoxycinnamic acid administered intraperitoneally at 25–200 mg/kg does not significantly alter the duration of immobility in the forced swimming test in mice.
2',6'-dihydroxy-4'-methoxy-3'-methyldihydrochalcone exhibits radical scavenging properties towards the DPPH radical as determined by spectrophotometric analysis.
2', 6'-dihydroxy-4'-methoxy-3'-methyldihydrochalcone is a dihydrochalcone isolated from Myrica serrata leaf dichloromethane extract.

2',6'-dihydroxy-3'-methyl-4'-methoxy-dihydrochalcone, isolated from Eucalyptus maculata, exhibits potent antimicrobial activity against seven microorganisms with minimum inhibitory concentrations ranging from 1.0 to 31 mg/l.

Zn(II)-curcumin at doses of 12, 24, and 48 mg/kg significantly decreases malondialdehyde (MDA) content in gastric mucosa of acetic acid-treated rats to 2.88 ± 0.85, 2.37 ± 0.55, and 2.08 ± 0.41 nmol TBA/mg protein, respectively, compared to control.

Zn(II)-curcumin at 24 mg/kg reduces malondialdehyde (MDA) content in gastric mucosa to 2.37 ± 0.55 nmol TBA/mg protein, which is lower than curcumin at 12 mg/kg (3.00 ± 0.74 nmol TBA/mg protein) at the same dose.
Zn(II)-curcumin SDs were administered orally at doses equivalent to 12, 24, and 48 mg/kg in a rat model of gastric ulcers.
Propyl ferulate is a phenolic acid derivative assessed for its antioxidant properties in preserving the quality of refined sunflower oil.
The recombinant type C feruloyl esterase from Fusarium oxysporum exhibits strong preference for the hydrolysis of n-propyl ferulate among tested substrates.
Acremonide is a newly identified phthalide derivative isolated from the mangrove-derived fungus Acremonium sp. PSU-MA70.
Acremonide is a newly isolated phthalide derivative from the endophytic fungus Acremonium sp. PSU-MA70.
acremonide is an isocoumarin derivative obtained from the endophytic fungus Acremonium sp. and exhibits antibiotic activity against Candida albicans and Cryptococcus neoformans.
The yield of isopropyl ferulate using immobilized lipase (12.5 mg/mL) at 45°C in 2 mL DMSO with 100 mM isopropanol and 25 mM ferulic acid for 3 hours is approximately 80%.
Indole bromide undergoes oxidative addition to Pd(0) in palladium-catalyzed intramolecular iminoannulation reactions.
