Indole phosphate is hydrolyzed by a specific enzyme encoded by the iph gene in Nostoc commune UTEX 584, with activity detectable only when cells are partially depleted of phosphorus and is suppressed by addition of phosphate to assay buffers.
An indole phosphate hydrolase gene was isolated from Nostoc commune UTEX 584 and expressed in E. coli phoA mutants using low-phosphate-XP medium.
The methylene spacer length in bisindole-1,3,4-oxadiazole derivatives plays a major role in ligand folding and affects the compound's apoptotic and Bcl-2 binding profile.

Indole acetic acid methyl ester can be converted to an alkylidene derivative that reacts with 4-methoxystyrene to yield azepino[1,2-a]indoles through a [2+2] cycloaddition and cyclobutane ring-opening.
Indole-isoxazole hybrids have been synthesized and evaluated as EGFR inhibitors.
Indole-isoxazole conjugates function as dual inhibitors of histone deacetylases (HDACs) and bromodomain-containing protein BRD4, and have been developed as promising anticancer agents.
Indole azaborines can be synthesized using a strategy that involves activation of organotrifluoroborate precursors with a fluorophile and reaction with o-aminostyrene derivatives.
[1,2,4]triazolo[1,5-a]pyrimidine indole derivatives are designed as anticancer agents that suppress the ERK signaling pathway.
Indole-3-propionyl-L-alanine (IPA-Ala) is an auxin conjugate that, upon hydrolysis in Brassica rapa seedlings, releases indole-3-propionic acid (IPA), which inhibits root growth.
6-azaindoles are generally the most effective fXa inhibitors among the tested series.
5/6-OH-3MI is a metabolite of skatole whose production in pigs is specifically affected by inhibitors of human CYP2A6 and CYP2E1.
1a is a 6-azaindole pyrrolidine compound whose crystal structure with human factor Xa (fXa) has been used to inform the design of more potent fXa inhibitors.
The melting point of benzimidazolium bromides can be correlated with molecular descriptors that reflect both intermolecular interactions and intramolecular electronic effects.
Benzo[g]indole biscarbamates are tricyclic compounds with biscarbamoyl groups and a conjugated 4,5-dihydro bridge, which may enhance their electronic properties and tumor growth inhibition activity.

5-(2-Aminopropyl)indole is a newly available new psychoactive substance that can be differentiated from its isomer 3-(2-aminopropyl)indole by subtle differences in mass spectral and UV analytical conditions.
5-(2-aminopropyl)indole (5-IT) is isomeric with a-methyltryptamine (AMT), making them potentially confused during routine laboratory analysis.
5-(2-Aminopropyl)indole, along with its positional isomers, exhibits pronounced monoamine oxidase (MAO) inhibitory activity in pig liver homogenate assays using serotonin as substrate.

The inhibitory potency of quinolin-2-one-pyrimidine hybrids against P-gp is favored by the number of methoxy groups, an optimal molecular length in the extended conformation, and at least one flexible methylene group bridging the quinolinone to the pyrimidine moiety.
The quinolone pyrimidine scaffold is a key feature that confers higher potency in the development of NDH-2 inhibitors for Mycobacterium tuberculosis.
(dibenzo[b,d]thiophen-4-yl)-2-morpholino-4H-chromen-4-one (NU7441) is a potent inhibitor of DNA-dependent protein kinase (DNA-PK) with an IC50 of 42 ± 2 nM.

(Dibenzo[b,d]thiophen-4-yl)-2-morpholino-4H-chromen-4-one (NU7441) analogs have been studied for their inhibitory activity against DNA-dependent protein kinase and in vitro and in vivo anticancer activity.

1,2,3,4-tetrahydrochromeno[3,2-c]pyridin-10-one derivatives are designed to possess single or dual cholinesterase (ChE) and monoamine oxidase (MAO) inhibitory activity and other activities relevant to Alzheimer's disease.

1,2,3,4-tetrahydrochromeno[3,2-c]pyridine-10-one derivatives have been synthesized and shown to exhibit multitargeting inhibitory activity against enzymes and processes involved in Alzheimer's disease, including acetylcholinesterase, butyrylcholinesterase, monoamine oxidases A and B, beta-amyloid aggregation, and reactive oxygen species production.

1,2,3,4-tetrahydrochromeno[3,2-c]pyridin-10-one is a chemical scaffold used to develop compounds with multimodal anti-Alzheimer's disease (AD) profiles, including mono- and multitargeting inhibition of monoamine oxidases (MAOs) and cholinesterases (ChEs), anti-aggregating activity on amyloid beta (Ab), and neuroprotection against Ab and reactive oxygen species (ROS).
3,4-diphenylcoumarin derivatives were synthesized and studied for their receptor binding affinities to human estrogen receptors ERa and ERb.
3-morpholino 4,6-diphenylpyridazine inhibits TXA2 synthetase in vitro with an ID50 of 5.3 × 10⁻´ M (α 5.5 × 10⁻¹ M) and is completely inactive on PG12 synthetase.
Substituents at positions 5 and 7 of the 3-phenyl-4-hydroxycoumarin ring influence its inhibitory potency against HIV-1 protease.
3-Phenyl-4-hydroxycoumarin derivatives have been screened as inhibitors of HIV-1-protease.
4-oxapyrene-5-one is an intermediate product formed during the Py/TiO2 photocatalytic degradation process, as confirmed by GC-MS and IR spectroscopy.
4-oxapyrene-5-one is an intermediate product formed during the TiO2-catalyzed photooxidation of pyrene.
4-oxapyrene-5-one is an intermediate product formed during the photocatalytic oxidation of pyrene preadsorbed on TiO2 under UV irradiation.
Flavonol antimony complexes show weak anti-promastigote activity but demonstrate anti-amastigote effects with potency greater than meglumine antimonate.

5-oxo-4,5-dihydropyrano[3,2-c]chromene derivatives, when fused with an N-benzylpyridinium scaffold, act as potent acetylcholinesterase inhibitors (AChEIs) with dual binding site inhibition of the catalytic active site and peripheral anionic site of AChE.

1-hydroxy-3-[2-(pyrrolidin-1-yl)ethoxy]-9H-xanthen-9-one is a xanthone derivative that acts as a potent acetylcholinesterase inhibitor with high selectivity (IC50 = 2.403 ± 0.002 µM for AChE, IC50 = 31.221 ± 0.002 µM for BuChE), exhibits antioxidant activity (IC50 = 2.662 ± 0.003 µM), and is a mixed-type inhibitor interacting with both the catalytic anionic site and peripheral anionic site of AChE.

The copper complex of 1-hydroxy-3-[2-(pyrrolidin-1-yl)ethoxy]-9H-xanthen-9-one shows significantly enhanced acetylcholinesterase inhibitory activity (IC50 = 0.934 ± 0.002 µM) and antioxidant activity (IC50 = 1.064 ± 0.003 µM) compared to the parent compound.

3-(2-(pyrrolidinyl)ethoxy)-1- hydroxy-9H-xanthen-9-one is a xanthone derivative with high inhibitory activity against acetylcholinesterase (AChE) and high specificity for AChE, showing an IC50 of 2.403 µM for AChE and 31.221 µM for butyrylcholinesterase (BuChE).
3-vinylchromone derivatives are privileged structures found in many biologically active compounds and serve as versatile synthetic building blocks.

6-methoxy-2-[4-(2-methoxyphenyl)piperazin-1-yl]-9H-xanthen-9-one (HBK-11) is a xanthone derivative with potential antidepressant activity, reducing immobility time by 32% to 50% at 20 mg/kg in mice at 10 mg/kg in the FST assay.
6-methoxy-2-[4-(2-methoxyphenyl)piperazin-1-yl]-9Hxanthen-9-one (HBK-11) is a xanthone derivative investigated for antidepressant-like properties and its effects on motor performance in mice.

4-bromo-N-benzylphenethylamine derivatives have been investigated as PET imaging agents for the 5-HT 2A receptor, with N-benzyl substitution and aryl groups containing hydrogen bond acceptors such as ether conferring high activity.
Benzylhydrazine dihydrochloride is tumorigenic, as demonstrated in this study.
Benzylhydrazine dihydrochloride is tumorigenic and increases lung tumor incidence from 21% to 42% in female Swiss mice when administered continuously in drinking water.

Benzylhydrazine dihydrochloride, when administered in drinking water to Swiss mice for life, increases the incidence of lung tumors in females (from 21% to 42%), blood vessel tumors in females (from 5% to 10%), and lymphoreticular tissue tumors in females (from 24% to 34%).

Tifacogin treatment in patients with severe sepsis is associated with decreased plasma levels of prothrombin fragment 1.2 and thrombin:antithrombin complex at 24 and 96 hours postinitiation of treatment compared to placebo.
Introduction of a fluorine atom at the meta position of N-butylbenzylamine enhances its antimycobacterial activity.
4-(4-(benzylamino)butoxy)-9H-carbazole is a functionalised carbazole derivative with antibacterial activity, showing almost complete inhibition of Gram-positive bacterial growth at 50 µg/mL.
4-(4-(benzylamino)butoxy)-9H-carbazole exhibits antibacterial activity against Gram-negative bacteria P. aeruginosa, causing about 50% growth inhibition at 20 µg/mL.

n-octyl-4-hydroxybenzylamine (LINS03003) is a synthetic derivative of gibbilimbol B that exhibits potent antiparasitic activity with IC50 values of 5.5 µM against amastigotes of Trypanosoma cruzi and 1.8 µM against amastigotes of Leishmania infantum.

The rate-determining step in CPA-catalyzed hydrolysis of Bz-Gly-Phe is the nucleophilic attack of a scissile bond by water, accompanied by concerted proton transfer to Glu270 (general base catalysis).
Bz-Gly-OPh undergoes phenyl ester ligation with cysteine (Cys) to selectively yield Bz-Gly-Cys, indicating that thiol capture of Cys predominates in this ligation reaction.
3-(4-benzylamino)-1,2,4,5-tetrazine can be conjugated to succinimidyl esters of visible light-emitting BODIPY dyes to create fluorogenic tetrazine probes for intracellular imaging.

N-[2-(4-hydroxyphenyl)ethyl]-5-phenylpentylamine hydrochloride is a potent and selective antagonist of the NR1A/2B subunit combination of NMDA receptors, with an IC50 of 8 nM and over 1000-fold selectivity with respect to NR1A/2A and NR1A/2C receptors.

Benzylaminocyclopentane structures act as potent ±²-D-galactosidase inhibitors, with their potency comparable to D-galactopyranosylamines with N-benzyl or N-heptyl substituents, although assay conditions and stability of N-alkyl derivatives in aqueous solution differ.
Cyclopentylbenzylamine (11c) exhibits a 4-6-fold increase in binding affinity to the human MC4R compared to the parent benzylamine (11a), with a K i of 380 nM.
Bis-9-aminoacridine derivatives that are unsubstituted or have limited substitution on the acridine short axis can bind to DNA without requiring binding site exclusion.

Bis-9-aminoacridine serves as a core structure for novel antitumor bisacridine derivatives with anti-inflammatory activity, where nonaromatic semi-rigid linkers are used to modify the acridine ring nitrogen and improve water solubility.
Bis-9-aminoacridine induces conformational changes in RecA and DNA similar to ATP gamma S, as measured by circular dichroism.
1,4-di-(phenylthioureido) butane acts as a P2Y 6 receptor antagonist and prevents the UDP-mediated inhibition of osmotic swelling in retinal glial cells.
4-phenylbutyraldehyde can modify the heme prosthetic group of cytochrome P450 BM3 (F87G mutant) in the presence of hydrogen peroxide, resulting in a heme adduct with a maximum absorbance at 408 nm.
(S)-3 aminotridecan-4-one is a ligand analogue that can be used to study and potentially control histidine kinase activity by altering the head and tail of the molecule.
(S)-3-aminotridecan-4-one (Am-CAI-1) is produced in vitro by CqsA from l-aminobutyrate and decanoyl-coenzyme A.
(S)-3-aminotridecan-4-one (Am-CAI-1) is produced from (S)-2-aminobutyrate and decanoyl-CoA by CqsA in vitro.
(S)-3 aminotridecan-4-one, also known as amino-CAI-1, is a natural ligand detected by the sensor protein CqsS in Vibrio cholerae.
(S)-3-aminotridecan-4-one (Am-CAI-1) is produced in vitro by CqsA-catalyzed formation of a carbon–carbon bond between (S)-2-aminobutyrate and decanoyl-Coenzyme A.
Progesterone treatment restores plasma levels of 2-Palmitoylglycine to baseline in LPS-treated 7-day pregnant Balb/c mice.
Dodecylglucuronamide surfactant can form niosomal vesicles with diameters ranging from about 250 to 480 nm and a low polydispersity index, indicating a relatively homogenous vesicle population.
Dodecylglucuronamide surfactant forms spherical vesicles without the need for cholesterol as a membrane additive, likely due to an optimal critical packing parameter.

Dodecylglucuronamide forms niosomes that can encapsulate hydroxylic drugs such as doxorubicin and 5-fluorouracil, resulting in smaller vesicle diameters due to attractive interactions between its polar heads and the drugs.

Dodecylglucuronamide-based vesicles exhibit very high HC50 values for hemolysis, indicating low toxicity and making them potentially suitable for pharmaceutical (parenteral administration) and cosmetic applications.

(Z)-9-tetradecanal is a component of sex pheromone blends in Heliothis virescens, Heliothis subflexa, Helicoverpa armigera, and Helicoverpa assulta moths, where it is used in combination with (Z)-11-hexadecenal and other minor fatty aldehydes and alcohols.

(Z)-9-tetradecanal (Z9-14:Ald) is a component of the sex pheromone produced by female H.armigera and can increase moth attraction at low concentrations but inhibit attraction at high concentrations when combined with other compounds.
Small amounts of (Z)-9-tetradecanal (Z9-14:Ald) added to the main pheromone blend significantly reduce the capture of male Heliothis assulta in field tests.
(Z)-9-tetradecanal (Z9-14:Ald) acts as an agonist in male Heliothis armigera attraction blends at low concentrations and as an antagonist at high concentrations.

l-2-aminooctanoic acid can be used as a residue substitute for the Arg residue at the C2 position in PBAN analogs to assess their in vitro PBAN-like activity in a Ca2+ imaging assay using BomPBANR–EGFP-expressing Sf9 cells.

Undecanoyl-5-aminolevulinic acid exhibits a 40% decrease in cell viability at 0.3 mM under photodynamic therapy (PDT), and concentrations above 0.3 mM result in complete cell death, which is attributed to its intrinsic toxicity rather than the PDT effect.
Octylgalacturonic acid, a soy elicitor, induces the production of reactive oxygen species in soy cell suspension culture by activating heterotrimeric G proteins.
In soy cell suspension culture, octylgalacturonic acid induces an oxidative burst that is related to activation of phospholipase C.
S-heptyl-cysteine can be introduced into proteins via Cys-X scanning, resulting in homologous Met derivatives with altered chemical and enzymatic properties.
Stearic-capric acid can be incorporated into activated-attapulgite to form form-stable composite phase change materials (PCMs) with confirmed thermal and chemical reliability.
mCPP (morphine-conditioned prepulse component) expression in the nucleus accumbens is increased during morphine withdrawal in rats and can be suppressed by the TRPV1 antagonist CPZ.
N-substituted rac-cis-N-methyl-1,2,3,4,4a,9a-hexahydrobenzofuro[2,3-c]pyridin-6-ol analogues have high affinity for opioid receptors and exhibit significant antinociceptive activity.
17α-methylestradiol has been synthesized as a carbon-11 labeled compound for use as a potential receptor binding radiopharmaceutical.
Carbamate derivatives of 17α-methylestradiol show strongly impaired affinity for the estrogen receptor and do not localize in receptor-rich tissue via an estrogen receptor-mediated process in vivo.
17α-methylestradiol is the aromatase-mediated metabolite of methyltestosterone in fish.

17α-methylestradiol (ME2) is detectable in the plasma of male and female fathead minnows after exposure to 20 or 200 g MT/l for 7 days, with concentrations ranging from 3 to 24 ng/ml depending on sex and exposure level.
Estratetraenol may affect psychological processes via sensory systems that are independent of the vomeronasal organ.
Estratetraenol elicits sex-specific differences in mood responses, with women showing higher Positive Stimulated mood and men showing lower Positive Stimulated mood compared to propylene glycol.

3-Hydroxy-2,2-dimethyl-1-benzofuran (3HDT) promotes the preferential generation of reactive oxygen species (ROS) and caspase 3/7 activation, leading to apoptosis in triple-negative breast cancer (TNBC) cells via oxidative stress.

3-Hydroxy-2,2-dimethyl-1-benzofuran (3HDT) treatment induces mitochondrial membrane potential depletion in triple-negative breast cancer (TNBC) cells in a dose- and time-dependent manner, leading to preferential oxidative stress and MMP loss in TNBC cells compared to normal cells.

3-(1,1-dimethylbutyl)-6,6,9-trimethyl-6a,7,10,10a-tetrahydro-6H-benzo[c]chromene (JWH-133) is a CB2-selective agonist that, when administered intrathecally, can produce antinociception in the mouse partial sciatic nerve ligation model of neuropathic pain, but not in the rat formalin paw model of inflammatory pain.

2-chloroestrone forms a trimethylsilyl ether whose mass spectrum shows a molecular ion peak at m/z 376 and a characteristic fragment ion at m/z 197 (M + ÀC 12 H 19 O), which can aid in its identification by GC/MS.
2-chloroestrone is a chlorinated metabolite of estrone formed rapidly during the reaction of estrone with sodium hypochlorite in buffer solutions at pH 7 and 9.
2-chloroestrone inhibits sulfatase in human placental microsomes with an apparent Km of 15.0 nM and an inhibition constant of 0.58, acting competitively.
1,3,5(10)-oestratriene-3,15β,16α,17β-tetrol can be produced from IS&l 6p-dihydroxy DHA by placental homogenates in vitro.
Total urinary concentrations of α-carboxyethyl-6-hydroxychroman sulfate are approximately 15 times greater in children with diabetes compared to age-matched controls.
12-oxoestradiol can be detected in protein solutions down to 5 × 10⁻ⁱ M by photon-counting spectrofluorimetry.
12-oxoestradiol is a fluorescent estrogen ligand that becomes maximally fluorescent only after ultraviolet irradiation.
12-oxoestradiol is used as a ligand in fluorometric assays to determine estrogen receptor content in cytosol preparations.
12-oxoestradiol enables nuclear fluorescence of estrogen receptors in MCF-7 human breast cancer cells at 10⁻⁹ M concentration.
H-7 induces rapid changes in astrocyte morphology that are similar to those elicited by PMA.

N1,N5,N10,N14-tetrakis[3-(4-hydroxyphenyl)-2-propenoyl]-1,5,10,14-tetraazatetradecane, also known as tetracoumaroyl spermine, is a potent NK1 receptor antagonist with Ki values of 21.9 nM and 3.3 nM for guinea pig ileum and human NK1 receptor assays, respectively.

Para-aminomethyl-N-hydroxy cinnamic acid amide derivatives exhibit structure-activity relationships where the amino group substitution pattern influences HDAC inhibitory activity and cell growth inhibition.

(2E)-N-(3-chlorophenyl)-3-phenylprop-2-enamide is a potent, reversible, and selective inhibitor of recombinant human monoamine oxidase B (MAO-B) with an IC50 of 0.53 µM and a binding affinity at least 37-fold higher for MAO-B than for MAO-A.
N-(Dansylamino)hexylaminocarbonylpentyl-1,5-dideoxy-1,5-imino-D-galactitol is a strong competitive inhibitor of beta-galactosidase.
N-(Dansylamino)hexylaminocarbonylpentyl-1,5-dideoxy-1,5-imino-D-galactitol enhances residual beta-galactosidase activities in fibroblasts.

N-(dansylamino)hexylaminocarbonylpentyl-1,5-dideoxy-1,5-imino-d-galactitol is a DGJ-derived compound that acts as a chaperone for GLB1, increasing enzyme activity by approximately 2-fold at 1 µM and up to 10-fold after 500 µM treatment in human fibroblasts with the p.R201C mutation.
N-(dansylamino)hexylaminocarbonylpentyl-1,5-dideoxy-1,5-imino-d-galactitol inhibits human GLB1 with a Ki of 0.7 µM and has a lower affinity at the lysosome than in the ER.

N-(Dansylamino)hexylaminocarbonylpentyl-1,5-dideoxy-1,5-imino-D-galactitol serves as a lead compound for developing diagnostic compounds for tracking mutant beta-galactosidase and aberrant GM1 gangliosides by live cell imaging.

(2E)-N-[3,5-bis(trifluoromethyl)phenyl]-3-phenylprop-2-enamide shows antimicrobial activity with a minimum inhibitory concentration (MIC) of 22.27 µM against Staphylococcus aureus, three methicillin-resistant S. aureus strains, Mycobacterium tuberculosis H37Ra, Fusarium avenaceum, and Bipolaris sorokiniana.
The rate of the isomerization process of dansyl-D-arginine with trypsin increases linearly with decreasing pH below neutral.

The fluorescence intensity increase of dansyl-D-arginine with trypsin follows a two-step mechanism involving a fast bimolecular association and a slower unimolecular isomerization process, with the isomerization rate depending on pH.
Methoxy derivatives of dibenzo[c,g]azecine display higher affinities for dopamine receptors than their hydroxy analogs.

Bisdeoxycurcumin binds to human serum albumin (HSA) through a static quenching mechanism with approximately one binding site, interacting on both site I and site II, and increases α-helix structures at low concentrations.
(E)-4-(2-(2-naphthyl)vinyl)pyridine forms synHT dimers with high stereoselectivity upon irradiation with a 250 W high-pressure mercury lamp in acidic solution.
4-hydroxycinnamic acid (l-phenylalanine methyl ester) amide inhibits human acyl-CoA:cholesterol acyltransferase-1 and -2 activities with apparent IC50 values around 60 µM.
4-hydroxycinnamic acid (l-phenylalanine methyl ester) amide acts as an antioxidant against copper-mediated low-density lipoprotein (LDL) oxidation with an apparent IC50 of 52 µM.

N-(2-(4-Bromophenoxy)ethyl)-2,6-dimethylimidazo[1,2-a]pyridine-3-carboxamide (IMB-1402) displays an MIC of 0.025 µg/ml against drug-sensitive Mtb H37Rv and acceptable safety and pharmacokinetic properties.
Cinnamamide derivatives containing a 4-hydroxy-3-methoxyphenyl group on the phenyl ring generally show potent inhibition towards tyrosinase.
(2R)-N-[5-(4-bromophenyl)-6H-1,3,4-thiadiazin-2-yl]-2-[(phenylsulfonyl)amino]propanamide is a selective inhibitor of MMP-9 with a K(i) of 40 nM.
Sarisan is a myristicin analogue isolated from Piper solmsianum leaf essential oil.
(+)-fargesin is a furofuran lignan with a single methylenedioxy bridge that accumulates in Sesamum alatum due to the absence of CYP81Q1 and the limited biosynthetic capacity of CYP81Q3.
(-)-3-hydroxy-4-methoxy-8,9-methylenedioxypterocarpan is a constituent of Tephrosia purpurea that induces quinone reductase (QR) activity in cultured Hepa 1c1c7 mouse hepatoma cells.
(-)-3-hydroxy-4-methoxy-8,9-methylenedioxypterocarpan is a constituent of Tephrosia purpurea identified by comparison of its physical and spectroscopic data with literature values.
4-allyl-2,6-dimethoxy-3-(3-methyl-2-butenyl)phenol protects against A23187-induced histamine release and TNF-α production and/or secretion from RBL-2H3 cells.

4-allyl-2,6-dimethoxy-3-(3-methyl-2-butenyl)phenol inhibits histamine release from rat basophilic leukemia (RBL-2H3) cells stimulated with A23187 and decreases TNF-alpha levels in RBL-2H3 cell supernatants after A23187 treatment.
Seconeolitsine protects Streptococcus pneumoniae from growth inhibition when TopA activity is overproduced in vivo.
Seconeolitsine shows strong interactions with the nucleotide-binding site of Streptococcus pneumoniae TopA in its closed protein conformation, as indicated by docking calculations.

Seconeolitsine inhibits the growth of Streptococcus pneumoniae, including clinical isolates resistant to fluoroquinolones and other antibiotics, at concentrations required to inhibit S. pneumoniae topoisomerase I relaxation activity.
Seconeolitsine is a phenanthrene alkaloid that inhibits Streptococcus pneumoniae DNA-topoisomerase I (TopA) activity and bacterial cell growth at concentrations around 17 µM.
dG-AL-DNA adducts are detected in the renal cortex of patients with endomyocardial fibrosis (EN) and at significantly lower levels in transitional cell cancers from residents of EN villages.

1,2,6,7-tetrahydroxy-4-methoxyphenanthrene exhibits vasorelaxant activity in rat aorta rings stimulated by 25 mM but not 60 mM K+, and inhibits Ba2+ current through CaV1.2 channels in a concentration-dependent manner.

1,5-dihydroxy-3,6-dimethoxy-2,7-diprenylxanthone shows selective activity against Plasmodium falciparum with an IC50 of 7.25 µM, while being inactive or highly inactive against other tested parasites and being non-cytotoxic at concentrations up to 64 µM.
1,5-dihydroxy-3,6-dimethoxy-2,7-diprenylxanthone displays selective activity against Plasmodium falciparum.
2-amino-3-(3,4,5-trimethoxybenzoyl)-4,5,6,7-tetrahydrothieno[b]pyridine is a molecular skeleton for tubulin polymerization inhibitors evaluated for antiproliferative activity and cell cycle effects.
(Z)-3-(5-ethyl-4-hydroxy-3-methoxy-1-naphthalenyl)-2-methyl-2-propenoic acid is an IL-1 inhibitor that acts at the transcriptional level without cyclo-oxygenase inhibitory activity.

(Z)-3-(5-ethyl-4-hydroxy-3-methoxy-1-naphthalenyl)-2-methyl-2-propeno ic acid inhibits IL-1 generation from human monocytes with an IC50 of 3.0 µM and from rat exudated macrophages with an IC50 of 1.4 µM.
(Z)-3-(5-ethyl-4-hydroxy-3-methoxy-1-naphthalenyl)-2-methyl-2-propeno ic acid shows potent antiinflammatory effects in animal models of inflammation.
8-methoxy-3,9-dihydroxy coumestan (LQB93) is a non-steroidal synthetic derivative of wedelolactone that inhibits Na+,K+-ATPase through a mechanism of action distinct from ouabain.
Burseranin is a podophyllotoxin-like 4aaryltetralin-type lignan isolated from the methanol extract of B. graveolens stems.
Demethylwedelolactone, a derivative of wedelolactone in which a methoxy group is replaced by a hydroxyl group, does not inhibit caspase-11 expression.
preussiafuran B is a highly substituted dibenzofuran derivative isolated from Preussia sp. CAD4, a tropical fungus from Enantia chlorantha Oliv.
Preussiafuran B is a highly substituted dibenzofuran isolated from the endophytic fungus Preussia sp. strain CAD4 associated with Enantia chlorantha.
preussiafuran B exhibits antiplasmodial activity against erythrocytic stages of chloroquine-resistant P. falciparum (NF54) and shows moderate cytotoxicity in L6 cell lines with an IC50 of 14.8 μM.
2,6-dimethyl-5-methoxyl-7-hydroxylchromone is a chromone derivative isolated from the endophytic fungus Xylomelasma sp. Samif07 derived from Salvia miltiorrhiza Bunge.
2,7-dihydroxy-5-methyl-1naphthoic acid is the native substrate of the NcsB1 O-Methyltransferase in Neocarzinostatin biosynthesis.

2-(2,6-dihydroxy-4-methylbenzoyl)-6-hydroxybenzoic acid is a newly isolated compound from the endophytic fungus G. chlorocephala cultivated with nicotinamide (10 μM) as a histone deacetylase (HADC) inhibitor.
Penialidin B is a polyketide isolated from Penicillium sp., an endophytic fungus from Garcinia nobilis, and exhibits antibacterial activity with MIC values of 10 µg/mL against S. aureus and E. coli.
Bellidifolin is a xanthone compound isolated from the roots of Swertia punctata.
1,5,8-trihydroxy-3-methoxyxanthone is a tetraoxygenated xanthone present in Swertia purpurascens Wall.
Neonambiterphenyl A is a newly identified p-terphenyl compound isolated from the EtOAc extract of cultured mycelium of Neonothopanus nambi.

Neonambiterphenyl A, a newly identified p-terphenyl from Neonothopanus nambi PW3, shows cytotoxicity against cancer cell lines KB, NCI-H187, and MCF-7 with IC50 values ranging from 1.45 to 49.31 µg/mL.

Tetramethylphenol content in Ephedra sinica Stapf can be quantitatively determined by measuring its peak area in electron-impact and SIM mode, with a linear relationship between concentration (0.3 to 13.2 µg/mL) and peak area in the range of 0.3 to 13.2 µg/mL.
Trimethylhydroquinone can react with compound 3 under acidic conditions to form a 2,2-disubstituted chromene precursor of troglitazone.

Trimethylhydroquinone derivatives have been synthesized and evaluated for anti-lipid peroxidation activity, inhibition of rat basophilic leukemia-1 (RBL-1) cell 5-lipoxygenase, and reduction of 48 h homologous passive cutaneous anaphylaxis (PCA) activity in rats.
Pentagallic acid gallate increases renal superoxide dismutase (SOD) expression in hyperoxaluric rat kidneys.
T4HN is an intermediate in the melanin biosynthesis pathway of C. lagenarium.
Germicidin H is a polyketide compound identified as a candidate metabolite from Streptomyces sp. 18A01 based on m/z values of 219.075 and demonstrated HK2 inhibitory activity.
3''-deoxy-6'-O-desmethylcandidusin B, a terphenyl compound from Penicillium chermesinum (ZH4-E2), inhibits acetylcholinesterase (AChE) with an IC50 value of 7.8 µM.
Rubrofusarin is a compound isolated from the bioactive methanol-soluble extract of Cassia obliqua stem and fruits that exhibits antimycobacterial activity.
Periplocoside E is a pregnane glycoside isolated from Periploca sepium Bge that acts as a potent immunosuppressive agent by directly inhibiting T cell activation in vitro and in vivo.
Tropeoside A1 is a disaccharide furostanol saponin isolated from red onion (Allium cepa var. Tropea) that possesses antispasmodic activity in the guinea-pig isolated ileum.
A pig microsomal enzyme catalyzes the 7a-hydroxylation of 3a-hydroxy-5-cholestenoic acid, but not cholesterol.
Pig liver microsomes catalyze the 7β-hydroxylation of 3α-hydroxy-5-cholestenic acid, with 90% of the activity found in a complete system containing NADPH or NADPH-cytochrome P-450 reductase.
Phyllanthoside is a natural product identified as a novel inhibitor of eukaryotic protein synthesis, specifically interfering with translation elongation and functioning in vivo and in vitro.
25-thiacholesterol iodide is a potent inhibitor of delta 24-sterol methyl transferase (24-SMT) in yeast, with an in vivo IC50 of 0.07 µM for inhibiting C-24 methylation of sterols.
Chloro cholesterol undergoes allylic oxidation to form 7-keto cholesterol in high yield when catalyzed by T-HYDRO® and Rh 2 (cap) 4.

Cholesterol-lysine, when used to construct a lipopeptide analog, does not induce strong T cell-dependent protective immunity against lethal HSV-1 infection in mice compared to Nepsilon-palmitoyl-lysine.
Cholesterol-lysine conjugate is used as a lipid in solid lipid nanoparticles (SLNs) to increase entrapment of metformin and improve its topical delivery.
Cholesterol-lysine-based lipopeptides induce a Th2-type immune response rather than a Th1 response in mice.
3-acetyltylosin resistance in Staphylococcus aureus is associated with decreased sensitivity of ribosomes (type I) or decreased uptake (type II), and is not inducible.
In type I ribosomes of Staphylococcus aureus, 3-acetyltylosin causes little or no inhibition of protein synthesis in vitro.
In type II ribosomes of Staphylococcus aureus, 3-acetyltylosin does not inhibit protein synthesis in vitro.

Olivomycin B is a natural aureolic acid antibiotic whose structure-activity relationships for drug-DNA complex formation and cytotoxicity have been studied by varying acyl residues in its sugar moieties.
Olivomycin B contains two acyl residues in its carbohydrate chain, which confers high DNA-binding constants, cytotoxicity, and potent topoisomerase I inhibitory activity.
Chromomycin induces erythroid differentiation in K562 cells, as evidenced by increased expression of Hb Portland and Hb Gower 1 globin genes after 7 days of treatment at 6 nM.
Chromomycin induces erythroid differentiation of K562 cells, leading to increased production of Hb Gower 1 and Hb Portland.
Chromomycin augments erythroid differentiation and increases g-globin mRNA content in K562 cells.

3beta,21-dihydroxy-5-pregnen-20-one (3betaDelta5-21-OHpregnenolone) is a minor metabolite of progesterone in human keratinocytes, suggesting the presence of reverse Delta4minus signDelta5 isomerase activity.
nebrosteroid I is a newly isolated 19γ-oxygenated steroid from the Formosan soft coral Nephthea chabroli.
NMF (N-methylformamide) at a non-toxic dose (1%) can potentiate the cytotoxicity of 5-fluorouracil in human colon cancer HT29 cells when administered after 5-fluorouracil.
D-alanine-D-alanine is an inhibitor analogue of D-alanine-D-lactate that enables crystallization of Enterococcus facieum D-alanine-D-lactate ligase (VanA).

Leu-Val-Gly is a part of the N-terminal binding sequence of human cystatin C and, when incorporated into a peptide (Boc-Leu-Val-Agly-OEt), confers efficient reversible inhibition of papain and cathepsin K with Ki values in the nanomolar range.
Pyloricidin B, a peptidic moiety composed of L-valine-L-leucine, exhibits potent and highly selective anti-Helicobacter pylori activity.
Ala-Ser-Leu is a novel angiotensin-converting enzyme (ACE) inhibitory peptide identified from silkworm pupa (Bombyx mori) protein hydrolysate, with an IC50 value of 102.15 µM.

Quinoxaline-2-mercapto-acetylurea analogues inhibit viral egress of Marburg and Ebola viruses by targeting the PPxY motif in the viral matrix protein, thereby blocking virus-host interactions required for virus egress and dissemination.
Quinoxaline-2-mercaptoacetyl urea analogs have been tested for antiviral properties against Marburg and Ebola virus in HEK293T cells using VP40 VLP budding assays.
Quinoxalin-2-mercapto-acetyl-urea analogs exhibit low nanomolar potency in inhibiting viral egress in Marburg and Ebola VP40 VLP budding assays in HEK293T cells.
Alpha-fluoro-ureidopropionic acid (FUPA) is a metabolite of 5-fluorouracil that can be quantified in human plasma using hydrophilic interaction chromatography with isocratic elution.

1-methyl-2-p-allophanoylbenzylhydrazine hydrobromide is a methylhydrazine derivative with strong cytostatic activity, capable of inhibiting the growth of Ehrlich carcinoma, Crocker-sarcoma 256, and epithelioma of the uterus T 8.
3-oxoisohexanoate significantly decreases intracellular pH and both proton and pyruvate efflux in bloodstream trypomastigotes of Trypanosoma brucei.
Chlorofluoroacetic acid is converted to glyoxylic acid by rhGSTZ, with approximately 93% of the deuterium retained in the product.
Chlorofluoroacetic acid is biotransformed by glutathione transferase zeta (GSTZ) to glyoxylic acid with negligible inactivation of the enzyme.

Leucine methylphosphonic acid, when combined with a hydroxamic acid group, forms a compound with significant inhibitory activity against Helicoverpa armigera larval growth and digestive aminopeptidase activity.
N6-(2-methylbutyryl) lysine is a novel lysine acylation structure identified in biological samples, subject to sirtuin regulation, and relevant to aging and disease research.
N6-(2-methylbutyryl) lysine is a novel acylated lysine modification detected in mouse liver, kidney, heart, and brain using HPLC-MS/MS quantification.
Lactic acid isobutylester (LAIE) inhibits lactate-induced proton influx in rabbit corneal epithelium by 47% at 1 mM concentration during pre-perfusion.

(R)-3-methyl-2-oxopentanoate (R-KMV) is a 2-oxo acid derived from valine whose fractional renal excretion in patients with maple syrup urine disease ranges from 0.1% to 35.9% and is significantly correlated with plasma R-KMV concentrations.

Conjugation of the Cysteine-Arginine-Glycine-Valine (Cys-TAT/RGD) peptide to the PMMA-b-PHEMA/DOX/Cys-TAT/RGD structure via a carbamate bond enhances cellular uptake in integrin α v β 3 receptor-positive U87 cells.
The formation of trichloroacrylamide during chlorination is reduced by 50–92% when UV/H2O2 pretreatment is applied to high-dissolved organic nitrogen (DON) water.
Sorbic acid nitrite, given at 7.5–1 mg/kg orally for 30 days, increases mitotic index in mouse bone marrow, indicating an effect on spindle activity.
Sorbic acid nitrite treatment may increase the mitotic index by affecting the spindle apparatus.
2-fluoro-cis,cis-muconate is a substrate for chloromuconate cycloisomerase from R. eutropha JMP134, exhibiting high substrate affinity but relatively low catalytic activity (k cat).
2-fluoro-cis,cis-muconate is a relatively poor substrate for the pJP4-encoded chloromuconate cycloisomerase TfdD, showing the lowest specificity constant among tested cis,cismuconates.
Trans-3-chloroacrylic acid is produced as a dead-end product during the growth of Pseudomonas sp. EK1, while R. erythropolis strains EK2 and EK5 degrade this compound.
cVA induces gender-specific differences in behavioral response in Drosophila, which are derived from sexually dimorphic third-order olfactory neurons.
The mechanism of WhiB3 4Fe-4S cluster degradation is similar to that of the fumarate nitrate regulator.
Fumarate nitrate regulation, mediated by the Fnr protein, is involved in the iron-dependent regulation of the MnSOD gene (sodA) in Escherichia coli.
Avian spermatozoa contain comparatively high concentrations of 22:4n-6 fatty acids within their phospholipids.
Octadecenoic and octadecadienoic acid exhibit differential expression in the local ablation versus palliative care group in HCC patients.
Octadecenoic and octadecadienoic acid display differential expression in hepatocellular carcinoma (HCC) patients undergoing local ablation versus palliative care.
n-hexylamine sorbate exhibits antifungal activity against pathogenic fungi.
15-MPA is a pinusolide derivative isolated from Biota orientalis leaves that exhibits neuroprotective activity against glutamate-induced toxicity in primary cultured rat cortical cells.
1-aminocyclopropane-1-acrylic acid (ACC) is an intermediate in the ethylene biosynthetic pathway, formed from S-adenosyl methionine and subsequently converted to ethylene.

Fumaric acid can be hyperpolarized using a trityl radical and a gadolinium chelate in phosphate buffer at 180°C and 10 bar, achieving a polarization range of 7% to 10% in a 3.35-T Hypersense polarizer.
9-KOT (9-ketooctadecatrienoic acid) induces waving of Arabidopsis thaliana roots and growth arrest of lateral roots.
The inhibitory effect of 12-MTA on PC3 cell growth is at least partially due to its inhibition of 5-HETE formation.
12-MTA inhibits the formation of 5-HETE in PC3 cells, and this inhibition is associated with reduced cell proliferation.
Addition of 25 mg/ml 12-MTA inhibits both 5-HETE formation and cell proliferation in PC3 cells to the same extent.
5-hydroxyl-5-methyl-2-hexenoic acid is an antifungal compound isolated from the mycelium of Actinoplanes sp. HBDN08.
4-carbomethoxy-2-(2'-hydroxyphenyl)benzoxazole is a simplified analog of UK-1 that displays cytotoxic activity against cancer cell lines and forms complexes with metal ions such as Mg2+ and Zn2+.
Methyl 2-fluorobenzoate undergoes enzymatic dihydroxylation by E. coli JM109 (pDTG601A) to yield one diol regioselectively.
Methyl 2-ethylbenzoate serves as a substrate for P450 BM3-catalyzed biotransformation, yielding optically active 3methylphthalides with 7% yield and 31% enantiomeric excess.
Methyl 2-ethylbenzoate serves as a substrate for P450 BM3-catalyzed C-H activation, resulting in the formation of isocoumarin as a by-product due to ·hydroxylation at the ethyl moiety.

Methyl 2-ethylbenzoate can undergo enantioselective benzylic hydroxylation by P450 BM3, yielding products with enantiomeric excesses (ee) ranging from 16% to 76% depending on the enzyme variant and reaction conditions.
Methyl 2-ethylbenzoate can be oxidized by P450 BM3 variants through protein engineering to produce 3-methylisobenzofuran-1(3H)-one.
Methyl 2-(trifluoromethyl)benzoate serves as a substrate for fungal enzyme-catalyzed formation of arene oxide and oxepine metabolites during aromatic hydroxylation.
CFTHP is an antifungal phenazine derivative produced by Streptomyces lomondensis S015, characterized by NMR.
In anguibactin biosynthesis in Vibrio anguillarum, the enzyme angE is responsible for activating 2,3-dihydroxybenzoyl acetate (DHBA), a precursor molecule, by adenylylation.

Methyl 2-(5-fluoro-2-hydroxyphenyl)-1H-benzo[d]imidazole-5-carboxylate induces mitotic arrest and apoptosis in HeLa cancer cells by specifically targeting the microtubule system and causing G2-M phase cell cycle arrest.

Methyl 2-(5-fluoro-2-hydroxyphenyl)-1H-benzo[d]imidazole-5-carboxylate demonstrates synergistic effects with conventional anticancer drugs such as colchicine, nocodazole, paclitaxel, and doxorubicin in cervical cancer treatment.

5-(2-hydroxybenzoyl)-3-methyl-1-(2-pyridinyl)pyrazol-4-carboxylic acid methyl ester forms Ni(II) complexes that exhibit relatively high cytotoxic activity towards HL-60 and NALM-6 leukaemia cells and WM-115 melanoma cells.
Benzoxazole carboxylic acid can be converted to its methyl ester via a catalytic reaction with CO2 using tzNHC-copper(I) complexes as catalysts.
NPA-binding proteins are proposed as essential elements of the auxin efflux machinery, functioning in conjunction with carrier proteins at the basal end of transport cells.
1-hydroxy-6-methoxyphenazine, a phenazine derivative lacking N-oxides, shows reduced cytotoxicity in MOLM-13 AML cells compared to iodinin analogues with N-oxides.
o-methoxycarbonylphenyl 2,3,4,6-tetra-O-benzyl-1-thio-beta-D-galactopyranoside is a thioglycosyl donor used for synthesizing glycoconjugate polymers with Gb(3) and isoGb(3) clusters as side chains.
o-methoxycarbonylphenyl 2,3,4,6-tetra-O-benzyl-1-thio-beta-D-galactopyranoside-derived glycoconjugate polymers with Gb(3) clusters are highly active in inhibiting Shiga toxin toxicity.
5,7,2'-trihydroxyflavone inhibits activation of the Epstein-Barr virus early antigen (EBV-EA) and suppresses skin tumor promotion in a two-stage carcinogenesis test in vivo in mice.
Titanocene salicylic acid complex binds to hIAPP19-37 via hydrophobic interaction and inhibits its aggregation.
CTols content is positively correlated with scald development in the blushed and shaded peels of unbagged 'd'Anjou' pears, as well as in the whole peel of bagged fruit.
The crystal structure of N-(4-chlorobenzoyl)benzenesulfonamide features N–H bonds in the C–SO2–NH–C(O) segments anti to the C=O bonds, a conformation observed in related N-aromatic sulfonamides.
Dibenzazecine derivatives exhibit slightly improved selectivity profiles toward the D1 dopamine receptor subtype family with the same or higher affinities.
N-benzensulfonyl 2-(2-pyridylethyl) -benzimidazole derivatives have been synthesized and studied for their antiviral activity.
Iso-propylphenyl sulfone reacts smoothly with imines in the (phenylsulfonyl)alkylation reaction, providing the expected products in good yield with excellent diastereoselectivity.
Iso-propylphenyl sulfone reacts smoothly with imines in the (phenylsulfonyl)alkylation reaction, providing the expected products in good yield with excellent diastereoselectivity.

S-(Difluoromethyl)benzenesulfonothioate (PhSO2SCF2H) is an effective difluoromethylthiolating reagent that acts as a radical acceptor, combining with alkyl or aryl radicals generated from Ag/persulfate-involved oxidation.
9AAz induces a RecA:dsDNA complex with a bp:RecA ratio of approximately 1.5, suggesting that 1 RecA subunit binds to each acridine chromophore intercalated into the DNA.
1-(Benzothiazol-2-yl)-1-(4-chlorophenyl)ethanol acts as a positive allosteric modulator (PAM) of the calcium-sensing receptor (CaSR) in a functional cell-based assay.
Benzoxazole carboxylic acid can be converted to its methyl ester via a catalytic reaction with CO2 using tzNHC-copper(I) complexes as catalysts.
Diphenylborinic anhydride is a potent analog of 2-APB and acts as a vascular gap junction-blocking agent affecting junctional and nonjunctional conductances in native arteriolar cells.
Quinolin-chlorobenzothioate (QCBT7) induces proteasome inhibition, hypoxic response, endoplasmic reticulum stress, and glycolysis, leading to cancer cell death.
Quinolin-chlorobenzothioate (QCBT7) is a proteasome inhibitor that targets the regulatory subunit of the proteasome and induces cytotoxicity in cancer cell lines.

6-chloro-2-styrylquinazolin-4(3H)one is a styrylquinazolinone scaffold with potent activity as an inhibitor of tubulin polymerization and cell growth, exemplified by an IC50 of 0.001 µg/ml for L1210 cell growth inhibition and 2.5 µM for tubulin polymerization inhibition.

The inhibition of HIV-1 reverse transcriptase by nitrophenyl phenyl sulfone requires a protein segment between residues 158 and 190, and possibly an additional segment between residues 192 and 202, for sensitivity.
3-phenylquinazolinone compounds, such as icFSP1, act as inhibitors of ferroptosis suppressor protein 1 (FSP1) by disrupting the FSP1-membrane interaction and inducing ferroptosis in tumor cells.

3-phenylquinazolinone compounds are in vivo-efficacious inhibitors of hFSP1 that trigger ferroptosis by causing dissociation of FSP1 from the membrane and promoting FSP1 condensate formation via phase separation.

N-(4-sulfamylbenzoyl)benzylamine (SBB) is a class of inhibitors that bind to Human Carbonic Anhydrase II (HCAII) via aromatic interactions, and fluorination of the benzyl group modifies edge-to-face interactions with Phe131, thereby influencing inhibitor affinity.

2-n-butyl-4-chloro-1-{[2-(1H-tetrazol-5-yl)- biphenyl-4-yl]methyl}imidazole-5-carboxylic acid (EXP 3174), the active metabolite of losartan, antagonizes Ang II-induced K(+)-stimulated [(3)H] noradrenaline release in the rat hypothalamus via the angiotensin (AT1) receptor subtype.
Bis-9-aminoacridine derivatives that are unsubstituted or have limited substitution on the acridine short axis can bind to DNA without requiring binding site exclusion.
Bis-9-aminoacridine induces conformational changes in RecA and DNA similar to ATP gamma S, as measured by circular dichroism.

DBP can undergo anodic oxidation in the presence of dA or ribonucleoside A, forming adducts such as DBP-10-WdA, DBP-10-N7Ade, and DBP-10-N6A, depending on the molar ratio and electron equivalents consumed.

7H-pyrrolo[2,3-d]pyrimidin-4-amine derivatives with a phenyl group at C(2) and a dimethyl substitution at C(5) and C(6) show a sixfold increase in affinity for the A1 adenosine receptor compared to the non-disubstituted analog.

Substitution at the exocyclic nitrogen of 7H-pyrrolo[2,3-d]pyrimidin-4-amine with various heterocycles, such as pyridin-2-yl, pyridin-4-yl, thiophen-2-yl, and furan-2-yl, retains high affinity for the A1 adenosine receptor.
3,9-dihydro-9-oxo-5H-imidazo[1,2-a]purine is a tricyclic system that can be incorporated into analogues of acyclovir and ganciclovir to yield compounds with antiviral activity.
[1,2,4]triazolo[1,5-a]pyrimidin-7(4H)-ones are synthesized by condensation of 3-amino-1,2,4-triazoles with ethyl acetoacetate or ethyl 4,4,4-trifluoroacetoacetate in refluxing toluene.

[1,2,4]-triazolo [1,5-a]pyrimidine-7(4H)-one derivatives act as anticonvulsants with potent activity, clear action mechanisms on GABAergic neurotransmission, low neurotoxicity, and high protective index.
Derivatives of the imidazo[1,2-b][1,2,4,5]tetrazine ring system have been reported to possess antitumor activities.

Imidazo[1,2-b][1,2,4,5]tetrazine structures are formed as products from the reaction of 1-amino-2-methylthio-4-phenylimidazole with hydrazonoyl halides, involving hydrazidine intermediates and elimination of methanethiol.
Imidazo[1,2-b][1,2,4,5]tetrazine 1a-d serves as a starting material for the synthesis of various compounds in the presence of thiol and triethylamine.
Imidazo[1,2-b][1,2,4,5]tetrazine 1a-d serves as a starting material for the synthesis of various compounds in the presence of thiol and triethylamine.
3h is an imidazo[1,2-b][1,2,4,5]tetrazine compound with relatively high antimycobacterial activity against M. tuberculosis H37Rv.
Substituted imidazo[1,2-b][1,2,4,5]tetrazines exhibit promising minimum inhibitory concentrations (MICs) against both drug-susceptible and drug-resistant Mycobacterium tuberculosis strains.
[1,2,4]triazolo[1,5-a]pyrimidine indole derivatives are designed as anticancer agents that suppress the ERK signaling pathway.
7-oxo-4,7-dihydropyrazolo[1,5-a]pyrimidine-3-carboxylic acid derivatives are potent in vitro angiotensin II (AII) antagonists but lack oral antihypertensive activity.
6-ethynylpurine can be synthesized in isolated yields of 71–99% via copper-free Sonogashira coupling of unprotected chloropurines using 2 mol % Na2PdCl4 and 4 mol % cataCXium F sulf.

Some 6-ethynylpurine bases and nucleosides are cytostatic and can be used as intermediates in syntheses such as conjugate additions or [2 + 2 + 2]-cycloadditions for compounds with cytostatic or anti-HCV activity.

The trifluoromethyl group at the 2-position of the 3-phenyl-4H-pyrazolo[1,5-a]pyrimidin-7-one scaffold is the preferred substituent for potent inhibition of M. tuberculosis DXS, with ethyl, methyl, and hydrogen substituents resulting in progressively lower activity.
The 5-naphthenyl analog of 3-phenyl-4H-pyrazolo[1,5-a]pyrimidin-7-one is a more potent inhibitor of M. tuberculosis DXS than other analogs.
An electron-donating group para-methoxy on the phenyl ring of 3-phenyl-4H-pyrazolo[1,5-a]pyrimidin-7-one enhances its inhibitory activity against M. tuberculosis DXS.
A substituent at the 7-position of 1,3-dimethyl-(1H,8H)-imidazo[2,1-f]purine-2,4-dione is essential for receptor affinity and selectivity, particularly towards 5-HT 1A and 5-HT 7 receptors.

Rhododaurichromanic acid A is a natural chromane with important medicinal value, and can be accessed via a synthetic route involving a sequential anionic pericyclic strategy including a Knoevenagel condensation and 6π-electron electrocyclic ring closure.
Rhododaurichromanic acid A has been synthesized by a bioinspired strategy.
siphonodictyal B2 is a sulfated compound isolated from the burrowing sponge Aka coralliphagum and its biological activity is linked to the presence of an ortho-hydroquinone moiety.
Neocurdione, a sesquiterpene constituent, dose-dependently inhibits the increase in sAST and sALT induced by D-GalN/LPS in mice at oral doses of 12.5–50 mg/kg.

3,4-dihydroxyclerodan-13Z-en-15-oic acid is a clerodane diterpene isolated from Detarium microcarpum fruit pulp extract that shows both antifungal activity against Cladosporium cucumerinum and inhibition of acetylcholinesterase.
(3R)-5,6,7-trihydroxy-3-isopropyl-3-methylisochroman-1-one (TIM) is a novel antioxidant isolated from Alpinia katsumadai Hayata with neuroprotective effects.

(3R)-5,6,7-trihydroxy-3-isopropyl-3-methylisochroman-1-one (TIM) protects mouse photoreceptor-derived 661W cells from lipoteichoic acid-induced toxicity by decreasing intracellular ROS and MDA levels, enhancing SOD activity and GSH levels, and increasing Nrf2 protein expression.

(3R)-5,6,7-trihydroxy-3-isopropyl-3-methylisochroman-1-one (TIM) attenuates the oxidative stress and inflammatory response in 661W cells exposed to lipoteichoic acid, including decreased pro-inflammatory factors IL-1beta, IL-12, and TNFalpha, and inhibition of nuclear factor kappa B and Egr1, Fosl1, and Lox12 gene expression.
Glyasperin A is a known flavonoid isolated from Macaranga gigantea leaves and exhibits cytotoxic activity against P-388 cells with an IC50 of 6.0 µM.
Varioxiranol I is a newly isolated lactone compound from the sponge-associated fungus Emericella variecolor, exhibiting moderate cytotoxic activity against a panel of tumor cell lines.
Cacospongionolide B inhibits the human synovial group II phospholipase A2 with potency similar to manoalide.
mycaperoxide H was isolated from the methanol extract of Mycale sp. sponge and purified by ODS flash chromatography and ODS HPLC, yielding 1.2 mg.
Mycaperoxide H exhibits cytotoxicity against HeLa cells with an IC50 value of 0.8 µg/mL.
Mycaperoxide H is cytotoxic to HeLa cells with an IC50 value of 0.8 µg/mL.
Bromophycolide H exhibits potent antimalarial activity against Plasmodium falciparum with an IC50 of 0.3–0.9 μM.
Bromophycolide H is a 15- or 16-membered lactone diterpenoid that exhibits potent antimalarial activity with IC50 values of 0.3–0.9 mM.
PbTx-3 is a brevetoxin with a different polyether ladder backbone that can induce swelling of single living frog myelinated nerve fibres.
Annulin C is a polyketide that acts as a submicromolar inhibitor of indoleamine 2,3-dioxygenase (IDO).

Hygrocin C, isolated from Streptomyces sp. SCSGAA 0027, inhibits biofilm formation of Staphylococcus aureus and Bacillus amyloliquefaciens at concentrations below the minimum inhibitory concentration (MIC).
Analogues of endomorphin-2 containing (2S)-2-methyl-3-(2,6-dimethyl-4-hydroxyphenyl)propanoic acid as the N-terminal amino group are moderately potent mu opioid antagonists.

Stilbene isothiocyanate can be used to label the N-terminus of thymopentin analogues, resulting in a mono-labeled analogue (SITS-TP-5) that retains the ability to induce Thy 1.2 surface marker expression on nude mouse prothymocytes and inhibit radiolabeled thymopentin binding to human lymphocytes.

2,3-dihydro-3-methyl-1,4-benzodioxin can be synthesized by reacting propargylic tertiary carbonates with benzene-1,2-diol, resulting in a mixture of regioisomers with the major stereoisomer having E-stereochemistry.
MBDB is capable of producing stimulus effects in animals that are best classified as PMMA-like, rather than amphetamine-like.

3,4-Methylenedioxy-N-benzylamine is an indirect serotonin (5-HT) releaser that increases prepulse inhibition (PPI) in 5-HT 1B knockout (1BKO) mice but has no effect in wild-type (WT) mice at 10 mg/kg.
Benzodioxol-5-ylpropargyl alcohol reacts with various thiols to produce propargyl sulphides in moderate to high yields under Lewis acid-catalyzed conditions.

PEITC increases MX accumulation in BCRP-overexpressing MCF-7/MX100 cells by 3.5- to 5.8-fold at 50 M, and enhances MX accumulation in NCI-H460/MX20 cells by 2.4- to 3.1-fold at 50 M, indicating inhibition of BCRP.
Benzodioxol-5-ylpropargyl alcohol reacts with various thiols to produce propargyl sulphides in moderate to high yields under Lewis acid-catalyzed conditions.
2-phenyloxazoline serves as an essential directing group for ruthenium-catalyzed ortho C-H bond carbonylation in the benzene ring, enabling propionylation with high site selectivity.
A phenyloxazoline-based hydroxamate inhibits Escherichia coli LpxC with a K(i) of approximately 50 nM.
A phenyloxazoline-based hydroxamate does not inhibit Aquifex aeolicus LpxC.
4-benzopyranol exhibits greater binding affinity to serum albumin than other benzopyranones.
bpV(pic) confers neuroprotection after cerebral ischemia–reperfusion injury by inhibiting PTEN lipid phosphatase activity and activating ERK 1/2.
HMMA is a metabolite of MDMA that can be analyzed by LC-MS using a linear gradient of 10 mM ammonium formate buffer (pH 3.5, formic acid) and methanol (5% to 40% in 20 min) as the mobile phase.

a-MeDA is a highly reactive compound formed during the metabolism of MDA and MDMA, and is implicated as a main cause of hepatotoxicity by inducing loss of GSH homeostasis, decreasing antioxidant enzyme activities, and causing cell death in rat hepatocytes.

The aminomethylpyrimidine ring in 6-methylenedioxyphenyl-aminomethylpyrimidine forms an important and symmetrical hydrogen bonding network with Y662, N710, and two glutamic acid residues (E205, E206) of DPP-IV.
3,4-methylenedioxyphenyl-2-butanamine (BDB) is excreted into perspiration, with peak concentrations of 23 ng/patch detected in skin patches analyzed by GC/MS after administration of 100 mg MBDB.
6-phenyl-dibenzo(d,f)(1,3)dioxepines undergo isomerization to a cyclohexadienone spiro benzo[b]furan under electron ionization (EI) mass spectrometry.

6-phenyl-dibenzo(d,f)(1,3)dioxepine exists as three isomeric sets: o-, m-, and p-substituted isomers, which are difficult to distinguish by EI mass spectrometry but can be differentiated by CID fragmentation patterns.
Piperonyl cinnamate exhibits antileishmanial activity against Leishmania infantum with an IC50 of 42.80 µM and a selectivity index (SI) greater than 32.86.
ADTN, a D2 dopamine agonist, selectively inhibits Mg 2÷-stimulated ATPase activity in vitro, indicating a specific effect on this enzyme.

3-n-butyl-2,3-dihydro-1H-isoindol-1-one exhibits antiplatelet activity, likely through modulation of the AA-thromboxane A2 cascade and involvement of the NO-cyclic guanosine monophosphate signaling pathway.
2,3-dihydro-1-butyl-2-thioxoindole-3-one demonstrates enhanced antimycobacterial activity and is more active than isoniazide against several mycobacterial strains.

2,3-dihydro-1-butyl-2-thioxoindole-3-one exhibits in vitro antimycobacterial activity against Mycobacterium tuberculosis, M. kansasii, M. fortuitum, M. intracellulare, and M. avium, showing higher activity than isoniazid.
Etazepine is a new anticonvulsant that protects mice and rats from a wide variety of convulsant agents at doses 16–45 times lower than those causing neurotoxic effects.
AOAA is an anticonvulsant agent whose effects against penicillin-induced seizures diminish with pretreatment times beyond 75 minutes.
6-methoxy n-butyl phthalide undergoes oxidative metabolism in rats following oral administration, producing six detectable metabolites in urine within 0–24 hours.
2-methyl-3-pentadecyl-1,4-naphthoquinone is used as an internal standard to improve the accuracy of vitamin K homologue determination.
2-methyl-3-pentadecyl-1,4-naphthoquinone is used as an internal standard in the post-column photoproduct (PO) detection of vitamin K homologues in human plasma.
Tubingenoic anhydride A is a novel compound with antifungal properties identified from Aspergillus tubingensis.

tubingenoic anhydride A is a novel metabolite produced by Aspergillus tubingensis strain OY907 that exhibits antifungal activity against N. crassa, with wild-type N. crassa being highly sensitive to 0.8 mg/mL of the purified compound.
Tubingenoic anhydride A is a polyketide metabolite produced by Marine-Derived Aspergillus tubingensis that exhibits antifungal activity against N. crassa with a MIC of 330 μM.
HQNO is not produced from HHQ and is not overproduced or labeled when HHQ-d4 is added to PA14 cultures, indicating that HHQ is not a precursor of HQNO in the HAQ biosynthetic pathway.
Bucolome is a uricosuric agent that intensifies the anticoagulant effect of warfarin.
Bucolome increases plasma unbound fractions of (S)- and (R)-warfarin by 2-fold in vivo.
Gamma-heptanolactone can be assimilated as a carbon source by Rhodococcus erythropolis R138, and its degradation is mediated by the lactonase QsdA.
2-nonyl-4-quinolone-N-oxide is a 2-alkyl-4-quinolone-N-oxide (AHQNO) and a biosynthetic precursor for 2-nonyl-3-hydroxy-4-quinolones in Pseudomonas aeruginosa.
2-nonyl-4-quinolone N-oxide (NQNO) is one of the three major 4-quinolone N-oxides produced by Pseudomonas aeruginosa and is among the most abundant AQNOs in culture medium.
2-nonyl-4-quinolone N-oxide is a native bacterial quinolone that inhibits diatom growth through specific inhibition of both photosynthesis and respiration in vivo.

2-ethyl-1-indanone undergoes asymmetric α-arylation with chloroarenes catalyzed by Ni(COD)2 and (ε)-BINAP, resulting in coupled products with high enantiomeric excess (90–99%) and good to excellent yield.
7-ethylisatin is a more effective inhibitor of ANP-stimulated cardiac GC activity compared to isatin.
Copper dichromate dihydrate (CuCr 2 O 7 Ç(H 2 O) 2) is present as a dominant component in CCA-treated wood waste after PFL treatment.
Silver chromate is the fibrillar precipitate formed in nervous tissue as a result of Golgi chromate-silver impregnation, as confirmed by X-ray diffraction.
Silver chromate is relatively soluble, as no insoluble remnants were visible in light microscope after 1 hour treatment of Golgi-impregnated sections in distilled water.
Quinolinium dichromate acts as an oxidant in acidic medium, reacting with isoniazid in a 4:1 stoichiometry to form a complex that decomposes via a protonated species.
Quinolinium dichromate-mediated oxidation of diols proceeds almost twice as fast in D2O than in H2O.
Quinolinium dichromate in water oxidizes vicinal and nonvicinal diols to their corresponding alpha-hydroxy carbonyl compounds.
Cobalt Tungsten Oxide Hydroxide Hydrate (CTOHH) catalyzes the selective oxidation of aromatic alcohols to their respective aldehydes and ketones using molecular oxygen as the oxidant.
Bistrimethylsilyl chromate can catalyze the oxidation of primary and secondary alcohols to aldehydes and ketones, respectively, using H5IO6 in high yields with only 3.8% mol of the catalyst.
Copper nitrate induces dimerization of bovine serum albumin by binding to its metal sites.

241 Am nitrate is a pure chemical form that can be effectively treated by intravenous administration, injection, infusion, aerosol, or oral administration of Zn-DTPA, especially when treatment is initiated shortly after exposure.
Inhalation exposure of mice to 241 Am(N03)3 at doses of 550 Bq results in a dose-dependent reduction in the total number of pulmonary alveolar macrophages (PAM).

Nitroglycerin does not directly yield nitric oxide (NO) or an ⅐ NO-like species that activates soluble guanylate cyclase; instead, cellular metabolism is required to convert it to a species that induces ⅐ NO-like activation of soluble guanylate cyclase.

Nitroglycerin, administered sublingually, produces a venodilator response that is attenuated by 40% in the placebo group and reduced by 10% in the enalapril group after 74 hours of chronic transdermal patch application, compared to acute 2-hour postpatch responses.
Nitroglycerin is a vasodilator whose effectiveness in inducing a vasodilatory response can be reduced in patients with heart failure, potentially due to activation of vasoconstricive mechanisms.
Ferric hydroxide nitrate forms uniformly sized spherical micelles with a diameter of 70 Å in aqueous solution and exhibits magnetic and Mossbauer properties identical to those of ferritin.
ACOH is used as a leaching agent for cathode material spent LIBs containing Li, Co, and Mn, with optimal conditions being 3 M ACOH, 7.5 vol% H2O2, 70°C, 40 min, and 20 g/L pulp density.

The strongest OH stretching Raman band in iron(II) sulfate tetrahydrate, at 3253 cm⁻¹, is attributed to symmetric OH stretching vibrations and sharpens to 3220 cm⁻¹ upon cooling to 40 K, consistent with increased hydrogen bonding.

Iron(II) sulfate tetrahydrate exhibits Raman lines at 446 and 456 cm⁻¹ (doubledt), 456 and 479 cm⁻¹ (doubledt), 423 and 494 cm⁻¹ (bands), and 429 and 451 cm⁻¹ (doubledt), which are attributed to the doubly degenerate ν2 (E) bending mode of the sulfate ion at 450 cm⁻¹ in aqueous solution.

In iron(II) sulfate tetrahydrate, the symmetric ν1 (A1) stretching mode of the sulfate ion shifts from 977 to 990 and up to 1018 cm⁻¹ as the number of water molecules decreases, due to changes in site symmetry and coordination bonding.

Iron(II) sulfate tetrahydrate exhibits up to 10 individual OH stretching Raman bands between 3254 and 3594 cm⁻¹ as the temperature decreases to 8 K, indicating extensive hydrogen bonding within the crystal lattice.
5-hydroperoxymethyl-2'-deoxyuridine is a gamma radiolysis product of thymidine.
The genetic mutagenicity of 5-hydroperoxymethyl-2'-deoxyuridine is attributed to its hydroperoxide moiety, which reacts with Fe to generate hydroxyl radicals that oxidize DNA bases.
3',5'-diamino-3',5'-dideoxythymidine undergoes regioselective acylation by Candida antarctica lipase B, favoring the 5'-position over the 3'-position by up to 80-fold.

Medium hydro-Candida antarctica lipase B (CAL-B) catalyzes the regioselective acylation of 3',5'-diamino-3',5'-dideoxythymidine with nonactivated esters, favoring the 5'-position over the 3'-position by up to 80-fold.
5-dodecyloxymethyl-2ʹdeoxyuridine exhibits antitubercular activity with a MIC of 20 µg/ml against Mycobacterium tuberculosis H37Rv strain and 10 µg/ml against the drug-resistant MS-115 strain.

5-dodecyloxymethyl-2'-deoxyuridine is a C-5 modified nucleoside derivative with a dodecyloxymethyl substituent that exhibits significant antitubercular activity against M. tuberculosis H37Rv and drug-resistant MS-115 strains, with a MIC of 20 µg/mL.
5-dodecyloxymethyl-2'deoxyuridine derivatives, when used as 3'- and 5'-modified compounds, exhibit antimycobacterial activity and cytotoxicity against M. tuberculosis strains H37Rv and MS-115.
5-Dodecyloxymethyl-2'deoxyuridine derivatives with various substituents on the sugar moiety exhibit antimycobacterial activity depending on the length of the substituent at the C5 position.

5-vinyldeoxyuridine, when incorporated at the 5'-end of an oligodeoxyribonucleotide, enables non-enzymatic, template-directed, reversible photoligation with cytosine and thymine at the 3'-end upon photoirradiation at 366 nm.
S-dThd can be used as a labeled substrate to measure dThd kinase activity in L1210 cell extracts.
5-(1-Methoxy-2-chloroethyl)-2'-deoxyuridine is less active than the corresponding 5-(1-hydroxy-2,2-dihaloethyl) analog against HSV-1, HSV-2, VZV, and EBV.
5-(1-methoxy-2-chloroethyl)2'-deoxyuridine is less active than the corresponding bromo analogue against HSV-1 in Vero cells.

3′-amino-3′-deoxythymidine monophosphate (NH2TMP) is a nucleoside analogue that lacks the steric hindrance of an azido group at the 3′ position, which is relevant for its phosphorylation by human thymidylate kinase (TMPK).
Replacement of TMP with 3′-amino-3′-deoxythymidine monophosphate in human TMPK complexes induces full P-loop closure.
3'-isocyano-3'-deoxythymidine is considerably more toxic to MT-4 cells than 2',3'-dideoxyuridine derivatives.
3'-isocyano-3'-deoxythymidine has a much greater affinity (Ki) for MT-4 cell dThd kinase than 2',3'-dideoxyuridine derivatives.
FddIUrd is a 5-iodo-derivative of 3'-fluoro-2',3'-dideoxyuridine that inhibits HIV-1 replication in MT4 cells with an ED50 of about 0.2–0.4 µM.
Octathymidylate can be covalently linked to an acridine derivative and, upon UV irradiation, forms a local triple helix with viral DNA at A-T rich regions within the viral origin of replication.

Octathymidylate, when covalently linked to an intercalating agent and complementary to a crucial A-T-rich region in the SV40 origin of replication, inhibits replication of SV40 viral DNA in CV1 cells by binding to open regions or forming local triple helices.
(-)-(2R,4R)-1-(2-Hydroxymethyl-1,3-dioxolan-4-yl)thymine (DOT) inhibits wild-type and NRTI-resistant HIV-1 reverse transcriptase activity.

Derivatives of 2,2-diphenyl-succinic acid (compounds 8-19) are devoid of mutagenic activity against the wild-type Vibrio harveyi BB7 strain but demonstrate strong antimutagenic activity (51% to 71%) against this strain.

(R)-2-amino-2-carbamoyl-3-phenylpropanoic acid is formed by the enantioselective hydrolysis of α-benzylated α-aminomalonamide 5h catalyzed by Rhodococcus erythropolis AJ270, with high enantiomeric purity.

m-phenylenediacetic acid reacts with tetranuclear copper(II) complexes to yield both discrete tetracopper cationic complexes and cluster polymers, such as [Cu4{mu-(alpha-D-Glc-1P)}2(mu-m-C6H4(CH2COO)2)(bpy)4](NO3)2 and {[Cu4{mu-(alpha-D-Glc-1P)}2(mu-m-C6H4(CH2COO)2)(bpy)4](NO3)2}n.
Resistance to PALA in rodent cells can arise through at least two different stepwise mechanisms, and extra copies of the CAD gene can be acquired via multiple mechanisms.
Pentachlorophenylalanine (pHA) is the major product of L-tyrosine oxidation by hypochlorous acid (HOCl) and is found at sites of inflammation and atherosclerotic tissue in vivo.

Triphenylethanamine (TPE) is a series of CETP inhibitors with higher potency than DPPE analogs, and structure-activity relationship studies in the TPE series informed the design of more potent compounds.

2-benzyl-2-methyl-3-mercaptopropanoic acid is a thiol-containing compound designed as an inhibitor of carboxypeptidase A, with the expectation that its inhibitory potency would be higher than that of its carboxylate analogue.

2-benzyl-2-methyl-3-mercaptopropanoic acid is a thiol-containing compound designed as an inhibitor of carboxypeptidase A, with the expectation that its inhibitory potency would be higher than that of its carboxylate analogue.
[2-(diethylamino)ethyl 2,2-diphenylpentenoate] hydrochloride (SKF-525A) reduces both alpha-C-hydroxylation and pyridine N-oxidation of NNK in hamster lung explants.

A-methyl-3-carboxyphenylglycine is a nonspecific mGluR antagonist that impedes performance in the Morris water maze and spatial alternation tasks, but does not affect brightness discrimination in a Y-maze.

RS-(+/-) chlorophenoxyisobutyric acid (CPP) modulates the gating of the rat skeletal muscle chloride channel rCIC-1 by decreasing the time constants of both fast and slow deactivating components of whole cell currents at hyperpolarizing potentials.

In n-3 PUFA adequate rats, the loss half-life of [4,5-3H]DHA in phospholipids ranged from 23 days in phosphatidylcholine (PC) to 58 days in phosphatidylethanolamine (PS), as determined by slope analysis.
Glycolic and mandelic acid derivatives act as highly potent and selective inhibitors of factor Xa.
4-(phenylthio)butanoic acid (PTBA) is a small molecule histone deacetylase inhibitor (HDACi) that expands embryonic kidney progenitor cells (EKPCs) in zebrafish embryos.
4-(phenylthio)butanoic acid is a histone deacetylase inhibitor (HDACi) that expands the pool of renal progenitor cells in zebrafish embryos in a proliferation-dependent manner.
Both (2S,3R)- and (2R,3S)-hydroxycitric acid enantiomers produced by Bacillus megaterium G45C and Streptomyces sp. U121 are optically pure.

Methoxybenzoylpropionic acid is a small-molecular-weight, noncarbohydrate inhibitor of L-, P-, and E-selectin that markedly reduces cyclic flow variations and myocardial myeloperoxidase activity in a canine coronary Folts' model under high shear stress.
(2R)-dihomocitric acid is a key compound differentially expressed in SLCMV-infected and healthy cassava samples across multiple cultivars.
Nitromalic acid inhibits spontaneous and oxidant-induced lipid peroxidation (LPO) in rat brain homogenate, but its antioxidant activity is less pronounced than that of hypocard.
Sulphation of N-OH-2ABP is observed in human tissues throughout fetal, neonatal, and adult life, and the resulting sulphate esters are reactive compounds capable of inducing DNA lesions.
5-[(4-hydroxybenzyl)oxy]methyl-2-furaldehyde was isolated from the rhizome of Gastrodia elata Blume.
5-(4-hydroxybenzyloxymethyl)-furan-2-carbaldehyde is a newly isolated benzofuran compound from the rhizome of Gastrodia elata.
5-hydroxymethylfurfural calcium acetate (HMFCA) can be produced from HMF by P. putida ATCC 47054, with yields up to 98.7% and a maximum concentration of 11.09 g/L from G. amansii hydrolysates.
2-hydroxy-3-(4-hydroxyphenyl)propanal is a monomeric product obtained from lignin depolymerization using CuSO4–H2O2 catalyst and microwave irradiation.

p-hydroxyphenylbut-3-ene-2-one (benzalacetone, P1) is a polyketide-like intermediate formed by the condensation of malonyl CoA and p-coumaryl CoA, catalyzed by benzalacetone synthase (BAS) in the biosynthesis of raspberry ketone.

Ethyl 4-hydroxyphenylacetate is a substrate in Novozym 435-catalyzed transesterification with oleyl alcohol, with the reaction proceeding to maximum conversion in the presence of an additional 3-methoxy group.

1,4-bis[(6-hydroxymethyl-2-pyridyl)-2-oxapropyl]benzene forms a 1:1 complex with Cu(II) or Zn(II) that acts as the active catalytic species for the hydrolysis of p-nitrophenyl phosphate in CTAB micellar solution at 30°C.
l-tyrosine ethyl ester, when immobilized on CH-Sepharose 4B and combined with vanadate, enables affinity chromatography for the retention and purification of Helix pomatia arylsulfatase.
In endothelium-blocked porcine right coronary arteries, 4-nitro-benzylnitrate (1c) shows higher vasodilating activity than glyceroltrinitrate (GTN).
Ac-Phe-Tyr-NH2 (N-terminally acetylated dipeptide with a glycine residue) can function as a low-affinity substrate for PepT1.

Two-dimensional 1H/13C PISEMA spectra can be used to measure heteronuclear dipolar couplings associated with all carbon functional groups found in proteins, including those without directly bonded hydrogens.
Tyrosine carbon radical is a product formed by oxidation of hemoglobin (Hb) by pneumococci in the presence of H2O2, as identified in EPR experiments with Hb-Fe3+ and exogenous H2O2.
The presence of tyrosyl iodide in thyroid hormone analogs enhances their inhibition of 5'-iodothyronine deiodinase (5'-ITD) activity in rat liver homogenates.
Tyrosine chloroamine, as part of the myeloperoxidase system, produces higher amounts of pHA-ethanolamine in LDL than tyrosine bromoamine.
Inhibition of catecholamine synthesis by α-methyltyrosine depletes neuronal catecholamine stores and can robustly affect mood in vulnerable patients.
System A, which involves α-methyltyrosine, contributes only a small amount to 123 I-IMT transport into C6 glioma cells.
[18 F]fluoro-ethyl-tyrosine (FET) is an amino acid PET tracer that is not metabolized but is transported into brain tumor tissue.
m-HPAA, a monophenol derivative of rutin, has been reported to influence glucose-induced protein damage and the early to late stages of AGE formation.
(4-Hydroxybenzyl)thiocarbamic acid, a compound from traditional Indian medicine, was identified as a top inhibitor of fructose-bisphosphate aldolase in Candida albicans based on docking scores.

n-octyl-4-hydroxybenzylamine (LINS03003) is a synthetic derivative of gibbilimbol B that exhibits potent antiparasitic activity with IC50 values of 5.5 µM against amastigotes of Trypanosoma cruzi and 1.8 µM against amastigotes of Leishmania infantum.
Streptovaricin U possesses structural motifs derived from polypropionate biosynthetic pathways that are amenable to the methodology of using sulfur ylides to construct polypropionate chains.

Streptovaricin U is an ansa-like antibiotic that contains a C-3/C-13 fragment synthesized via stereoselective addition of sulfur ylides to aldehydes, enabling the construction of polypropionate frameworks.
Streptoval C is a streptovaricin whose structure is assigned by completion of assignments of H-7, H-11, and H-7 on oxidation.
Streptoval C inhibits terminal deoxynucleotide transferase (TdT) and HSV-DP by binding to the enzymes themselves, rather than to the template-primer, initiator, substrates, or divalent cations.
Streptoval C preferentially inhibits terminal deoxynucleotidyltransferase (TdT) of leukemic lymphoblasts over DNA polymerases a, b, and y in both normal and leukemic cells.
Asteltoxin F is a marine sponge-derived fungal compound that is an analogue of asteltoxin and exhibits antiviral activity against H3N2 influenza virus with an IC50 of 8.9 ± 0.3 μM.
asteltoxin F is a newly isolated compound from Aspergillus sp. SCSIO XWS02F40 with reported antiviral activity against H1N1 and H3N2 influenza viruses.
rac-7-hydroxy-5-methoxy-6-methylflavanone is a flavonoid isolated from Eugenia rigida DC. and its structure has been confirmed by single-crystal X-ray diffraction crystallography.
Sarcotin A is a furanosesterterpene isolated from a Sarcotragus sponge species and was obtained in high yield (0.07%, wet weight) from this source.
Sarcotin A is a furanosesterterpene isolated from the marine sponge Sarcotragus sp. (family Thorectidae, order Dictyoceratida), and its C-21 configuration was revised based on NMR and CD data.
Rifamycinoside A is a 6-O-α-L-rhamnosyl derivative of 11,12seco-28-desmethyl-28-hydroxyrifamycin W, with a planar structure established by NMR and biosynthetic analysis.
Pladienolide C is a natural product isolated from Streptomyces platensis that targets the SF3b subunit of the spliceosome.
nogalamycin N-oxide (NSC116555) demonstrates cytotoxic effects against non-small-cell lung cancer A549 cells with an IC50 of 0.19 ± 0.01 µM, which is more potent than gefitinib.
nogalamycin N-oxide (NSC116555) is a potent EGFR-tyrosine kinase inhibitor with an IC50 of 37.71 nM against EGFR-TK.

N-acetyl-N-demethylmayamycin is a polycyclic quinone compound isolated from strain 182SMLY and exhibits inhibitory activity against the proliferation of glioma cells and the growth of methicillin-resistant Staphylococcus aureus and Escherichia coli.
N-acetyl-N-demethylmayamycin is a polycyclic anthraquinone that suppresses proliferation of glioma cell lines with IC50 values ranging from 0.5 to 7.3 µM and induces apoptosis in these cells.
Aurofusarin negatively affects the accumulation of carotenoids and vitamin E in quail.
Aurofusarin supplementation in the diet decreases vitamin A concentration in the egg yolk of quail.
3,6,8-trihydroxy-3-[3,5-dimethyl-2-oxo-3(E)-heptenyl]-2,3-dihydronaphthalen-1(4H)-one is a newly identified antifungal metabolite with a unique carbon skeleton.

3,6,8-trihydroxy-3-[3,5-dimethyl-2-oxo-3(E)-heptenyl]-2,3-dihydronaphthalen-1(4H)-one exhibits in vitro antifungal activity against Candida albicans, Tricophyton rubrum, and Aspergillus niger with MICs of 40, 20, and 80 µg/ml, respectively.
In isolated guinea pig aorta, 2-trifluoromethylphenylhistamine acts as a partial agonist at histamine H1 receptors, with a relative activity of 94%.

2-trifluoromethylphenylhistamine is a halogenated histamine derivative that inhibits 3H-mepyramine binding in bovine vascular smooth muscle membranes, and its inhibition potency is shifted to the right and the curve becomes steeper in the presence of GTP (0.1 mM), indicating reduced efficacy.
BHOA is a histamine D-decanoyl homoserine lactone (HDC) inhibitor that ameliorates SD-induced vertigo by restoring functional performance and vestibular scores at 24 and 48 hours post-SD.
4-(2'-acetoxyethyl) imidazole undergoes hydrolysis catalyzed by imidazole.

Imidazole-4-acetic acid (I4AA) is a ligand that binds to GABAC receptors, and the response of the rho2A receptor to I4AA is altered when the serine residue at position 320 in the second transmembrane domain is replaced by proline, resulting in pharmacology similar to the rho1B receptor.
The oxidation of histidinal in the first halfreaction of HDH proceeds via an aldehyde hydrate intermediate.
Histidinal is involved in a slow irreversible hydride transfer in the second half-reaction of a two-step enzymatic process.
Hydration of histidinal is proposed as a bond-reordering event that can contribute to R-secondary isotope effects in mechanistic studies of histidinol-catalyzed reactions.
Histidinal is a synthetic substrate that is oxidized to histidine at about 3-fold faster than its overall catalytic oxidation in Brassica HDH at pH 7.2.

4-[3-(3-(trifluoromethyl)phenylthio)propyl]-1H-imidazole functions as an antagonist at histamine H(3) receptors in vitro, as demonstrated by its inhibition of [(3)H]histamine release in rat cerebral cortex synaptosomes.
Monobromohistidine is formed when human serum albumin is brominated with bromoperoxidase or myeloperoxidase.
Methyl histidine inhibits the chicken olfactory bulb carnosine synthetase enzyme, consistent with its role as a substrate.
Urinary excretion of N-methylhistidine in rats decreases markedly and immediately upon protein depletion for approximately 10 days, then plateaus.
Feeding rats a 20% casein diet leads to a progressive increase in urinary excretion of N-methylhistidine.
Alpha-fluoromethylhistidine depletes neural histamine and does not prevent caloric stimulation-induced inhibition of locus coeruleus neuronal activity in rats.
AIB uptake in bovine endothelial cells is a complex process that is stimulated by insulin and IGF-I, and its regulation involves mechanisms distinct from those in other cell types.
N-tert-Butoxycarbonyl-l-histidine methyl ester is used as a model compound for histidine to demonstrate the lack of reaction between squaric acid modified polyethers and histidine residues.

N-Boc-L-histidine methyl ester functions as an axial ligand in cobalt(III) complexes, and its use causes a downfield shift in the 59 Co NMR spectrum, reflecting changes in electron density at the cobalt nucleus.
