N-Boc-L-histidine methyl ester is used as an N-donor ligand in cobalt(II) and cobalt(III) Schiff base complexes, with both amino and carboxylic groups protected by Boc to prevent side reactions during peptide coupling.

Histidine acts as a nucleophile that can induce instantaneous decomposition of the Fe(II) cylinder complex 6a in CD3CN/D2O (97:3) at 40°C, as evidenced by disappearance of the MLCT band at 423 nm and formation of free ligand 3a in 1H-NMR and ESI-MS.
CP-122721 1c, a 2,5-disubstituted benzylamine derivative with a cyclopropoxy 5-trifluoromethoxy group, displays sub-nanomolar hNK1 receptor affinity and significant hERG channel binding.
1-{(1S)-2-[amino]-1-[3-(trifluoromethoxy)phenyl]ethyl}cyclohexanol analogues are designed to improve in vitro metabolic stability and brain penetration.

Carbonylcyanide trifluoromethyl phenylhydrazone (FCCP) stimulates the rate of controlled respiration and depresses A~H, but its effect is not equivalent to that of ADP, valinomycin + nigericin, or A23187 under steady flux conditions.
Trifluoromethoxyphenol inhibits volume-sensitive anion channels with an IC50 of approximately 0.56 mM for half inhibition of Cl current.
Nonhalogenated electron-donating substituents such as 3-methoxy on the phenyl urea reduce binding affinity for MCH-R1.

CP-122721 1b, a 2,5-disubstituted benzylamine derivative with a 2-methoxy or cyclopropoxy 5-trifluoromethoxy group, exhibits sub-nanomolar affinity for the hNK1 receptor but also shows significant binding to the hERG channel.

3-methoxyphenylthiosemicarbazide, as part of spiro compound 6c, confers high selectivity against HCT-116 colorectal carcinoma and Jurkat leukemia cells, with a selectivity index exceeding 10 compared to normal COS-7 and BJ-5ta cells.

3-methoxyphenylthiosemicarbazide, as a chain in spiro compound 6c, confers high selectivity against HCT-116 colorectal carcinoma and Jurkat leukemia cells, with a selectivity index exceeding 10 compared to normal COS-7 and BJ-5ta cells.

CP-122721 1b, a 2,5-disubstituted benzylamine derivative with a 2-methoxy or cyclopropoxy 5-trifluoromethoxy group, exhibits sub-nanomolar affinity for the hNK1 receptor but also shows significant binding to the hERG channel.
7-trifluoromethoxy-3-methylquinoxaline, when substituted at the 7-position of quinoxaline, leads to improved PDE10A IC50 but does not enhance brain penetration.
1-(2-(2-chlorophenyl)benzo[d]oxazol-5-yl)-3-(4-(trifluoromethoxy)phenyl)urea is a type II inhibitor of VEGFR-2 kinase with an IC50 of 0.25 µM.
1-(2-(2-chlorophenyl)benzo[d]oxazol-5-yl)-3-(4-(trifluoromethoxy)phenyl)urea exhibits potent anti-angiogenesis activity in the chick chorioallantoic membrane (CAM) assay.

1-(2-(2-chlorophenyl)benzo[d]oxazol-5-yl)-3-(4-(trifluoromethoxy)phenyl)urea shows cytotoxic activity against HUVEC, H1975, A549, and HeLa cell lines with IC50 values of 8.46, 1.40, 7.61, and 0.28 µM, respectively.

4-(Diphenylphosphono)-3-methyl-2-butenenitrile can be deprotonated with sodium hydride to form an anion that reacts with aldehydes to yield isomeric mixtures of products, with all-E-retinonitrile as the main product.

4-(diphenylphosphono)-3-methylbut-2-enenitrile participates in HWE coupling with β-ionylidene acetaldehyde to yield 11Z-retinal mixed with the all-E form, but with lower Z-isomer selectivity compared to ethyl diethylphosphonoacetate.
2-trifluoromethoxybenzenesulfinate yields phenyl sulfinate ester 20 with good levels of enantioselectivity in the described reaction.
N-(4′-trifluoromethoxybenzoyl)-2-aminoethyl phosphate (F6) is an analogue of indole-3-D-glycerol 3-phosphate (IGP) used in studies of tryptophan synthase substrate binding and catalysis.

N-(4'-trifluoromethoxybenzoyl)-2-aminoethyl phosphate (F6) binds to the α-site of tryptophan synthase and its CF3 groups provide 19F NMR signals that sample the channel microenvironment at the α-β subunit interface, allowing distinction between open and closed β-subunit conformations.

N-(4'-trifluoromethoxybenzoyl)-2-aminoethyl phosphate (F6) is a high-affinity alpha-site ligand for the tryptophan synthase bienzyme complex, binding to the alpha-site and increasing the affinity of the beta-site for trans-3-indole-3'-acrylate by 4-5-fold.

N-(4'-trifluoromethoxybenzoyl)-2-aminoethyl phosphate (F6) is a substrate analogue that acts as a sensitive 19F NMR probe for detecting changes in the beta-subunit conformation of tryptophan synthase.
The Gly-Gly-Pro-Nle sequence is arranged in subsites S1–S4 of the cathepsin K active site, facilitating the stepwise release of PGE1 from HPMA copolymer conjugates.
The tripeptide methionine-lysine-proline (MKP) is responsible for the suppressive effect of bovine casein-derived peptide (CH-3) in preventing cognitive decline in Alzheimer's disease mouse models.
Oral administration of methionine-lysine-proline (MKP) at 0.5 mg/kg/day attenuates Abeta1-42-induced cognitive impairment and increases cerebral blood flow in AD model mice.
Threonine-lysine-proline is an inhibitor of monocyte differentiation that attenuates elevated HERV RNA levels and reverse transcriptase activity in PMA-stimulated U937 monocytoid cell cultures.
Acute oral administration of 5 mg/kg 3-thienylalanine-ornithine-proline (TOP) in spontaneously hypertensive rats reduces tail systolic pressure.

3-thienylalanine-ornithine-proline treatment leads to a significant increase in oxidative stress marker activity in heart, lungs, liver, and kidney compared to untreated SHRs, as measured by SOD, GR, and catalase activities.
DPD is a fatty acid conjugate of DDAVP tested for anti-diuretic activity in Brattleboro rats with hypothalamic diabetes insipidus.
Tetra-n-butylphosphonium l-prolinate ([P4444][Pro]) is an amino acid ionic liquid widely investigated as a CO2-chemsorptive ionic liquid.
L-proline-malic acid (L-Pro-Maa) is a natural deep eutectic solvent that enhances the extraction and oral bioavailability of puerarin from Radix Pueraria.
The uptake of 125I-labelled Tyr-Pro, a tyrosine-containing dipeptide, is energized by an inwardly directed H+ gradient, is electrogenic, and is inhibited by peptides.
Pro-Tyr is a good inhibitor of Gly-Sar uptake by the intestinal peptide transporter PEPT1.
Substitution of the C2 position in BomPBAN with l-2-aminooctanoic acid leads to a peptide with reduced PBAN-like activity in activating the PBAN receptor and stimulating Ca2+ influx in insect cells.

l-2-aminooctanoic acid can be used as a residue substitute for the Arg residue at the C2 position in PBAN analogs to assess their in vitro PBAN-like activity in a Ca2+ imaging assay using BomPBANR–EGFP-expressing Sf9 cells.

The anionic diazeniumdiolate of l-proline (PROLI/N2O2) can be formed by reacting l-proline with nitric oxide in the presence of a suitable base and, when embedded into polyurethane, releases nitric oxide at a steady rate over 7 weeks in PBS buffer at 37°C.
After 2–5 hours in Manduca larvae, Tyr-Leu-Pro-Thr accounts for less than 0.5% of the tritiated metabolites derived from ingested proctolin.
Hyp-Gly is extremely poorly hydrolyzed in the cytosol of rat intestinal epithelial cells.
Hyp-Gly, a peptide derived from collagen, is absorbed into the body mainly in its intact form.

Glycine-hydroxyproline dipeptide in prolidase-deficient urine is present at much lower concentrations than glycine-proline, and its resonances are not detectable in either 1H NMR or COSY urine spectra.
DiAzKs (Diazirine-Lysine) is a UV-activatable amino acid that can be site-specifically incorporated into proteins via genetic code expansion, enabling site-specific cross-linking.
Diazirine lysine is a photoactivatable lysine analog that can be incorporated into proteins using the pyrrolysyl tRNA machinery, allowing for site-specific photo-crosslinking in E. coli cells.

1,5-bis(4-hydroxy-2,3,5-trimethylphenoxy)-pentane exhibits antioxidative and 5-lipoxygenase (5-LD) inhibiting activities and is expected to be effective against tissue injury, cancer, and inflammation.
Trimethylhydroquinone can react with compound 3 under acidic conditions to form a 2,2-disubstituted chromene precursor of troglitazone.

Trimethylhydroquinone derivatives have been synthesized and evaluated for anti-lipid peroxidation activity, inhibition of rat basophilic leukemia-1 (RBL-1) cell 5-lipoxygenase, and reduction of 48 h homologous passive cutaneous anaphylaxis (PCA) activity in rats.
2,3,6-trimethoxy-5-methylphenol potently inhibits LPS-induced nitric oxide production in a dose-dependent manner, with an IC50 value of 1.8 ± 0.2 µg/mL.

2-n-amyloxybenzamide exhibits fungistatic activity and is more active than N-n-butyl-3-phenylsalicylamide, undecylenic acid, salicylanilide, Nystatin, phenylmercuric acetate, sodium ethylmercurithiosalicylate, and 8-hydroxyquinoline.
n-amyloxybenzamide exhibits maximal antifungal activity among tested salicylamide derivatives, with greater activity observed after 14 days of incubation.
Vitamin K5 (VK2) induces apoptosis in MDS-KZ cells by down-regulating BCL-2 and upregulating BAX, leading to caspase-3 activation via mitochondrial dysfunction.
1,2-Bis-[2-bis(carboxymethyl)amino-5-fluoro-4-methylphenoxy]ethane (MFBAPTA) is a fluorine-labelled Ca#+ chelator used in F-NMR spectroscopy to measure cardiac [Ca#+ ]i.
1,2-Bis-[2-bis(carboxymethyl)amino-5-fluoro-4-methylphenoxy]ethane is a (19)F-NMR Ca(2+) indicator with a dissociation constant for Ca(2+) between 46 and 537 nM.
MFBAPTA is a high-affinity Ca#+ NMR indicator with a Kd of 270 nM, used to measure intracellular Ca#+ concentration in intact hearts.
Decylparaben shows activity towards rodent pregnane X receptor (rPXR) but not human pregnane X receptor (hPXR).
CNBCA is a potent and selective inhibitor of SHP2, with an IC50 of 34.6 µM in the PTPase assay using a GST fusion PTP domain.

CNBCA is an SHP2 inhibitor that suppresses cell proliferation, anchorage independent growth, and cancer stem cell properties (mammosphere formation) in breast cancer cells (BT474 and MDA-MB468) at a concentration of 500 nM.

3-ethoxy-4-methoxyphenol is a purified metabolite from Xenorhabdus hominickii that acts as a phospholipase A2 (PLA2) inhibitor, significantly inhibiting PLA2 activity in Spodoptera exigua hemocytes in a dose-dependent manner.

Tetramethylphenol content in Ephedra sinica Stapf can be quantitatively determined by measuring its peak area in electron-impact and SIM mode, with a linear relationship between concentration (0.3 to 13.2 µg/mL) and peak area in the range of 0.3 to 13.2 µg/mL.

4-(tridecyloxy)benzaldehyde oxime acts as a co-antioxidant that significantly increases ascorbyl palmitate stability in oil-dispersed-in-water (o/w) microemulsions by reducing ascorbyl palmitate radical back to ascorbyl palmitate, due to its free-radical scavenging activity.

4-(tridecyloxy)benzaldehyde oxime is non-cytotoxic to keratinocyte cells at concentrations used to enhance ascorbyl palmitate stability in microemulsions, although cytotoxicity increases with higher microemulsion concentrations.

2-O-octyl-L-ascorbic acid is a monosubstituted ascorbic acid derivative that shows significant antigen-stimulated degranulation inhibitory activity in RBL-2H3 cells, with its activity depending on alkyl chain length.
3,5,3',5'-tetramethyl-DL-thyronine is the first analog of thyroxine with non-halogen substituents on the alanine-bearing ring to show significant thyroxine-like activity.
3,5,3',5'-tetramethyl-DL-thyronine (Me4T) shows 92% of the activity of L-thyroxine (T4) in maintaining kidney slice metabolism.
N(2)-benzylguanosine-modified RNA reduces editing activity of ADAR2, indicating that the dsRBM I binding site at the GluR-B Q/R site is important for editing.

Bz-FapydG is a bulky Fapy-DNA adduct that can form unproductive base excision repair (BER) enzyme-DNA complexes in vivo, potentially interfering with nucleotide excision repair (NER) and contributing to the persistence of DNA damage and increased cancer risk.
7-hydroxy-6-hydroxymethyl-5,6,7,8-tetrahydropyrimido[1,2-a]purin-10(1H)one is a hydrolysis product of N-(2-hydroxy-1-oxiranylethyl)-2'-deoxyguanosine formed by acid hydrolysis of DEB-DNA adducts.

2-(phosphonoethoxy)ethyl hypoxanthine is a 6-oxopurine acyclic nucleoside phosphonate that inhibits hypoxanthine-guanine-xanthine phosphoribosyltransferase (HGXPRT), a key enzyme in the purine salvage pathway of human malarial parasites.

2-(phosphonoethoxy)ethyl hypoxanthine is a good inhibitor of PfHGXPRT with a Ki value of 0.1 ± 0.02 µM and a selectivity ratio Ki(Hu)/Ki(Pf) of 10, indicating higher selectivity for the parasite enzyme.
5-glucosylhydroxymethylcytosine blocks the recognition and cleavage activity of MspJI toward 5-hydroxymethylcytosine.
Guanosinecytosine (GC)-rich DNA-binding drugs, such as chromomycin, can influence the pattern of erythroid differentiation and ␥-globin gene expression.
2-C-glucopyranosyl 1H-imidazo[4,5-b]pyridine acts as a potent inhibitor of rabbit muscle glycogen phosphorylase b with a maximal inhibitor concentration (IC50) of 625 µM.

Among the tested compounds, 2-C-glucopyranosyl 1H-imidazo[4,5-b]pyridine exhibits the lowest IC50 value for inhibition of rabbit muscle glycogen phosphorylase b, indicating the highest inhibitory potency.

2'-deoxy-2'-methylene guanosine (MdGuo) inhibits the growth of mouse and human tumor cell lines in culture and is phosphorylated by kinases distinct from those activating 2'-deoxy-2'-methylene adenosine.

N-glucosylthiophene-2-carboxamides are glycoconjugates that can be acetylated and their per-O-acetylated derivatives show significant inhibition of [3H]thymidine incorporation in bovine aortic endothelial cells, indicating reduced cell proliferation.

Uridine monophosphorus in the form of a conjugate is preferentially taken up by hepatocytes due to its asialoglycoprotein component, whereas free uridine monophosphorus is dispersed throughout the body and may not provide sufficient protection against galactosamine toxicity.
Uridine monophosphorus, when administered as a conjugate targeted to hepatocytes, protects hepatocytes from galactosamine toxicity in vivo.
[5'-3H]guanosine can be synthesized by enzymatically removing the ribose moiety from [2,8,5'-3H]adenosine using purine nucleoside phosphorylase and coupling it to unlabeled guanine.
In Giardia lamblia, [5'-3H]guanosine is incorporated into guanine nucleotides but not into adenine nucleotides, and the ribose moiety is not incorporated with the guanine base.
1-beta-D-arabinofuranosyl-E-5-(2-chlorovinyl)uracil (CV-ara-U) is selectively inhibitory to HSV-1 multiplication.

1-beta-D-arabinofuranosyl-E-5-(2-chlorovinyl)uracil (CV-ara-U) is more than 10 times as active as 1-beta-D-arabinofuranosylthymine and 5-iodo-deoxyuridine against HSV-1 and much less active against HSV-2.
1-beta-D-arabinofuranosyl-E-5-(2-chlorovinyl)uracil (CV-ara-U) inhibits HSV-1-induced cytopathogenic effect and plaque formation in HEL-F cells at concentrations as low as 0.1 µg/ml.
1-beta-D-arabinofuranosyl-E-5-chlorovinyluracil exhibits antiviral activity against salmon herpesvirus (OMV) in vitro with an IC50 of 0.003 µg/ml in Yamame (Oncorhynchus masou) kidney cells.
2-deoxyxanthosine is a deaminated product of 2'-deoxyguanosine formed by reaction with nitrous acid or nitric oxide at acidic or neutral pH.
Mono(4-butyl-2-methyl-5-xylenyl) phthalate increases FeNO by 8.7% (95% CI 1.9–16.0%) in children with reported wheeze.
Mono(4-butyl-2-methyl-5-xylenyl) phthalate is associated with increased airway inflammation as measured by fractional excretion of nitric oxide (FeNO) in children aged 4–9 years.
Monoheptyl phthalate can activate PPARs in vitro but does not act as a PP because its aliphatic side chain is rapidly cleaved in vivo.
Mono-n-heptyl phthalate is a urinary phthalate metabolite quantified in human urine by a method including enzymatic deglucuronidation and tandem mass spectrometry.
Mono-n-heptyl phthalate is a urinary metabolite of phthalates that can be measured in human urine using isotope dilution-HPLC-MS/MS.
Monoheptyl phthalate is a urinary metabolite of phthalate exposure quantified by enzymatic deconjugation and HPLC-MS/MS.
mono(4-butyl-5-hydroxyphenyl) phthalate does not exhibit estrogenic activity in yeast-based estrogen receptor transcription assays.
Mono(4-nonylphenyl) phthalate is a high molecular weight phthalate metabolite associated with obesity in adults according to multivariable regression analyses.
Mono(4-octylphenyl) phthalate is a high molecular weight phthalate metabolite that has been linked to obesity in adults using multivariable regression models.
monoethylhexyl phthalate, the main metabolite of DEHP, is recognized as a hepatic peroxisome proliferator and may be hepatocarcinogenic in rodents.
Acute exposure to mono-2-(ethylhexyl) phthalate in 28-day-old rats initially inhibits physiologic germ cell apoptosis for 3 hours, followed by a significant increase in apoptosis.
The primary cellular target for mono-2-(ethylhexyl) phthalate-induced testicular injury is the Sertoli cell.
Mono(n-butyl-5-oxohexyl) phthalate, also referred to as DBP, has been associated with decreased human sperm motility and reduced semen quality when present at environmental concentrations.
Acute exposure to mono-2-(ethylhexyl) phthalate in 28-day-old rats initially inhibits physiologic germ cell apoptosis for 3 hours, followed by a significant increase in apoptosis.
The primary cellular target for mono-2-(ethylhexyl) phthalate-induced testicular injury is the Sertoli cell.
Higher pregnancy-averaged mono-2(ethyl-5-oxohexyl) phthalate concentrations are associated with higher child BMI at 4 years (0.20 SDS change per natural log increase in exposure).
Mono-(5-oxooctyl) phthalate is a urinary metabolite detected in rats dosed with DnOP, with a concentration of 3.78 nmol/mL in rat liver microsome incubations of D4-DnOP.

The urinary level of Mono(2-butyl-5-hydroxy-5-methylhexyl) phthalate in 8–15 year old children is positively associated with obesity indicators such as Body Mass Index (BMI) and Waist Circumference (WC).
The geometric mean (GM) of urinary Mono(2-butyl-5-hydroxy-5-methylhexyl) phthalate levels before the DEHP food contamination event was 6.88 µg/g of creatinine.
Mono(2-butyl-5-oxo-1,4-pentadienyl) phthalate is a potent inhibitor of fetal testosterone production in rats in vivo.
3-methyl-aminothiophene dicarboxylic acid (3-MATIDA) is a potent and selective mGlu1 receptor antagonist with an IC50 of 6.3 µM and no activity on mGlu5 receptors.

3-methyl-aminothiophene dicarboxylic acid (3-MATIDA) is a selective and potent mGlu 1 receptor antagonist that exhibits neuroprotective effects in cultured neocortical cells and hippocampal slices subjected to oxygen-glucose deprivation (OGD) in vitro.

3-methyl-aminothiophene dicarboxylic acid, a metabotropic glutamate 1 (mGlu 1) receptor antagonist, reduces neuronal cell death induced by oxygen and glucose deprivation in hippocampal slices in culture.

3-methyl-aminothiophene dicarboxylic acid is a potent, possibly selective, and systemically active antagonist of mGlu1 receptors with neuroprotective activity in models of post-ischemic neuronal death.
3-MATIDA is a mGlu1 antagonist with greater selectivity for mGlu1 over mGlu5 receptors and has been shown to be neuroprotective in vitro, though it is a weak compound with low μM affinity.
α-bromo-L-alanine reacts with the pyridoxal form of aspartate aminotransferase isoenzymes, forming pyruvate via a η/3 elimination reaction and inactivates the enzyme.

2-methylbenzimidazoyl cinnamaldehyde analogs exhibit antimicrobial activity against methicillin-sensitive Staphylococcus aureus and E. coli, and also show activity against Gram-negative pathogens including Acinetobacter baumannii.
Keto-amino-guanosine (T1M G) is a nucleoside analogue that forms a potent complex with molnupiravir, as shown by computational studies.

The optimized complex of amino-molnupiravir with keto-amino-guanosine in water exhibits an Eint/Ebind value of -16.06/-13.63 kcal/mol when PCM is included, indicating strong binding in the presence of water.
The keto-amino-guanosine complex of molnupiravir is energetically favored, as indicated by lower relative energy and the presence of three hydrogen bonds.

The interaction of amino-molnupiravir with keto-amino-guanosine results in changes to the EHOMO, ELUMO, EFL, and Egap electronic properties, with numerically observed values of -7.260/-0.670/-3.965/6.589 eV for the amino-molnupiravir–keto-amino-guanosine (T1M G) complex.
Ampicillin can be quantitatively measured in human serum using a solid-phase extraction assay with high recovery (98.4%) and HPLC, employing Empore Disk Cartridges SDB-XC for extraction.
6-tritylaminopenicillanic acid does not undergo epimerization at the 6-position under the described reaction conditions.
Tetramethylcyclopropyl carboxamide is a cyclopropyl analogue of valproic acid (VPD) that does not undergo hydrolytic biotransformation to its corresponding inactive acid in rats or dogs.

Tetramethylcyclopropyl carboxamide (TMCD) is a broad-spectrum anticonvulsant agent that protects against Metrazol-induced seizures in mice (ED50 = 57 mg/kg) and rats (ED50 = 52 mg/kg), but does not induce neural tube defects in SWV mice.
N-(2-Acetyl-3-aminophenyl)acetamide can be converted to mutagenic species by HUC lysates and subcellular fractions.
(1S,2S,3S,5R,6S)-2-amino-3-fluorobicyclo[3.1.0]hexane-2,6-dicarboxylic acid, also known as MGS0008, is a rigid glutamate analog with potent agonist activity for mGlu2/3 receptors.

(1S,2S,3S,5R,6S)-2-amino-3-fluorobicyclo[3.1.0]hexane-2,6-dicarboxylic acid (MGS0008) is an mGlu2/3 receptor agonist that attenuates locomotor hyperactivity induced by NMDA receptor antagonists in rodents.

(+)-(1S,2S,3S,5R, 6S)-2-amino-3-fluorobicyclo[3.1.0]hexane-2,6-dicarboxylic acid (compound +7) is a fluorinated derivative of a group II mGluR agonist that shows higher oral activity in laboratory animals compared to compound 4.

(1S,2S,3S,5R,6S)-2-amino-3-fluorobicyclo[3.1.0]hexane-2,6-dicarboxylic acid (MGS0008) demonstrates antipsychotic potential in rat in vivo models by significantly decreasing phencyclidine-induced locomotor hyperactivity and conditioned avoidance responses.

DCFNa can be electrochemically degraded using a combination of electro-oxidation and electrocoagulation on stainless steel (SS) anode, resulting in reduced COD (chemical oxygen demand) and efficient removal of the parent drug and its degradation intermediates.

2,4-Dichlorophenol sodium salt (DCFNa) can form inclusion complexes with δCD, and its adsorption behavior on colloidal silver surfaces has been studied using vibrational and surface-enhanced Raman spectroscopy.
3-chloro-2′,5′dihydroxybiphenyl displays moderate 8-oxodG-forming activity among the para dihydroxy metabolites in a nonenzymatic Cu(II) system.
Sodium dichlorocresolate is a potential alternative disinfectant to sodium hypochlorite for household-based water treatment interventions.
Hydroxy 2,4,6-trichloro biphenyl is the most effective competitor for hPR binding in yeast, as measured by [3H]progesterone competition binding assays.
Cl2pyNO serves as the oxidant for the epoxidation of styrene to styrene oxide when catalyzed by dendritic ruthenium porphyrins 5-[G-n] m.

(R)-1-(3-chlorophenyl) ethanol can be synthesized from 3'-chloroacetophenone with enantiomeric excess greater than 99% using a light-driven, engineered Rhodobacter sphaeroides/Leifsonia sp. alcohol dehydrogenase system in n-hexane/aqueous biphasic media.
A2E is a modest singlet-oxygen quencher, as indicated by its effect on singlet-oxygen emission lifetime in the presence of 80% cyclohexane-d12 and 20% acetone-d6.
Exposure to 1,2,3,4-tetrachloro-7-naphthol (HCBD) is associated with highly significant increases in individual and summed bile acid measures, with a dose-effect relationship observed.
2,3,4,5-tetrachloro-m-terphenyl is a weak inducer of EROD activity in immature male Wistar rats, with a competitive receptor binding EC50 greater than 10⁻⁵ M.

The mean AUC of 3'-hydroxydiclofenac after a single 150 mg oral dose of diclofenac is significantly different between healthy subjects and patients with alcoholic cirrhosis, but not between healthy subjects and chronic active hepatitis patients.
3'-hydroxydiclofenac is exclusively formed from diclofenac by human CYP2C9.

The cellular resistance to 3-chlorodiphenylamine-induced oxidative damage in active sludge is increased approximately threefold by the addition of di-O-12-methyl-tetradecanoyl-3-O-beta-D-galactopyranosyl-sn-glycerol glycoglycerolipid at 100 mg/l.
6,8-dichloro-4-quinolinol protects methyl linoleate against AAPH-induced oxidation.
6,8-dichloro-4-quinolinol can play an antioxidant role in AAPH-induced oxidation of DNA.

7,8-Dimethyl-1-hydroxyphenazine 5,10-dioxide exhibits higher cytotoxic potency against AML cells (EC50 = 2.8 µM, MOLM-13, normoxia) compared to 1-hydroxyphenazine 5,10-dioxide (EC50 = 11 µM, MOLM-13 cells, normoxia).

2-chloroiodosylbenzene is a meta-substituted iodosylbenzene derivative that acts as a more efficient catalyst for liver microsomal androstenedione hydroxylation than its meta- or para-substituted analogues.
3,3',4,4'-tetrachlorobiphenyl (3,3'4,4'-TeCB) levels in sebum are higher than in blood in Yusho and Yu-Cheng patients, likely due to higher atmospheric contamination.

TCB undergoes oxidation in aqueous solution by Fe(III)/H2O2, with its decomposition rate influenced by the presence of hydroxycyclohexadienyl radicals and the catalytic effect of TCB on the oxidation of atrazine.
7-chloro-N-4-hydroxybutylisatin is an N-substituted 7-chloro-isatin derivative whose inhibition potency toward caspases-3 and -7 is significantly improved to the nanomolar level after N-substitution.
The phosphate group of Spo0A is labile to both acidic and basic conditions.

γ-L-glutamyl hydrazine is involved in the biosynthesis of fosfazinomycin and kinamycin, with one nitrogen atom originating from nitrous acid and the hydrazine moiety being acetylated and transferred through a glutamyl carrier into the biosynthetic pathways of these antibiotics.
L-glutamic acid-gamma-hydrazide induces GABA accumulation in the substantia nigra of rats, and this effect is reduced on the side opposite to the posterior hypothalamic hemisection.
L-glutamic acid-gamma-hydrazide, when injected into mice, decreases glutamate decarboxylase (GAD) activity and increases GABA levels in the brain.

Leucine methylphosphonic acid, when combined with a hydroxamic acid group, forms a compound with significant inhibitory activity against Helicoverpa armigera larval growth and digestive aminopeptidase activity.
Ornithine is transported into rat liver mitochondrial matrix via a proton-compensated electroneutral transporter that exchanges ornithine for a proton.
Glufosinate can be separated and determined using micellar electrokinetic chromatography with laser-induced fluorescence detection after derivatization with fluorescein isothiocyanate isomer I.

Under the described protocol, [3-13C]serine contributes approximately 100% of the methyl groups used for total body homocysteine remethylation, while accounting for only about 2.8% of total serine flux.
[3-13C]serine is produced in Saccharomyces cerevisiae from [13C]formate primarily via the cytosolic C1-tetrahydrofolate synthase pathway.
[3-13C]serine is detected in ethanolic extracts of Nicotiana plumbaginifolia cells grown with [13C-methyl]nicotine, indicating incorporation of label from nicotine metabolism.

2-Phosphonophenylalanine (2-PMPA) is used in combination with NAAG to study their effects on glial cell membrane potential, with the ED50 for NAAG+2-PMPA being comparable to that for L-NAAG and glutamate.
At a dose of 150 mg/kg, 2-PMPA increases the electroconvulsive threshold in mice by 28.8% (from 7.3 to 9.4 mA).
At low doses of 50 and 100 mg/kg, 2-PMPA elevates the electroconvulsive threshold in mice by 6.8% and 19.2%, respectively, though these increases are not statistically significant.
At a dose of 200 mg/kg, 2-PMPA increases the electroconvulsive threshold in mice by 38.4% (from 7.3 to 10.1 mA).

2,9-diamino-5,6-dicarboxy-4,7-dithiadecanedioic acid is a chemically stable and selective antidote for Hg(II) poisoning with inherently low toxicity, forming reasonably stable complexes with several divalent metal ions.
2,9-diamino-5,6-dicarboxy-4,7-dithiadecanedioic acid, as a chelator with vicinal thioether groups, is water-soluble, has low toxicity, and can chelate toxic metals.

2,9-diamino-5,6-dicarboxy-4,7-dithiadecanedioic acid is a chelating agent containing vicinal thioether groups that enhances excretion of lead, reverses certain lead-induced biochemical alterations, and mobilizes tissue lead in poisoned rats.
SIRT5 catalyzes the demalonylase activity of N ε -malonyl-lysine.
N6-malonyllysine is selectively hydrolyzed by the sirtuin enzyme SIRT5.
SIRT5 exhibits strong N ε -malonyl-lysine demalonylase activity.
Hexamethonium hydrochloride (15 mg/kg, ip) administered 15 minutes before choline (120 mg/kg, ip) in rats does not alter the choline-induced increase in serum glucose.
Isoborneol specifically inhibits the glycosylation of HSV-1 gB polypeptide in infected cells but does not affect the glycosylation of cellular polypeptides.
Isoborneol specifically inhibits HSV-1 glycosylation, and this inhibition is independent of the viral thymidine kinase gene.

The inhibitory effect of isoborneol on HSV-1 viral glycosylation is independent of the presence of a functional thymidine kinase gene, as shown by its effect on a thymidine kinase-deficient HSV-1 mutant.

Choline thiocholine, generated from the reaction of acetylcholinesterase (AChE) and acetylthiocholine (ATCh), has a special affinity for Cu 2+ and can extract it from Cu 2+ -g-C 3 N 4 nanosheets, leading to fluorescence recovery.

3 beta,6 beta,17 beta-trihydroxy-5 alpha-androstane, administered as a single 2 mg/rat subcutaneous dose in long-term castrated male rats, does not significantly affect serum FSH, prolactin, or LH levels at 2, 5, 8, or 24 hours.

5α-dihydrotestosterone is metabolized in canine normal and hyperplastic prostate explants to C17α-0α-steroids, with lower accumulation of 8.5 nM [3H]-5α-dihydrotestosterone in hyperplastic tissue compared to normal prostate.
5a-androstane-3a,7a,17-triol is present in the faeces of C57BL/6 germfree rats.
CYP102A1 chimeras C(78-82,F87A,327-332) and C(78-82,F87A,328-330) produce 6,7-epoxyfarnesol and 10,11-epoxyfarnesol in a 2:3 ratio as primary products.

(+)-bornyl p-coumarate is an antibacterial agent isolated from the crude chloroform bark extract of Piper caninum and exhibits activity against Gram-positive bacteria such as Bacillus cereus, Staphylococcus aureus, and Streptococcus pneumoniae.
MDL 73404 is an a-tocopherol analogue that reduces infarct size in a rat model of myocardial ischaemia and reperfusion, with maximum reduction at a dose of 0.3 mg/kg per h.

MDL 73404 is a free radical scavenger that, when administered intravenously at 3 mg/kg per hour, reduces infarct size by 55% in a rat myocardial infarction model when started 30 minutes after artery occlusion.
(1-iodophenyl)choline analogues have been synthesized and evaluated as radioiodinated myocardial perfusion imaging agents.
PGF1beta, when infused into the lateral ventricle of adult male rat brain, produces a considerably less stimulation of LH release compared to PGE2, PGF2alpha, and PGF2beta.
Cholesta-8,14-dienol undergoes an oxygen-requiring rearrangement of the A–β bond from the A8 to A' positions, catalyzed by a 15-hydroxylase enzyme in rat liver.
Conicasterol E is a 7α,15β-dihydroxyconicasterol analogue and represents the first example of an SHP-sparing FXR modulator with PXR agonistic activity isolated from Theonella swinhoei.

Conicasterol E is a modulator of the farnesoid X receptor (FXR) whose potency on selective target genes such as OSTα and BSEP is very close to that of the endogenous mammalian ligand CDCA and lower than that of the synthetic agonist 6-ECDCA.

Rosane is a diterpenoid class present in Euphorbia ebracteolata roots, with some rosane-type diterpenoids exhibiting moderate anti-Mycobacterium tuberculosis activity (MIC values as low as 18 µg/mL) and moderate inhibition of the target enzyme GlmU (IC50 = 12.5 µg/mL).
Stenbolone is an anabolic steroid that can be detected in horse urine using HPLC-MS-MS following enzyme-treated sample processing and solid-phase extraction.

Stenbolone is metabolized in humans to 18-hydroxylated steroids, indicating the presence of hepatic 18-hydroxylase activity for 3,17-diketo and/or 17-hydroxy-3-keto C,9 steroids bearing a methyl group at the C1 or C2 position.
Pyridazino [3,4-b] [1,5]benzoxazepin-5(6H)-one analogs act as inhibitors of HIV-1 reverse transcriptase, with activity influenced by substituents at the 3- and 8-positions.

10-(3'-chloropropyl)phenoxazine is a relatively nontoxic chemosensitizer that, at nontoxic concentrations, potentiates the cytotoxicity of vincristine and vinblastine in multidrug-resistant GC3/Cl and KBChR-8-5 human cancer cell lines.
Phenothiazine-morpholine derivatives have been synthesized and evaluated for their effects on mechanisms involved in atherosclerosis, such as hyperlipidemia, oxidative stress, and inflammation.
Substituted 1,4-dihydroquinoxaline-2,3-diones (QXs) function as NMDA/glycine site antagonists.
1,4-dihydroquinoxaline-2,3-dione derivatives function as supramolecular hosts for water clusters, accommodating zero-, one-, and two-dimensional arrays of water in their hydrated structures.
1,4-dihydroquinoxaline-2,3-diones can function as antagonists at the glycine site of the NMDA receptor.
Benzimidazolinone derivatives, featuring a fused aryl ring with a fixed conformation relative to the cyclic urea, have been investigated for their activity and selectivity at neurokinin receptors.
Benzopyranopyrrolidine compounds have been reported as dopamine D 3 receptor antagonists.
Benzopyranopyrrolidine, specifically S33138, is a novel compound that acts as a preferential antagonist of cloned human D(3) dopamine receptors over D(2L) and D(2S) receptors.
6-phenyl-dibenzo(d,f)(1,3)dioxepines undergo isomerization to a cyclohexadienone spiro benzo[b]furan under electron ionization (EI) mass spectrometry.

6-phenyl-dibenzo(d,f)(1,3)dioxepine exists as three isomeric sets: o-, m-, and p-substituted isomers, which are difficult to distinguish by EI mass spectrometry but can be differentiated by CID fragmentation patterns.

3-fluorochromanone derivatives with electron-withdrawing substituents such as fluoro, bromo, and nitro groups on the aryl ring are efficiently reduced by asymmetric transfer hydrogenation to give cis-fluorohydrins with high diastereoselectivity and enantioselectivity.
3-fluorochromanone serves as a substrate for ruthenium-catalyzed asymmetric transfer hydrogenation to yield enantioenriched cis-3-fluoro-chroman-4-ol derivatives.

The inhibitory activity of 4-methylpyrimido [4,5-b] [1, 4] benzothiazines against soybean lipoxygenase (SLO) depends on the orientation of the sulfur atom of the thiazine core towards the Fe 3+ -OH in the enzyme's active site, with subsequent oxidation of sulfur to sulfoxide.

Disubstituted 2-phenyl-benzopyranopyrimidine derivatives are essential for strong binding and stabilization of telomeric G-quadruplex DNA, whereas mono-substituted derivatives have much weaker effects.

2-phenyl-benzopyranopyrimidine derivatives with alkylamino side chains act as highly selective ligands for binding to telomeric G-quadruplex DNA, effectively stabilizing the G-quadruplex structure and showing significant selectivity over duplex DNA.

2-phenyl-benzopyranopyrimidine derivatives have been studied for their ability to inhibit telomerase and their cellular effects, with compound 11b identified as a promising telomerase inhibitor and telomeric G-quadruplex binding ligand for potential cancer treatment.
1,2-Dihydrochromeno[3,4-f]quinoline derivatives can exhibit potent progesterone receptor (PR) agonist activity when a benzylidene moiety is incorporated at the 5-position of the core ring system.
5-aryl 22 derivatives of 1,2-Dihydrochromeno[3,4-f]quinoline act as positive allosteric modulators (PR agonists) with high potency and strong binding affinity for the progesterone receptor.
1,2-dihydrochromeno[3,4-f]quinoline derivatives have been synthesized and evaluated as nonsteroidal progesterone receptor modulators with antiprogestant activity.

Hydroxybenzoic and cinnamic acid derivatives are among the chemical classes of polyphenolic compounds characterized and quantified in seven commercial berry juices using a two-dimensional liquid chromatography system.
Hydroxybenzoic and cinnamic acid derivatives are identified as phenolic components in almond hull extracts.
Resistant genotypes of groundnut have higher constitutive levels of cinnamic acid and salicylic acid compared to susceptible genotypes.
Cinnamoyloxybenzoic acid at low concentrations stimulates CAT expression and is implicated in the regulation of the CHSlS promoter via a cis-acting element.

Styryl hydroxy-benzoic acid analogues have been synthesized and tested in vitro for their ability to decrease intracellular glutathione levels in cancer cell lines including A172 (glioma), A375 (melanoma), U87 (glioma), and MCF7 (breast carcinoma).
Titanocene salicylic acid complex binds to hIAPP19-37 via hydrophobic interaction and inhibits its aggregation.
(E)-2-oxo-4-phenyl-3-butenoic acid acts as an inhibitor of pyruvate decarboxylase, and its complexes with the enzyme produce an enamine intermediate detectable by a 440 nm absorbance maximum.

Phenyl-substituted E-4-phenyl-2-keto-3-butenoic acid derivatives can act as time-dependent irreversible inhibitors of brewer's yeast pyruvate decarboxylase (EC 4.1.1.1), with electron-withdrawing substituents conferring this property.

(E)-2-oxo-4-phenyl-3-butenoic acid type inhibitors/alternate substrates enable detection of an enzyme-bound enamine intermediate at 440 nm in both pdc1 and pdc1-6 fusion enzymes of brewers' yeast pyruvate decarboxylase.
trans-cinnamate is produced from L-phenylalanine by the enzyme phenylalanine ammonia-lyase (PAL) and serves as a precursor for various phenylpropanoids such as lignins, £avonoids, and coumarins.

(E)-2-oxo-4-phenyl-3-butenoic acid type inhibitors/alternate substrates enable detection of an enzyme-bound enamine intermediate at 440 nm in both pdc1 and pdc1-6 fusion enzymes of brewers' yeast pyruvate decarboxylase.

1-[(E)-2-carboxyvinyl]-2-naphthoic acid is an ortho-cleavage product of phenanthrene by Mycobacterium aromativorans strain JS19b1T and is further metabolized to naphthalene-1,2-dicarboxylic acid and then to 1,2-dihydroxynaphthalene.
1-[(E)-2-carboxyvinyl]-2-naphthoic acid is used as a substrate in degradation studies with Mycobacterium aromativorans strain JS19b1T.
1-[(E)-2-carboxyvinyl]-2-naphthoic acid serves as a precursor for the formation of naphthalene-1,2-diol in Arthrobacter sp. P1-1 culture tests.
In the AmpC/4-(2-carboxyvinyl)phenyl boronic acid complex (1KE0), the carboxylate group of the inhibitor interacts with a single ordered water molecule in the AmpC binding site.
2'-hydroxy-4'-methoxychalcone potently reduces iNOS-catalyzed nitric oxide production.
4'-methoxy-2'-hydroxy-chalcone shows anti-tuberculosis activity with an inhibition rate of 78%.
2'-hydroxy-4'-methoxychalcone inhibits LPS-induced production of nitrite and TNF-α in RAW 264.7 macrophages by preventing LPS-induced degradation of IkB-α, thereby blocking NF-κB activation.
2'-hydroxy-4'-methoxychalcone suppresses LPS-induced production of nitrite and TNF-α in RAW 264.7 murine macrophage cells by blocking LPS-induced activation of NF-κB and AP-1 signaling.
2'-Hydroxy-4'-Methoxychalcone inhibits TPA-induced COX-2 protein induction but does not inhibit COX-2 activity or arachidonic acid release from membrane phospholipids.

1-(3,3-Dimethyl-2-oxopentanoyl)piperidine-2-carboxylic Acid 1-{3-[4-tert-Butoxycarbonyl-4-(9H-fluoren-9-ylmethoxycarbonylamino)butyrylamino]phenyl}-3-(3,4-dimethoxyphenyl)propyl Ester (MTXSLF) is a bifunctional molecule that can bind to either the FKBP12 presenter protein or the DHFR target protein, but not both simultaneously, due to destabilizing protein-protein interactions.

In cells with abundant and high-affinity FKBP12, 1-(3,3-Dimethyl-2-oxopentanoyl)piperidine-2-carboxylic Acid 1-{3-[4-tert-Butoxycarbonyl-4-(9H-fluoren-9-ylmethoxycarbonylamino)butyrylamino]phenyl}-3-(3,4-dimethoxyphenyl)propyl Ester (MTXSLF) preferentially partitions to bind FKBP, leaving DHFR uninhibited.

In cells lacking FKBP12 or with low or weak FKBP binding, 1-(3,3-Dimethyl-2-oxopentanoyl)piperidine-2-carboxylic Acid 1-{3-[4-tert-Butoxycarbonyl-4-(9H-fluoren-9-ylmethoxycarbonylamino)butyrylamino]phenyl}-3-(3,4-dimethoxyphenyl)propyl Ester (MTXSLF) selectively partitions to inhibit DHFR and elicit a cytotoxic response.
Boc-D-Dab(Fmoc)-OH is a protected D-amino acid derivative used as a turn unit in the synthesis of the 1R-Chl hairpin polyamide-chlorambucil conjugate.
Boc-His-Pro-Phe-His-Sta-Leu-Phe-NH2 is an inhibitor of the proteinase from Endothia parasitica, and its interaction with the enzyme has been studied by X-ray crystallography.

Vocacapsaicin is rapidly converted to its active metabolites CA-101 and capsaicin in rabbits, with systemic exposure to intact vocacapsaicin and its metabolites being transient, as plasma concentrations are below the limit of quantitation by 2 h for vocacapsaicin and by 4 h for CA-101 and capsaicin.

Vocacapsaicin is a novel prodrug of trans-capsaicin developed as a nonopioid, site-specific treatment for postsurgical pain management, designed to provide at least 96 hours of postsurgical analgesia after a single intraoperative administration.

Vocacapsaicin does not adversely affect bone healing in rats or rabbits after single perioperative administration, as assessed by multiple parameters including radiography, densitometry, and biomechanical testing.
Vocacapsaicin does not show a dose-response relationship for bone healing parameters in rats or rabbits after single perioperative administration.
Boc-Arg-Pro-lauryl ester laurate prolongs fibrinogen-thrombin clotting time in a concentration-dependent manner, with inhibition starting at a peptide concentration of 1.41 x 10^-7 M.

The anticoagulant activity of Boc-Arg-Pro-lauryl ester laurate depends on the presence of an arginine residue in the micellar environment, as its amidolytic activity is reduced in the presence of peptide micelles.
VX-148 is being developed as a second-generation IMPDH inhibitor for the treatment of autoimmune and immune-mediated inflammatory disorders.

MMAE can be conjugated to antibodies via maleimido-caproyl-vc-para-aminobenzyloxycarbonyl-MMAE (mc-vc-pabc-MMAE) linkers, resulting in near quantitative homogeneous substitution at the remaining cysteines after reduction of interchain disulfide bonds.

The anti-endosialin-MC-VC-PABC-MMAE antibody-drug conjugate is selectively cytotoxic to endosialin-positive cells in vitro and produces profound and durable antitumor efficacy in preclinical human tumor xenograft models of endosialin-positive sarcomas and neuroblastomas.
mc-vc-PABC-MMAE is a protease-sensitive linker motif used in antibody-drug conjugates (ADCs), including ADCETRIS®, and is also present in 13 clinical candidates.
Tasiamide is an acyclic peptide isolated from Symploca sp., with the molecular formula C42H67N7O10 and a molecular ion peak at m/z 852.4833 ([M+Na]+, 4·0.8 mmu) as determined by HRFABMS.
Tasiamide is an acyclic peptide containing a hydroxy acid, two N-methylamides, and an ester, structural features common among cyanobacterial peptides.
Tasiamide is an acyclic peptide isolated from the marine cyanobacterium Symploca sp.
Tasiamide displays cytotoxicity against KB cells with an IC50 of 0.48 µg/mL and against LoVo cells with an IC50 of 3.47 µg/mL.
Tasiamide exhibits cytotoxicity against KB and LoVo cancer cell lines with IC50 values of 0.48 µg/mL and 3.47 µg/mL, respectively.
D-alanine-D-alanine is an inhibitor analogue of D-alanine-D-lactate that enables crystallization of Enterococcus facieum D-alanine-D-lactate ligase (VanA).
Doubly modified D-γ-hydroxyvaline residues together with neighboring residues define a novel structural motif characterizing a new family of conopeptides termed γ-hydroxyconophans.
The D-γ-hydroxyvaline residue, when combined with a D-configuration at the R-carbon and specific interactions with surrounding L-amino acids, contributes to the stability of conopeptides.
D-γ-hydroxyvaline is a post-translationally modified residue present in conopeptides, playing a role in their stability and specificity for neuronal targets.

D-γ-hydroxyvaline (D-Hyv; V*) is a nonproteinogenous γ-hydroxylated amino acid that has been identified in ribosomally expressed polypeptide chains and is present in conopeptides from Conus gladiator and Conus mus.
The MeAIB-insensitive (Na+jCl-) dependent system for glycine uptake in mammary tissue is inhibited by preloading with alanine, sarcosine, histidine, or glycine.

MeAIB (alpha-methyl aminoisobutyric acid) is an inhibitor of Na(+)-dependent L-alanine transport in the isolated duodenal mucosa of Gallotia galloti, and its transport is completely inhibited by L-alanine, indicating competition for system A.
MeAIB (N-acetyl-L-isoleucine methyl ester) substantially inhibits glycine uptake in lactating mammary tissue when included at 10 mM in the uptake assay buffer.
β-methylnorleucin can be incorporated instead of isoleucin into recombinant E. coli hirudin at specific positions.
Beta-methylnorleucine is a methylated isoleucine derivative that is biosynthesized in Escherichia coli and incorporated into recombinant hirudin analogs.
The mechanism of deamination for (2S,3S)-3-ethylaspartic acid differs from that of (2S)-aspartic acid, as revealed by studies using C-3-deuteriated substrates.
Methylaspartase exhibits a primary deuterium isotope effect for the removal of the C-3 proton from (2S,3S)-3-ethylaspartic acid, but not for (2S)-aspartic acid.
(2S,3S)-3-Ethylaspartic acid and (2S,3S)-[3-2H]-3-ethylaspartic acid have similar KM values but are deaminated at different rates in the deamination of aspartic acid.
Pyloricidin B, a peptidic moiety composed of L-valine-L-leucine, exhibits potent and highly selective anti-Helicobacter pylori activity.
S-[(Z)-2-aminovinyl]-(3S)-3-methyl-D-cysteine (AviMeCys) is an unusual amino acid incorporated into the D-ring of mersacidin.

In l-Phe-l-Val peptide structures, the phenyl group of l-Phe is involved in intermolecular interactions, including aromatic stacking and hydrogen bonding, to a higher degree than typically observed in peptide structures.

Under the described protocol, [3-13C]serine contributes approximately 100% of the methyl groups used for total body homocysteine remethylation, while accounting for only about 2.8% of total serine flux.
[3-13C]serine is produced in Saccharomyces cerevisiae from [13C]formate primarily via the cytosolic C1-tetrahydrofolate synthase pathway.
[3-13C]serine is detected in ethanolic extracts of Nicotiana plumbaginifolia cells grown with [13C-methyl]nicotine, indicating incorporation of label from nicotine metabolism.
Topical aminolevulinic acid hydrochloride is used in photodynamic therapy of actinic keratoses, where it is converted to protoporphyrin IX upon blue light exposure.

4-[4-(beta-d-glucopyranosyloxy)phenyl]-2-butanone is a food ingredient that can be biotransformed by Phytolacca americana suspension cells to produce beta-glycosides, including 4-[(3S)-3-hydroxybutyl]phenyl-beta-d-glucopyranoside and 2-hydroxy-4-[(3S)-3-hydroxybutyl]phenyl-beta-d-glucopyranoside.

3-(4-hydroxyphenyl)propyl beta-D-glucopyranoside is a marker compound isolated from Korean Salix species and has been structurally elucidated using nuclear magnetic resonance spectroscopy and high-resolution liquid chromatography-mass spectrometry.
Desoxyrhaponticin is an anthraquinone derivative isolated from cultivated Korean Rhubarb Rhizomes (Rheum undulatum, JongDaeWhang).
The initially formed α-glucosyl formate by a Sulfolobus solfataricus β-glucosidase glycine nucleophile mutant is used as a glycosyl donor in transglycosylation reactions.
In the presence of formate and a nucleophile-less mutant of S. solfataricus β-glycosidase, α-glucosyl formate can act as a glycosyl donor in transglycosylation reactions.

2-beta-D-galactopyranosyloxyethyl methacrylate is synthesized via enzymatic transfer of a galactopyranosyl unit from p-nitrophenyl-beta-D-galactopyranoside to 2-hydroxyethyl methacrylate by a beta-glycosidase from Sulfolobus solfataricus.

7-beta-d-glucopyranosyloxy-4'-hydroxyflavylium (P3) exhibits a high cis-trans isomerization barrier in its chalcone form, resulting in a pseudo-equilibrium between all species except the trans-chalcone.

Methyl beta-D-glucopyranoside is slightly effective as an acceptor in the reaction of cycloinulo-oligosaccharide fructanotransferase with cycloinu-lohexaose, but strongly enhances hydrolytic activity.
Remogliflozin is a potent and selective inhibitor of human SGLT2, with a selectivity ratio (K i human SGLT1/K i human SGLT2) of 365.
4-nitrophenyl beta-D-N,N',N''-triacetylchitotriose is a substrate hydrolyzed by a chitinase encoded by the SL0018 (chiA) gene in Salmonella enterica Typhimurium SL1344.

4-amino-1-naphthyl beta-D-galactopyranoside serves as a substrate for beta-galactosidase in an enzymatic reaction that enables fast and selective electrochemical-chemical-chemical redox cycling for ultrasensitive detection.
p-nitrophenyl 6-O-octanoyl-beta-D-glucopyranoside is an acyl donor that mimics trehalose monomycolate and is used to study ag85-mediated acyl transfer reactions with arabinofuranoside acceptors.

p-nitrophenyl 6-O-octanoyl-β-D-glucopyranoside can act as an acyl donor in the presence of ag85C, donating acyl groups to synthetic arabinofuranosides with regiochemistry identical to that observed in whole-cell systems.
Indican is hydrolyzed by β-glucosidase from Polygonum tinctorium to form indoxyl and glucose.

2-(2-hydroxyphenyl)ethanol-O-beta-d-glucopyranoside is a phenolic glycoside isolated from Brassica rapa flowers that exhibits inhibitory activity against intestinal alpha-glucosidase with an IC50 value in the range of 14.43 to 50.20 µM.
Quercetin-3-O-galactoside-7-O-glucoside is a major flavonoid constituent found in both Cuscuta chinensis and Cuscuta australis, with purities above 98% in Tu-Si-Zi samples.

The crystal structure of methyl alpha-D-arabinofuranoside has been determined by low-temperature X-ray and neutron crystallography, resulting in a highly accurate and precise three-dimensional structure.
4-chloro-2-cyclopentylphenyl beta-D-galactopyranoside is used at 1 mM concentration in an enrichment medium to selectively enrich Shigella species in competition with E. coli.

The protein-ligand complex of 5,6-dihydroxy-2-(4-hydroxyphenyl)-7-[3,4,5-trihydroxy-6-(hydroxymethyl)oxane-2-yl]oxychromen-4-one with DENV-2 NS5 methyl transferase exhibits a binding free energy change of -38.01 ± 7.53 to -17.75 ± 11.03 kcal/mol (MM/PBSA model), indicating sustained binding spontaneity and product formation.

5,6-dihydroxy-2-(4-hydroxyphenyl)-7-[3,4,5-trihydroxy-6-(hydroxymethyl)oxane-2-yl]oxychromen-4-one (FLD3) binds to the dengue virus NS5 methyl transferase with a binding affinity of -10.1 kcal/mol, showing better binding than the native ligand (-7.6 kcal/mol) and diclofenac sodium (-7.3 kcal/mol) in molecular docking studies.
A highly enantioselective mutant of cyclohexanone monooxygenase (CHMO) oxidized methyl-p-methylbenzyl thioether to (R) sulfoxide with 98.7% enantiomeric excess.

Cyclohexanone monooxygenase from Acinetobacter calcoaceticus NCIMB 9871, when mutated, can convert methyl-p-methylbenzylsulfide with enantiomeric excess improved from 14%ee to 99%ee, showing both (R)- and (S)-selectivity.

RAPTA-C inhibits pH-dependent DNA damage and selectively damages DNA in cancer cells by coordinating DNA via chloride ligand loss and inducing single-stranded oligomer wrapping around the metal center.

Cumene hydroxyperoxide induces a time-dependent sequence of cellular events in PC12 cells, including decreases in cellular ATP content and mitochondrial membrane potential within 10–30 min, followed by reduced MTT reduction, decreased cellular GSH, actin filament disorganization, and detachment from the substratum between 30–60 min, and late manifestations such as intracellular calcium accumulation and plasma membrane permeabilization after 60–120 min.

Cumene hydroperoxide at low concentrations serves as a substrate for glutathione peroxidase in isolated hepatocytes, leading to marked changes in the cellular redox system and production of only small amounts of lipid peroxides.
Bis(4-methylphenyl)acetylene reacts with (4-methylphenyl)boronic acid under Pd(OAc)2 catalysis and Ag2CO3 oxidation in n-PrOH/H2O to form the corresponding hexaarylbutadiene.
Encapsulation of CAPE in PLGA nanoparticles regulates its dissolution and enhances its bioactivity.
Encapsulation of CAPE in PLGA nanoparticles increases its antimicrobial activity and bioavailability compared to free CAPE.
When encapsulated in PLGA nanoparticles, CAPE can inhibit microbial growth, whereas free CAPE does not show antimicrobial activity.
IDPN is a nitrile compound whose retinal toxicity can be quantitatively assessed using GFAP as a biomarker in rats.
p-chloro-beta-methylphenethylamine is an inhibitor of MAO-B, with its inhibitory activity determined by its conformation upon binding to the enzyme.
p-chloro-beta-methylphenethylamine is a short-acting, probably reversible, selective inhibitor of MAOB activity in mice, with complete recovery within 45 minutes after injection.
Cuminaldehyde semicarbazone (Hscum), the oxygen analog of cuminaldehyde-thiosemicarbazone, results in dramatically reduced proliferation and aflatoxin accumulation in Aspergillus flavus.
Tolyl-phenylmethane products are formed as d0, d2, d3, and d5 isotopomers following oxidation of C6H5CH3 and C6H5CD3 by [Mn2(O)2]4+.
4,4',4''-methylidynetri-anilin is used as a precursor in the synthesis of porous covalent organic frameworks (COFs-SWMU) via a facile ambient temperature approach.
Tri(4-aminophenyl) methane serves as a reactant in the Schiff base condensation reaction with terephthalaldehyde to produce uniform covalent organic nanospheres (CON-TT).
1-bromo-4-(p-tolylethynyl)benzene undergoes copper-catalyzed hydroamination with N-heterocycles to produce hydroaminated products in 63% yield.

bis(2,4,6-triisopropylphenyl) disulfide (TRIP disulfide) acts as an effective hydrogen atom transfer (HAT) co-catalyst for the VB12-catalyzed ring-opening hydrogenation of (2,3-epoxypropyl)benzene, yielding 87% of the Markovnikov alcohol product.
1,5-bis(4-methylphenyl)pentan-3-one (compound 3a) exhibits moderate antimicrobial activity against Streptococcus mitis (MIC = 100 µg/ml) and Streptococcus salivarius (MIC = 200 µg/ml).
p-chloro-phenylethanolamine attenuates contractile responses to both dopamine and serotonin in the quiescent aortic bulb of the clam Tapes watlingi.
The minimum detection concentration (MDC) of platencin in an agar-diffusion two-plate differential sensitivity antisense assay for FabF is 2 ng in Staphylococcus aureus.

Platencin shows MIC values of 0.5 g/ml against S. aureus (MSSA), 0.5 g/ml against S. aureus plus serum, 1 g/ml against S. aureus (MRSA), 1 g/ml against S. aureus (MRSA, macrolide R), 3 g/ml against S. aureus (MRSA, linezolid R), 0.5 g/ml against S. aureus (VISA, vancomycin I), 1 g/ml against E. faecalis (macrolide R), 0.1 εg/ml against E. faecium (vancomycin R), 1 g/ml against S. pneumoniae, and 4 g/ml against E. coli (tolC).
Platencin exhibits a minimum detection concentration (MDC) of 2 ng in an agar-diffusion two-plate differential sensitivity antisense assay for FabF in Staphylococcus aureus.
Platencin has a whole-cell activity IC50 of 0.19 g/ml for fatty acid synthesis inhibition in Staphylococcus aureus and 2.7 g/ml in Streptococcus pneumoniae.
Adamantaplatensimycin is a synthetic analogue of platensimycin in which the adamantyl moiety forms a cage-like structure mimicking the ketolide domain of the parent molecule.
Adamantaplatensimycin is a novel analogue of platensimycin with potent activity against drug-resistant strains such as MRSA and VREF.
Adamantaplatensimycin is a compound with two enantiomers, (À)-2 and (+)-2, that display different biological activities against MRSA and VREF.
The enantiomer (À)-2 of adamantaplatensimycin exhibits minimum inhibitory concentrations of 1.3–1.8 mg/mL against MRSA and VREF.
Mature ␣ 2(I) collagen is completely suppressed in its generation by 250 mM ethyl-2,4-dihydroxybenzoic acid, while mature ␣ 1(I) collagen is not completely inhibited.
Ethyl-2,4-dihydroxybenzoic acid, a prolyl hydroxylase inhibitor, inhibits the generation of both pro ␣ 1(I) and pro ␣ 2(I) collagen in A10 cells after 2 days of treatment.
GA 24 is a gibberellin intermediate in the biosynthetic pathway between ent-copalyl diphosphate and GA 19 or GA 24 in Arabidopsis.
Endogenous GA 24 levels in dwarf lettuce plants are reduced compared to wildtype plants.
GA 24 is an active gibberellin precursor that accumulates to higher levels in goe1 mutants compared to wild type plants.
3-Hydroxymonoethylglycinexylidide is a major unconjugated metabolite of xylidide and is quantified in plasma using liquid chromatography with a validated procedure.
3-Hydroxymonoethylglycinexylidide is a metabolite of lidocaine formed by 3-hydroxylation, and its formation from 3-hydroxylidocaine is competitively inhibited by lidocaine in rat liver microsomes.
Adenostemmoic acid B induces abnormal cytosolic membranous structures in Trypanosoma cruzi epimastigotes at a concentration of 20 µM after 48 hours of treatment.

Adenostemmoic acid B is a diterpenoid isolated from Gymnocoronis spilanthoides var subcordata that exhibits trypanocidal activity against Trypanosoma cruzi epimastigotes and amastigotes with IC50 values of 10.6 µM and 6.1 µM, respectively.
5-hydroxy-6-carboxy-2-di-n-propylaminotetralin is a major urinary metabolite of 5-hydroxy-6-methyl-2-di-n-propylaminotetralin in rats, accounting for 70% of urinary radioactivity.
In vivo, 4-(4-ethyl-piperazin-1-yl)-1-(2,3,4-trihydroxy-phenyl)butan-1-one (SP-04m) reduces 4-hydroxynonenal formation resulting from kainic acid-induced lipid peroxidation.

Micranoic acid B is a triterpenoid isolated from the diethyl ether extract of Schisandra pubescens stems and exhibits moderate to marginal cytotoxicity in vitro with GI50 values of 11.83–35.65 µM against A549, PC-3, KB, and KBvin human cancer cell lines.

N-succinimidyl N-(4-[ 211 At]astatophenethyl)succinamate (SAPS) is an organometallic linker used for 211At labeling of antibodies, and is identified as a viable candidate for radioimmunotherapy due to favorable blood clearance and biological accretion properties.
N-succinimidyl N-(4-[211At]astatophenethyl) succinamate is used as a linker to couple astatine-211 to monoclonal antibodies for vascular-targeted radioimmunotherapy.

N-succinimidyl N-(4-[ 211 At]astatophenethyl)succinamate (SAPS) is a 211 At linker used for labeling humanized anti-Tac monoclonal antibodies, exhibiting the longest plasma survival in vivo among tested SAPS-labeled conjugates and closely mimicking the clearance rate of 125I-labeled antibody in mice.
13-thiastearate causes a low but significant inhibition of stearate conversion to oleate in bloodstream-form T. brucei, reducing the conversion from 23.1% to 19% after 60 min incubation.

13-thiastearate strongly inhibits oleate incorporation into bloodstream-form T. brucei cells, reducing the conversion of exogenous oleate to linoleate from 20.4% to 7.7% after 30 min preincubation at 50 µM.
13-thiastearate at 50 µM is used to study its effect on the incorporation of radiolabeled fatty acids into membrane lipids of bloodstream-form Trypanosoma brucei.
13-thiastearate is trypanocidal against both bloodstream-form and procyclic form T. brucei.
Calcium gamma-hydroxybutyrate (Ca(GHB)2·2H2O) contains water of crystallization, as indicated by IR spectra showing water peaks around 3100 cm⁻¹.
Magnesium pentanoate acts as a catalyst in the cyclotetramerization of 3-(dibutylamino)phthalonitrile in n-pentanol to form 1,8,15,22-tetrakis(dibutylamino)phthalocyaninato magnesium complexes.
Magnesium pentanoate acts as a catalyst in the cyclotetramerization of 3-(dibutylamino)phthalonitrile in n-pentanol to form 1,8,15,22-tetrakis(dibutylamino)phthalocyaninato magnesium complexes.
3-hydroxy-5-methyl-4-isoxazolepropionate (AMPA/kainate) receptors are a major class of glutamate receptors responsible for mediating most synaptic responses in the central nervous system.
3-hydroxy-5-methyl-4-isoxazole-propionate (AMPA) is an excitatory glutamate receptor subtype that can be inhibited by topiramate to exert anti-epileptic effects.

The maximal velocity (Vmax) for the hydrolysis of p-methylphenyl glutarate is approximately two- to threefold less than the Vmax for the corresponding amide or methyl ester, and shows only a slight dependence on the pK of the phenol.

Succinimidyl undecanoate, in the form of dithiobis (succinimidyl undecanoate) (DSU), is used as a component in a protein biochip for the detection of serum IgG and IgM antibodies against Treponema pallidum in patients with syphilis.
Topically applied liposome-encapsulated copper palmitate provides significant protection against both Photofrin- and aminolevulinic acid-induced protoporphyrin IX photosensitivity in animal skin.
Topical copper palmitate treatment avoids the prolonged inflammatory skin reaction associated with Photofrin II-mediated photodynamic therapy.

Cadmium decanoate (CdC10) is a longer cadmium carboxylate whose reactivity is close to that of cadmium octanoate (CdC8), and its use in synthesis does not affect the chemical yield or quality of nanorod-like particles (NPLs).
Cd[CH 3 (CH 2 ) 10 COO] 2 is a metal alkanoate coating that demonstrates good superhydrophobicity on diverse substrates including metal, glass, and paper.
Lead(II) behenate, when used as a precursor with oleic acid as stabilizer, yields larger PbS nanoparticles of about 14.1 nm.
Europium stearate can be used to dope UHMWPE, and gamma-sterilization of UHMWPE doped with europium stearate is compatible with FTIR evaluation of oxidation.

The bridging diborylcarbene ligand can adopt an 'antivan't Hoff-Le Bel' configuration, resulting in a planar tetracoordinate carbon (ptC) atom with a calculated electronic configuration of sigma(2.978) pi(1.501) in the free ligand.
Trimethylhydrazinium groups in 3-(1,1,1-Trimethylhydrazin-1-ium-2-yl)propanoate participate in electrostatic interactions that connect molecular layers in the hydrated crystal structure.
Ruthenium(II) ethylenediamine forms complexes with dppz-CH3, dppz-NO2, and dppn ligands, which have been studied for their DNA binding properties and cytotoxicity in yeast.
The reduction potential of [Ru(en)3]2+/3+ shows poor agreement between calculated and experimental values, with a difference of approximately 246 mV.
Trimethylamine nitroxide (TMAO) is a gut microbiota-derived metabolite implicated in the pathogenesis of cardiovascular diseases.
N,N-dimethylhydrazine dihydrochloride (DMH) increases cholesterol and cardiolipin levels in mitochondria in the post-initiation phase, contributing to evasion of apoptosis in colorectal cancer.
Trimethylamine nitroxide (TMAO) is a gut microbiota-derived metabolite implicated in the pathogenesis of cardiovascular diseases.

Amino alcohol thiourea catalyzes the oxy-Michael addition of arylboronic acids to γ-hydroxyenone, yielding a diol product with up to 64% enantiomeric excess and 22% yield when treated with H2O2 and Na2CO3.

Long-term treatment with N,N'-bis-(3-aminopropyl)cyclohexane-1,4-diamine (DCD), a synthetic polyamine, dose-dependently reduces ethanol intake in genetically high-ethanol-consuming rats (UChB) without affecting overt behavior, food consumption, or total fluid intake.
N,N'-bis-(3-aminopropyl) cyclohexane-1,4-diamine (DCD) likely inhibits spinal cord nociceptive transmission by blocking NMDA receptor channels in dorsal horn neurons.

N,N'-bis-(3-aminopropyl) cyclohexane-1,4-diamine (DCD) is a synthetic polyamine that, when administered intravenously at 2.5, 5, and 10 mg/kg in rats, dose-dependently reduces integrated C-fiber reflex responses and wind-up activity.

N,N'-bis-(3-aminopropyl)cyclohexane-1,4-diamine acts as an inhibitor of alcohol consumption in vivo, reducing ethanol intake by up to 60% in genetically high-ethanol-consuming UChB rats after 60 days of daily oral dosing at 20 mg/kg.
The formation of a four-membered transition state on the silica surface is hindered by the larger steric demand of HN-(SiMe2R)3 trisilazanes, resulting in markedly reduced surface reactivity.

1, 3-diaminopropane dihydrochloride administration in rats led to markedly lower ornithine decarboxylase activity, bromodeoxyuridine labeling indices, and mRNA expression levels of cyclin D1 in the urinary bladder mucosa compared to untreated controls.

In a rat two-stage urinary bladder carcinogenesis model, administration of 1, 3-diaminopropane dihydrochloride reduced tumor size, multiplicity, and incidence, particularly of papillomas, compared to untreated controls.
Dithioethylamine acts as a bridging ligand in the H cluster of the [FeFe] hydrogenase from Clostridium pasteurianum, as depicted in computational structural models.

1, 3-diaminopropane dihydrochloride administration in rats led to markedly lower ornithine decarboxylase activity, bromodeoxyuridine labeling indices, and mRNA expression levels of cyclin D1 in the urinary bladder mucosa compared to untreated controls.

In a rat two-stage urinary bladder carcinogenesis model, administration of 1, 3-diaminopropane dihydrochloride reduced tumor size, multiplicity, and incidence, particularly of papillomas, compared to untreated controls.
3,4-diamino-3-cyclobuten-1,2-dione (squaramide) is a rigid planar scaffold used in the design of VLA-4 antagonists with potentially advantageous pharmacokinetic profiles.
3,4-diamino-3-cyclobutene-1,2-dione functions as an amino acid isostere when incorporated into VLA-4 integrin antagonists to improve the rate of clearance.
Rubrofusarin is a compound isolated from the bioactive methanol-soluble extract of Cassia obliqua stem and fruits that exhibits antimycobacterial activity.
Artocarpetin is a potent tyrosinase inhibitor that contains a 4-resorcinol moiety in the B ring of its flavone skeleton.
Artocarpetin is a furanoflavone isolated from Artocarpus heterophyllus wood that exhibits strong mushroom tyrosinase inhibitory activity with an IC50 lower than 50 µM.
Artocarpetin is a flavone analog with high inhibitory activity against mushroom tyrosinase, which is substantially decreased by methoxylation at the C7 position.
Bellidifolin is a xanthone compound isolated from the roots of Swertia punctata.
1,5,8-trihydroxy-3-methoxyxanthone is a tetraoxygenated xanthone present in Swertia purpurascens Wall.
Bellidifolin exhibits acetylcholinesterase (AChE) inhibitory activity, with potency similar to galanthamine in enzyme assays.
Reticulol inhibits metastasis of B16F10 melanoma cells in mice in a dose-dependent manner, with intravenous administration resulting in greater metastasis suppression than peritonitis administration.
Talacoumarin B exhibits moderate anti-Ab42 aggregation activity, representing the first reported Ab42 inhibitory aggregation activity for coumarins.
talacoumarin B is a 3-alkyl-6,8-dioxycoumarin with a rare structural moiety and exhibits moderate anti-Aβ42 aggregation activity, representing the first reported coumarin with this activity.
Talacoumarin B is a newly isolated coumarin compound from the fungus Talaromyces flavus BYD07-13, and has demonstrated anti-β42 aggregation, cytotoxic, and antimicrobial activities.
(3 E,7 Z)-1-(2,6-dihydroxy-4-methoxyphenyl)-deca-3,7-dien-1-one is a new alkyl phloroglucinol derivative isolated from Syzygium levinei.

(3 E,7 Z)-1-(2,6-dihydroxy-4-methoxyphenyl)-deca-3,7-dien-1-one inhibits the proliferation of leukemia K562 and HL-60 cells and induces differentiation of K562 cells, as indicated by morphological changes and increased intracellular hemoglobin.
6,8-dihydroxy-5-methoxy-3-methyl-1H-isochromen-1-one is an isocoumarin derivative with antibiotic activity, identified from the mangrove endophyte Phyllosticta capitalensis.
6,8-dihydroxy-5-methoxy-3-methyl-1H-isochromen-1-one exhibits α-glucosidase inhibitory activity with an IC50 value of 89.4 µM.

8-angeloyl-7-hydroxy-5-methoxycoumarin is a geometrical isomer of tigloylcoumarin and serves as a substrate for (-)quinine-catalyzed asymmetric intramolecular Michael addition, predominantly forming a (2R,3R)-enantiomer in the trans-chromanone system.
Demethylwedelolactone, a derivative of wedelolactone in which a methoxy group is replaced by a hydroxyl group, does not inhibit caspase-11 expression.
Pestalotiopsone F is an anticancer compound isolated from fungal endophytes.
5-carbomethoxymethyl-7-hydroxy-2-pentylchromone is a member of the pestalotiopsones isolated from Pestalotiopsis sp.
5-carbomethoxymethyl-7-hydroxy-2-pentylchromone exhibits antimicrobial activity against tested microbial strains and shows moderate antioxidant property.
4-Bromophenyl-5-chloropyrazine-2-carboxamide exhibits antimycobacterial activity with a minimum inhibitory concentration (MIC) of 12.5 μg/mL.
Diaminopyridine carboxamide is used as a structural template for designing Na v 1.7 modulators with improved selectivity for Na v 1.8 over Na v 1.5 and TTX-S channels.
DQP-1105 is a novel allosteric modulator that targets the GluN2C/D NMDA receptor.
Quinazolinecarboxamide compounds are inhibitors of mitochondrial threonine dehydrogenase (TDH) that impede cell growth and induce autophagy in mouse embryonic stem (ES) cells.

Quinazolinecarboxamide compounds selectively inhibit TDH-mediated threonine catabolism in mouse ES cells, failing to affect the growth of HeLa cells at concentrations 300 times higher than those required to kill ES cells.
Pyrazolo[4,3-h]quinazoline-3-carboxamides function as multi-cyclin-dependent kinase inhibitors by binding to the ATP pocket through key structural hydrogen-bonding motifs.

Pyrazolo[4,3-h]quinazoline-3-carboxamides function as inhibitors of CDK2 and CyA, with their inhibitory activity influenced by structural features identified through 3D-QSAR and molecular docking studies.
Pyrazolo[4,3-h]quinazoline-3-carboxamide analogs have been analyzed as CDK2 inhibitors using 3D-QSAR and docking studies.
4-bromophenyl biphenyl is oxidized by P450 2A13 and 2A6, with P450 2A13 showing higher product formation than P450 2A6.

Thiazole derivative 9b, containing a 4-bromophenyl group, is less potent as a COX-2 inhibitor than thiazole derivative 9a, due to the decreased COX-2 inhibitory potential associated with the presence of bromine.
The 4-bromophenyl derivative of thiazole (3g) shows trypanocidal activity against trypomastigotes with a CC50 of 2.2 µM.
A hybrid compound containing a 4-bromophenyl-thiazole moiety exhibits potent antiproliferative activity against A549 lung cancer cells with an IC50 of 0.40 µM.

2-[4-(4-Chloro-2-fluorophenoxy)phenyl]-pyrimidine-4-carboxamide (PPPA) is a novel structural analog of the state-dependent Naϩ channel blocker V102862, designed to have higher in vitro potency, faster binding kinetics, and more pronounced state dependence than existing Naϩ channel blockers.

PPPA {2-[4-(4-chloro-2-fluorophenoxy)phenyl]pyrimidine-4-carboxamide} is an analog of Naϩ channel blockers with enhanced state-dependent (inactivated versus resting channel) selectivity and a greater protective index against rotarod toxicity compared to carbamazepine and lamotrigine.

2-[4-(4-Chloro-2-fluorophenoxy)phenyl]-pyrimidine-4-carboxamide (PPPA) is a novel, broad-spectrum Na(+) channel blocker that is approximately 1000 times more potent, has 2000-fold faster binding kinetics, and at least 10-fold higher state dependence than carbamazepine and lamotrigine in recombinant rat Na(v)1.2 channels and native rat dorsal root ganglion neurons.

In rat pain models (partial sciatic nerve ligation, Freund's complete adjuvant, postincisional pain), 2-[4-(4-Chloro-2-fluorophenoxy)phenyl]-pyrimidine-4-carboxamide (PPPA) produces efficacy at minimal effective doses of 1 to 3 mg/kg orally, similar to clinically relevant comparators.
Imidazole-4-carboxamide is a purine-like nucleoside analogue that can serve as a scaffold for the attachment of variable side motifs to explore base pairing properties.
Imidazole-4-carboxamide is a nucleobase that can mimic adenine and has not been investigated as a substitute for adenine in isonucleosides.
Imidazole-4-carboxamide is used as a universal nucleobase in the synthesis of enantiomeric isodideoxynucleosides designed as biological mimics of anti-HIV active D- and L-isodideo-xyadenosines.
Propyl 5-chloropyrazinoate is a FAS I inhibitor that was tested in NMR ligand-binding experiments.
Stelleranol is a stereo isomer of spirobiflavonoids and exhibits potent anti-RSV (respiratory syncytial virus) activity.
Neochamaejasmine A is a major component in dried roots of Stellera chamaejasme L. and inhibits Organic anion transporter 1 (OAT1)-mediated lamivudine uptake with an IC50 of 8.35 µmol/L in vitro.
Aescultitannin B is a trimeric proanthocyanidin with an interflavanyl linkage that inhibits R-glucosidase type IV from Bacillus stearothermophilus with an IC50 value of 3.5 µM.
IND02-trimer, a cinnamon extract-derived polyphenol, binds to HIV-1 gp120 in the μM range and inhibits gp120 binding to CD4 and the anti-CD4i antibody mAb 17b, thereby blocking HIV-1 entry.
3-glucuronosyl quercetin can bind to the active site of myeloperoxidase (MPO) with its B-ring oriented to the heme plane in a manner similar to the aglycone quercetin.
Q3GA can act as an attenuator for lipid hydroperoxide-dependent oxidative stress in nerve cells.
Quercetin catechin at concentrations of 50 to 100 µmol/liter reduces the cytotoxicity of artificially prepared acetaldehyde-albumin complexes incubated at 37°C for 3 hours.
UGT1A1 shows a substrate inhibition pattern for 3,7,4'-trihydroxyflavonol glucuronidation with Ks values in the range of 0.60–5.41 µM for both 3-O- and 7-O-glucuronidation.
The Km values for 3,7,4'-trihydroxyflavonol glucuronidation by UGT1A1 are in the range of 0.13–1.57 µM.
3,5,7,3′,4′-pentahydroxyflavonol-3-O-R-L-arabinofuranoside, isolated from cranberry extracts, demonstrates potent antioxidant activity.
5,7,3'-trihydroxy-4'-methoxydihydroflavonol is a flavanone identified as an inducer of the nod gene in sterile root exudate of Vicia sativa.
3'-O-methyl-(-)-epicatechin-5-O-sulphate is a main O-methyl-(-)-epicatechin-sulfate metabolite identified in humans.
Alkylation of the 3,3',4'-hydroxyl group of quercetin with ethyl groups results in neither gain nor loss of its inhibitory rate against human prostate cancer cells.
3,3',4'-trihydroxyflavonol displays ferroptosis-inhibitory and antioxidant effects in comparison to its Diels-Alder dimer.

(-)-catechin gallate in grape products can be quantified by LC/MS/MS detection of its protonated molecular ion and characteristic fragment ions, with concentrations measured in the range of 36.72–18,800 ng/mL and showing good linearity (r2 > 0.99) under optimized low collision energy (20%).
Gericudranin E, isolated from the root bark of Cudrania tricuspidata, exhibits hepatoprotective effects.
Gericudranin E shows significant hepatoprotective effects with an EC50 value of 39.87 ± 0.7 µM against tacrine-induced cytotoxicity in Hep G2 cells.
2-(3,4-dihydroxyphenyl)-6-hydroxychroman-4-one displays antiparasitic activity by targeting pteridine reductase-1 and shows significant inhibition against T. brucei and L. infantum at 10 µM.
Aureusidin synthase does not catalyze the 3'-oxygenation of 4,4',6-trihydroxyaurone.
4,6,4′-trihydroxyaurone is a hydroxylated aurone derivative in which the C-4′ position is the most suitable for B-ring hydroxylation to achieve high tyrosinase inhibitory potency.

4-Benzoyl-2,5-dimethyl-3,4-bis(hydroxymethyl)pyrrole is a pyrrole derivative used as a core structure for synthesizing bis(N-methylcarbamoyl) derivatives with potential antineoplastic activity and modified reactivity due to electronic perturbations from phenyl ring position.
3-keto-3-phenylpropanoic acid, when supplied as an ethyl ester at millimolar amounts, does not exhibit precursor activity in fungal metabolism.
The first site of oxidation for HSD is the 3-phenoxyl group via a one-electron reduction.

HSD is a flavonoid with an electron-donating methoxy group on its B-ring, which elevates its reduction potential to 0.72 V compared to other flavonoids with catechol groups in the B-ring (E = 0.5–0.7 V).
HSD can scavenge and inactivate harmful radical species such as alkyl peroxyl radicals (E = 1.05 V) and superoxide radicals (E = 0.94 V) in the organism.

HSD's high chemical reactivity can prevent free radical-induced injuries by directly interacting with radical oxygen species (ROS), inducing antioxidant enzyme activation, chelating metal ions, reducing α-tocopherol radicals, inhibiting oxidases, and reducing nitric oxide oxidative stress, and increasing the antioxidant effects of low molecular antioxidants.

2-phenylbutyrophenone is a chiral phenyl alcohol that does not achieve baseline separation by capillary electrochromatography with negatively charged sulfated β-CD as chiral selector under standard conditions.

Enantioenriched 1,2-diphenylbutan-1-one can be recovered in essentially quantitative yield (>95%) with less than 10% erosion in enantiomeric excess (<10%) after nickel-catalyzed borylation, due to the reduced basicity of the reaction medium.

Bulky halide substituents at the 4-position of the phenylbutyrophenone moiety greatly improve affinity for the DA D 2 receptor and reduce 5-HT 2A affinity by approximately 35-fold, resulting in a reversed selectivity profile similar to classical neuroleptics.
Introduction of a 2-amino group onto the phenylbutyrophenone scaffold enhances binding affinity at all three target receptors (5-HT 2A, 5-HT 2C, and DA D 2) compared to the parent compound.
Phenylbutyrophenone derivatives function as antagonists at the 5-HT 2A, 5-HT 2C, and DA D 2 receptors, with selectivity profiles influenced by substituents at the phenyl ring.

3-(hydroxymethyl)benzophenone undergoes a highly efficient (Φ ∼ 0.6) intramolecular redox reaction in acidic (pH < 3) aqueous solution to form 3-formylbenzhydrol (95%) with trace amounts of the fully oxidized 3-formylbenzophenone (5%).
The presence of glucose or amino acids does not repress m-toluate utilization in bacteria harboring the pWW0 plasmid.
m-toluate is a water-soluble, non-volatile aromatic compound that serves as a model substrate for studying biodegradation of aromatic hydrocarbons in the mycorrhizosphere.
Bencaine abolishes inactivation of the action potential (AP) in nodes and axons by increasing intracellular calcium concentration, similar to hyperpolarization of the membrane.
Bencain is a local anesthetic that, like procaine, can be displaced from membrane lipids by an increase in intracellular calcium concentration, allowing restoration of node excitability.
