Bencain at a concentration of 0.19 mM reduces the amplitude and steepness of the action potential (AP) and increases the critical depolarization level (CDL) in nerve nodes, while at 0.38 mM it abolishes AP generation and causes local responses to strong stimuli.
Bencain at concentrations of 0.19 mM and 0.38 mM shifts the resting potential of nerve nodes by 3–6 mV toward hyperpolarization.
Bencain at 0.38 mM completely prevents action potential generation in Ranvier nodes and slightly increases the initial resting potential by 3–6 mV.
1,3-diphenyl-2-butene-1-one is identified as an oxygen-containing phenyl compound present in SVOCs from ABS and PS, likely resulting from degradation product reactions with oxygen.
Furoyl pyrroloquinolones are synthesized by direct acylation of pyrroloquinolones with acid chlorides, or more efficiently by coupling with aryl- and heteroarylboronic acids using coupling acids.
Furoyl pyrroloquinolones exhibit improved selectivity for PDE1 and PDE6 over PDE5 compared to sildenafil.
Substituents on the phenyl group of furoyl pyrroloquinolones can be modified to improve oral bioavailability.
Furoyl pyrroloquinolone is a highly selective and potent inhibitor of the phosphodiesterase 5 (PDE5) enzyme.
N-[7-(methylsulfanyl)[1,3]thiazolo[4,5-g][1,3]benzothiazol-2-yl]-2-furamide (compound 5E4) inhibits DYRK1A kinase by 97% at 1 µM in a Premier Screen-kinase panel.
Furanoic galactose is a structural component present in lipopeptidophosphoglycan (LPPG) isolated from Trypanosoma cruzi trypomastigotes.
In Trypanosoma cruzi trypomastigotes, furanoic galactose (galf) residues are present in both low molecular mass LPPG-like molecules and in glycoproteins of 80–90 kDa.
Furanoic galactose (galf) is a component of LPPG glycoconjugates found on the surface of Trypanosoma cruzi epimastigotes and trypomastigotes.
Direct chemical evidence shows that furanoic galactose (galf) residues are present in the N-linked oligosaccharide chains of certain surface glycoproteins in Trypanosoma cruzi.
In AMDase-catalyzed reactions, α-(furan-2-yl) malonic acid (12) is a superior substrate with Km = 10.1–14.5 mM and kcat = 188.2–177 s−1 mM−1, outperforming other disubstituted furanyl substrates.
Furanyl salicylate is a novel pharmacophore identified as a substrate mimetic with superior properties for drug design compared to phosphotyrosine.
Furanyl salicylate acts as a phosphotyrosine mimetic but is much less polar than phosphotyrosine and functions as a competitive inhibitor of Yersinia PTP YopH.
Furanyl salicylate binds in the active site pocket of YopH, forming hydrogen bonds with the side chains of Gln357 and/or Arg404, which are unique to YopH among PTPs.
2-amino-5-benzoyl-4-(2-furyl)thiazole analogs with arylalkyl or heteroarylalkyl substituents at the 2-amino position can achieve over 500-fold selectivity for A2A over A1 adenosine receptors.
2-amino-5-benzoyl-4-(2-furyl)thiazoles are antagonists of the human A2A adenosine receptor, with potency measured by Ki values typically less than 100 nM for hydrophobic 2-amino substituents.

2-amino-5-benzoyl-4-(2-furyl)thiazole derivatives have been developed as selective AdoA2A receptor antagonists, with one substituted derivative showing a Ki of 12 nM at human AdoA2A and over 500-fold selectivity versus AdoA1R.

2-amino-5-benzoyl-4-(2-furyl) thiazole derivatives act as antagonists of the human A2A adenosine receptor, with the most active compound showing a Ki of 12 nM and 580-fold selectivity over the A1 adenosine receptor.

FA-Phe-Ala-Ala (N-(3-[2-furyl]acryloyl)-L-phenylalanyl-L-alanyl-L-alanine) is hydrolyzed by both ACE N-domain and C-domain with similar apparent Km values, but is hydrolyzed faster by the testicular ACE (C-domain).

Furanacryloyl-L-phenylalanyl-glycine-glycine is a substrate used to measure the specific activity of rabbit lung angiotensin-converting enzyme at pH 7.5 and 25°C, with a specific activity of about 180 s⁻¹ for cleavage by the enzyme preparation.
Furylacryloyl-phenylalanyl-glycyl-glycine (FAPGG) is a synthetic substrate used to assay ACE activity.

Dietary intake of 3-Fluorobenzanthrone (FLA) affects hepatic aryl hydrocarbon hydroxylase (AHH) activity in rats, with lower AHH activities observed at dietary doses of 750 and 1500 mg/kg for 60 and 90 days, respectively.
ABAP has a total oxidant scavenging capacity (TOSC) of 3.1 Ϯ 0.3 in the KMBA assay for SIN-1-generated oxidants.

3-AT exhibits in vitro cytotoxicity against myeloid leukemia cells, with an effective concentration of approximately 100 µg/ml (100 µM) killing HL-60 cells, which is associated with apoptotic internucleosomal DNA cleavage.
3-AT shows high selectivity for leukemia cells over other cell lines, as 100 µg/ml has little effect on non-leukemia cell lines.
3-AT at concentrations above 200 µg/ml induces DNA degradation, evidenced by mild smearing but no clear internucleosomal ladder formation in agarose gels.
3-AT is capable of kidney retention, which may allow for lower dose administration while maintaining high efficacy in vivo.
4-Methylbenzanthrone reduces ventral prostate weight in a dose-dependent manner in developmentally exposed adult animals.
Administration of 4-Methylbenzanthrone to rats during gestation and lactation does not alter adult offspring body weight.
4-methylbenzanthrone reduces ERa mRNA expression but does not affect ERb mRNA in female medial preoptic area (MPO) neurons.

4-Methylbenzanthrone has effects on androgen receptor (AR) and estrogen receptor beta (ERα) in the prostate of adult offspring that are opposite at mRNA and protein levels compared to 3-Bromobenzanthrone.
4-methylbenzanthrone is a methylated derivative of benzanthrone that is tumorigenic in mouse skin.
MelQ induces tissue-specific types and distributions of mutations in the liver and bone marrow, which are distinct from those induced by other carcinogens.
3-(4-Toluidino)benzanthrone is a benzanthrone derivative that exhibits frameshifting mutagenicity in Salmonella typhimurium strain TA98 with about 65 revertants per nmol under microsomal activation.
BHT is a chemical carcinogen used in murine models to induce lung adenocarcinoma, often in combination with MCA.
3-hydroxy-DMBA is a phenolic metabolite of DMBA that elutes at a retention time of 35.1 min on h.p.l.c. and can be confused with 2- and 4-hydroxy derivatives due to their similar elution times.
3-OH-DMBA is a metabolite of DMBA that cannot be further metabolized to a 'bay-region' dihydrodiol-oxide.
3-OHDMBA is a metabolic product of DMBA by adrenal homogenate, but is difficult to separate chromatographically from 7-OHM-12-MBA.

3-hydroxy-DMBA is a phenolic metabolite of DMBA detected in rat mammary cell culture following enzyme treatment, and its levels are higher in YV cells compared to OV and P cells after β-glucuronidase treatment.
3-Hydroxydimethylbenzanthrone is a major organosoluble phenol metabolite produced from DMBA metabolism by mouse mammary epithelial cells.
4-amino-1,8-naphthalimide inhibits PARP with an EC50 of 180 nM.
The 4-(S)-methyl pyrrolidinedione group is majorly relevant for efficient binding to bacterial acetyl-CoA carboxylase.
The enantiomeric excess in the polymer backbone (ee p) has been calculated for 4-methyl-ε-caprolactone.
4-MeCL can be polymerized via ring-opening polymerization (ROP) using Novozym 435 as a catalyst, achieving enantioselectivity up to 0.88 enantiomeric excess in poly-(S)-4-MeCL.

(R)-4-methylcaprolactone can be produced from prochiral 4-methylcyclohexanone via asymmetric chemo-enzymatic Baeyer-Villiger oxidation using (+-)-4-methyloctanoic acid, Candida Antarctica lipase B, and 30% aqueous hydrogen peroxide.

(R)-4-methylcaprolactone can be obtained in optically pure form with a yield greater than 99% and enantiomeric excess (ee) of 96% (R) via chemo-enzymatic Baeyer-Villiger asymmetric oxidation of 4-methylcyclohexanone using Candida antarctica lipase B and H2O2.
The hydrazone derivative 3c bearing a 2-oxo-5-thiono-1,3-thiazolidine moiety is the most potent compound in the series for leishmanicidal activity.

Methylthiirane, when used as a ligand in the form of MTI6 (Au14(MTI)6), can induce chiral image charges and associated chiroptical effects in gold clusters when adsorbed in an achiral pattern on the cluster surface.

2-Methylaziridine acetate (2-MAA) treatment of cultured limbs for 1, 3, 6, or 24 hours normalizes acetylated p53 at lysine 379 (p53K379Ac) to β-actin and its quantification by densitometry in protein extracts.
4a-hydroxy-6(S)-methyltetrahydropterin follows linear Arrhenius kinetics with respect to temperature at pH 7.4 and 8.4, indicating a constant rate over the temperature range of 4 to 37 °C.
4a-hydroxy-6(S)-methyltetrahydropterin serves as a substrate for dehydratase activity assays measured spectrophotometrically at 10 °C in 25 mM BES-Na pH 7.4.
6(S)-methyl 4a-hydroxytetrahydropterin is a good substrate for 4a-hydroxytetrahydropterin dehydratase, as it is completely consumed by the enzyme in the absence of biphasic progress curves.

6(S)-methyl-4a-hydroxy-tetrahydropterin is a substrate for human intestinal dehydratase, with kinetic properties (Km and Vmax) identical to those of purified human liver dehydratase under physiological pH conditions.
6-oxopiperidine-3-carboxylic acids are 1,2-disubstituted carboxylic acids that can be synthesized via a one-pot multicomponent reaction involving alicyclic anhydrides, primary amines, and aldehydes.
Thioxothiazolidinone compounds can be synthesized via Knoevenagel condensation of substituted benzaldehydes and 2-thioxo-thiazolidin-4-one in the presence of β-alanine and refluxing acetic acid.
Thioxothiazolidinones are a class of compounds that have been explored as potential inhibitors of the zinc metalloprotease ADAMTS-5.
4-thioxothiazolidin-2-one serves as a precursor for the synthesis of 3,5-disubstituted thioxothiazolidinone derivatives through Knoevenagel condensation and subsequent N-alkylation.

Thioxothiazolidinone derivatives can act as competitive inhibitors of a subset of protein tyrosine phosphatases, including PTP1B, and can be uncharged, which may improve cellular permeability compared to highly charged PTP inhibitors.
2,5-dimethylcyclohexanone produced by the rhizosphere microbiome of rotation-grown peanut is not detected in the rhizosphere microbiome of monocropped peanut.

2,5-Dimethylcyclohexanone is a pure volatile organic compound (VOC) whose effect on mycelial growth of Fusarium oxysporum was assessed by exposing fungal cultures to different concentrations (0, 0.5, 5.0, 20, and 50 mg/L in air volume) for 7 days under dark, 28°C incubation.
cis-5-hydroperoxy-6-hydroxy-5,6-dihydrothymine is a major thymine hydroperoxide product generated from cellular DNA upon exposure to oxidizing agents.

Nickel porphyrin forms complexes with mutant and wild type ferrochelatase, and the resonance Raman spectrum of Ni(ProtoP) in the E287Q mutant shows spectral features with frequencies nearly identical to those of Ni(MesoP) in solution at pH 8.
Upon removal of two electrons from the silver(II) porphyrin dimer, a mixed-valent pi-cation radical species is formed, with its structure influenced by the nature of counter ions.
Silver(II) porphyrin can form monometallic dimers with a highly flexible ethane bridge, which enables stepwise electron removal from the metal center during oxidation.

Silver(II) porphyrin dimers can undergo stepwise oxidation to generate both AgII /AgIII mixed-valent and AgIII /AgIII porphyrin dimers, with 2e oxidation stabilizing the cis form due to metallophilic interaction.
silver(II) porphyrin is a water-soluble porphyrin complex with potential for photodynamic therapy and antibacterial applications due to its unique photophysical and chemical properties.
Zinc(II) bis-porphyrin forms complexes with cationic bis-porphyrins and can be used to study DNA-protein interactions in gel electrophoresis assays.
FeTPP(OH)4 catalyzes the oxidation and dechlorination of certain organic pollutants in soil suspensions, achieving up to 60–62% degradation in allophanic and red soil suspensions.
Hydroxy-iron(III) porphyrin can participate in hydrogen atom transfer and recombination reactions with etodolac radicals in the presence of oxo-iron(IV) porphyrins.
Porphyrin anilines can be derivatized to produce bromoacetamidoporphyrin synthons, which balance reactivity and moisture sensitivity in chemical synthesis.

Manganese tetrakis benzoic acid porphyrin at 10 μM improves endothelium-dependent relaxation in human saphenous vein endothelial function assays compared to control (57.0% vs 37.8%, P < 0.01) in an ex vivo model.
ZnHP interacts with liposomes, exhibiting a pH-dependent effective overall binding constant (K obs b) due to the different affinities of its protonated forms for the lipid bilayer.
H2TOPyP is an alkylated pyridine residue positioned on the periphery of cobalt porphyrins, modulating the structure and DNA binding behavior of the porphyrin-DNA complex.
FeT(2,6-F 2 -3-SO 3 P)P 3À is a metalloporphyrin catalyst that shows 19% remaining fraction after 6 hours of degradation in the presence of cyclophosphamide under oxidative conditions at pH 7.4.

Zn II TBAP is a metalloporphyrin that provides dose-dependent protection against Ca 2+ ionophore-induced toxicity in cultured cortical neurons, with lower IC50 values than Ni I TBAP and higher than CO-Ru II TBAP and Cl 2 -Sn IV TBAP.
H2TPyP is used as a ligand in the solvothermal synthesis of Fe(TPyP) complexes with ferrocene.
Tetrahydroxyphenylporphyrin, when coordinated to ruthenium as [Ru II (TTP)(CO)], acts as a catalyst for cycloaddition reactions with methyl acrylate, showing high activity in this context.
CuTMPyP is a copper-containing porphyrin species formed in situ from the interaction of Cu 2+ ions with [H2TMPyP]4+ under electrochemical conditions.

Copper(II) tetrahydroxyphenylporphyrin (CuTMPyP) is formed by the immediate incorporation of Cu 2+ into free base porphyrin and is stable in the anodic regime, with formation being irreversible and stable in both anodic and cathodic scans.

Cu(II)TMpyP-4 forms complexes with poly(dA-dC)-poly(dT-dG) polymer that exhibit an inverse relaxation time of 1100 sec⁻¹ at r0 -0.07 and a polymer concentration of 60 µM in temperature-jump kinetic experiments.
CuTCPH is a copper porphyrin used as a boron carrier in BNCT studies and has been found to be non-toxic in mice at therapeutically effective doses up to 400 mg/kg administered over 2 days.

The hydration of tricalcium oxy silicate (C3S) can be observed and its retardation influenced by the addition of polycarboxylate ether superplasticizers, as studied using fluorescence microscopy and calorimetry.
Orthoperiodate at pH 8 can be used as an oxidant to drive water oxidation catalyzed by hematite-core complexes with covalently attached polyoxometalate ligands.
Strontium nitrate suppresses sensory irritation caused by structurally and biologically unrelated chemical irritants over a pH range of 0.6–12.
CaPPS is a glycosaminoglycan that requires sulfation for its inhibitory effects against aggrecanase activity, with 3.8 sulfate groups per disaccharide being the most potent for inhibition.
CaPPS inhibits aggrecanase activity through direct binding and electrostatic interactions, with the degree of sulfation being critical for this activity.
CaPPS inhibits aggrecanase activity through direct binding and electrostatic interactions, with the degree of sulfation being critical for this activity.
Ca-PPS inhibits aggrecanase activity in explant cultures of bovine articular cartilage and in conditioned medium from bovine joint capsule explant cultures.
Ce(NH4)2(NO3)3 is used in equimolar amounts with EDTA to prepare a homogeneous Ce(IV)/EDTA complex for DNA modification at pH 7.0 and 37°C.
Nitrooxyalkyl moieties are incorporated into NO-donor antioxidants to provide vasodilator properties.

Nitroxy derivatives of lamtidine analogues act as histamine H2 receptor antagonists but are 10-fold less potent than analogues lacking NO-donor properties in gastroprotective activity against 0.6 N HCl-induced gastric lesions.
Nitrooxy functions as a nitric oxide (NO) donor moiety in the synthesis of alpha-tocopherol analogs with vasodilating and antiplatelet properties.
Nitrooxy serves as a nitric oxide-releasing group in alpha-tocopherol mimetics evaluated for their biological effects.
The [ETriN + NO3]- anion can be detected by IMS-MS, likely due to the presence of erythritol trinitrate in solution.

Dicalcium oxalate (Ca2C2O4¹) complex formation in urine solubilizes substantial amounts of calcium oxalate, reducing the sensitivity of urine calcium oxalate solubility to changes in urinary calcium over the physiological range.
Li2CaGeO4 is a top-ranked HF-scavenger coating material that contains s-block elements and is among the most effective candidates for protecting LiCoO2 from HF.
Ca-Mg phosphate possesses the intrinsic property of significantly blocking the growth process of nanoparticles, which is essential for efficient cellular uptake of DNA.
Calcium magnesium phosphate precipitates can adsorb DNA and prevent particle growth, resulting in increased cellular uptake of DNA and notably high transgene expression.

Epidote is a mineral present in some stone quarry particles that, when present in sufficient amounts, induces a marked increase in the release of proinflammatory cytokines interleukin-6 (IL-6) and interleukin-8 (IL-8) from human epithelial lung cells (A549).
The anisotropy spectrum of L-glyceric acid in the 170–280 nm region is indicative of enantiomeric excess, a property observed in hydroxycarboxylic acids and proteinogenic amino acids.

The L-enantiomer of glyceric acid calcium salt dihydrate, after purification to remove Ca2+, displays a characteristic anisotropy spectrum in aqueous D2O solution that matches that of serine, indicating the same optical activity.

3,5,3',4',5'-pentamethoxystilbene (MR-5) increases the interaction of CDK inhibitors with CDK4 and CDK2, strongly inhibits CDK4 kinase activity, and leads to accumulation of hypophosphorylated Rb in MCF-7 cells.

3,4,5-trimethoxyphenyl-4-(2-oxoimidazolidin-1-yl)benzenesulfonate (IMZ-602), a combretastatin A-4 mimetic and tubulin ligand, causes condensation of B2R-GFP, fluorescein-5-thiocarbamoyl-D-Arg-[Hyp3, Igl5, Oic7, Igl8]-BK, and beta-arrestin2-GFP into punctate structures close to the cell surface.
DMCM, a ␤-carboline, acts as a negative allosteric modulator and inhibits GABA-induced currents at the benzodiazepine site of GABA A receptors in cultured auditory neurons.

3-methoxy-4-hydroxyphenylethylethyleneglycol (MHPG) is a metabolite of norepinephrine, and its brain levels are elevated by aspartame and tyrosine administration in rat brain regions such as the hypothalamus, locus coeruleus, and brain stem.
Compound 10a, a N-(4-hydroxyphenyl)-3,4,5-trimethoxybenzamide derivative, exhibits maximal acetylcholinesterase inhibition at 50 µM with 97% inhibition.

N-(4-hydroxyphenyl)-3,4,5-trimethoxybenzamide derivatives with an N,N-dialkylaminoethoxy/propoxy moiety act as potential memory enhancers and exhibit acetylcholinesterase-inhibiting activity with IC50 values in the low micromolar range (4.0–16.5 µM).

N-(4-hydroxyphenyl)-3,4,5-trimethoxybenzamide derivatives interact with acetylcholinesterase by forming conserved hydrophobic interactions with the catalytic triad (HIS447), catalytic anionic site (TRP86, TYR337, PHE338), and peripheral anionic site (TYR72, TYR124, TRP286, TYR341) as shown by molecular docking and simulation studies.
In the extracellular space of rat brain, [11C]-homovanillic acid is the main component and its radioactivity increases with time, indicating rapid formation and slow elimination.

5-(4-ethoxyphenyl)-1-(3,4,5-trimethoxyphenyl)-1H-1,2,4-triazol-3-amine (YAN) is a poor substrate of P-glycoprotein (P-gp) and multidrug resistance-associated protein 1 (MRP1), which are over-expressed in A549/Taxol cells, and inhibits their expression and function.

5-(4-ethoxyphenyl)-1-(3,4,5-trimethoxyphenyl)-1H-1,2,4-triazol-3-amine (YAN) is a novel microtubule inhibitor that exhibits strong cytotoxicity towards both A549 and multidrug-resistant A549/Taxol lung cancer cells.

5-(4-ethoxyphenyl)-1-(3,4,5-trimethoxyphenyl)-1H-1,2,4-triazol-3-amine (YAN) overcomes multidrug resistance in lung cancer cells by suppressing P-gp and MRP1 function and inducing cell death independently of p53 and caspase.
3alpha,6beta-di-(3,4,5-trimethoxycinnamoyloxy)tropane is a tropane alkaloid aromatic ester that restores vinblastine sensitivity in multidrug-resistant KB-VI cells with an IC50 of 2.0–3.7 µM.
Methoxy derivatives of dibenzo[d,g]azecine display higher affinities for dopamine receptors than their hydroxy analogs.
Dimethoxytriazine can be used as a glycosyl donor in a two-step chemo-enzymatic route for the preparation of a-N-acetylglucosaminyl galactose derivatives.
3-methyl-N-phenethylpiperidine exhibits the greatest affinity for sigma-1 receptors among N-phenethylpiperidine derivatives.

2′-hydroxy-3,4,5,3′,4′-pentamethoxychalcone is a potent inhibitor of inducible nitric oxide synthase (iNOS) in LPS-treated BV-2 cells, with an IC50 of 1.1 µM for inhibiting nitric oxide production at concentrations below the threshold for cell toxicity.

2′-hydroxy-3,4,5,3′,4′pentamethoxychalcone exhibits potent antiinflammatory activity, including complete inhibition of nitric oxide (NO) production, reduction of iNOS protein, and downregulation of various pro-inflammatory cytokines.
2'-hydroxy-3,4,5,3',4'-pentamethoxychalcone is a potent anti-inflammatory agent with an IC50 of 1.10 µM for inhibiting lipopolysaccharide-activated BV-2 microglial cell nitric oxide production.
2-amino-3-(3,4,5-trimethoxybenzoyl)-4,5,6,7-tetrahydrothieno[b]pyridine is a molecular skeleton for tubulin polymerization inhibitors evaluated for antiproliferative activity and cell cycle effects.

The 2-ethoxycarbonyl-3-(3′,4′,5′-trimethoxyanilino)thiophene system exhibits limited tolerance for substituents on the phenyl ring at the 5-position, with the phenyl ring specifically recognized by tubulin.

N-cyclopentylglycine, when combined with a phosphonate building block containing a phenyl side chain, yields a potent and selective DPP II inhibitor with an IC50 of 3.8 µM and high selectivity over DPP IV (SI much higher than 33).

(S)-cyclopentylglycine is incorporated as a key pharmacophore element in the design of potent melanocortin receptor ligands, as seen in the antagonist GXH-38B-c[Gly-Cpg-d-Nal(2')-Arg-Trp-Glu]-Val-Val-Gly-NH(2), which acts as a potent antagonist at the human MC1 receptor (K(i) = 53 nM).
Replacement of proline by 2-piperidylglycine in a nonapeptide substrate for HIV proteinase converts the substrate into a selective inhibitor.
Cyclopentyl-L-aspartic acid can be synthesized in two steps from inexpensive starting materials and catalyst, providing a more efficient route compared to previously reported multistep syntheses.
4-aminocyclohexylglycine pyrrolidide serves as a lead structure for the development of DP-IV inhibitors with modified activity profiles.

Benzylaminocyclopentane structures act as potent ±²-D-galactosidase inhibitors, with their potency comparable to D-galactopyranosylamines with N-benzyl or N-heptyl substituents, although assay conditions and stability of N-alkyl derivatives in aqueous solution differ.
Cyclopentylbenzylamine (11c) exhibits a 4-6-fold increase in binding affinity to the human MC4R compared to the parent benzylamine (11a), with a K i of 380 nM.

Ethylenediamine-N,N'-diacetic acid (EDDA) is used as a co-ligand for HYNIC-based bifunctional chelators, resulting in a (99m)Tc-RC160/EDDA complex with the highest level of renal excretion and lowest blood levels among tested conjugates.
Cyclohexylcarbamoylphosphonic acid is most active against MMP-1 and shows significant selectivity among the MMP family.
Potassium hydroxymethylglycine (KHG) is a substrate for aldolase, exhibiting a sharp pH optimum at pH 8.8 for aldolytic cleavage and a Km of 1.0 × 10⁻⁴ M for DL-KHG at 37°C.

Proteolytic enzyme activity against S-Glu increases over the 70 days of cheese ripening, with substantially higher activity in cheese juice from L. lactis AM2 compared to L. lactis HP and L. lactis 303.

Cyclopent-2-ene-1-acetic acid acts as an alkene substrate that inhibits both anodic and cathodic luminol chemiluminescence emissions during electrocatalytic oxidation of Fe(III)TMPyP, indicating its role in catalytic oxidation of alkene.
Alkyl-, aryl-, alkylaryl-, and cycloalkyl-2-substituted α-carboxycyclopropylglycines have been designed as selective mGluR2 agonists and antagonists.

(2S,3S,4S)-alpha-carboxycyclopropyl-glycine is a selective agonist of group 2 metabotropic glutamate receptors (mGluR2/3) and does not potentiate NMDA receptor responses in mouse cortical wedges at concentrations up to 30–30 µM.

The depression of spontaneous motor behavior evoked by (2S,3S,4S)-alpha-carboxycyclopropyl-glycine in rats is likely mediated by activation of group II metabotropic glutamate receptors, as group I activation stimulates motor behavior and group III activation has no effect.

(2S,3S,4S)-alpha-carboxycyclopropyl-glycine is a preferential group II metabotropic glutamate receptor agonist that reduces spontaneous sniffing activity in rats but does not affect spontaneous locomotor or exploratory behavior in an open field with a hole-board.

Intrastriatal infusion of (2S,3S,4S)-alpha-carboxycyclopropyl-glycine at 15 and 30 nmol/0.5 µL further depresses spontaneous motor behavior in rats pretreated with the dopamine-D1 receptor antagonist (-)-trans-6,7,7a,8,9,13b-hexahydro-3-chloro-2-hydroxy-N-methyl-5H-benzo[d]naphtho-(2,1-b)azepine, but not when pretreated with the dopamine-D2 receptor antagonist haloperidol.
N-(carboxymethyl)serine is a product of nonenzymatic modification of phosphatidylserine, formed via reaction with products of carbohydrate (glycoxidation) or lipid (lipoxidation) oxidation.
The formation of N-(carboxymethyl)serine from phosphatidylserine is dependent on the presence of oxygen during incubation with glucose.
N-(carboxymethyl)serine can be quantified in lipid extracts of human red blood cell membranes following incubation with glucose.

Kansuinin D is a jatrophane-type diterpene isolated from the root of Euphorbia kansui that shows no induction of cell cleavage arrest in Xenopus cells at the blastular stage at concentrations up to 0.5 µg/ml.
Scutebarbatine W is a neo-clerodane diterpene isolated from Scutellaria barbata that efficiently inhibits nitric oxide production in BV2 microglial cells with an IC50 value lower than 50 µM.

Ryanodyl 3-(pyridine-3-carboxylate) is essentially inactive compared to ryanodine for insecticidal activity against Musca domestica adults and Tribolium castaneum larvae, for toxicity to mice, and for competition with [3H]ryanodine at the Ca(2+)-ryanodine receptor complex of skeletal muscle.

Kansuiphorin C is a diterpenoid isolated from the 95% EtOH extract of E. kansui roots and identified as a compound with inflammatory cell stimulating activity in in vitro models using exoteric mice splenic lymphocytes and rat peritoneal macrophages.
11alpha-O-benzoyl-12beta-O-acetyltenacigenin B induces apoptosis in Burkitt lymphoma cells by down-regulating BCL2 and BCL-XL and up-regulating BID.
1-deoxypaclitaxel is a tricyclic intermediate that can be considered adequately functionalized for conversion to paclitaxel analogs.

1-deoxypaclitaxel can be synthesized via a six-step route from a diketone precursor, achieving an 18% overall yield through transformations including stereoselective alpha-ketol generation, an EtAlCl2-catalyzed transannular hydride shift, regioselective monomesylation, and a Wagner-Meerwein 1,2-shift.
1-deoxypaclitaxel analogues can be synthesized from the naturally occurring compound 1-deoxybaccatin VI via selective deacylation at specific acetyl positions.

1-deoxypaclitaxel derivatives can be synthesized from baccatin VI by selective deacylation and C-13 side chain attachment, and some analogues exhibit tubulin polymerization and cytotoxicity activities comparable to paclitaxel.
7-hexanoyltaxol (QP2) is a taxane analogue delivered via a drug-delivery stent to inhibit neointimal tissue growth in native coronary lesions.
10-deacetyl-7-xylosyl paclitaxel possesses similar activity to paclitaxel in a tubulin depolymerization assay.
2',3',5'-tri-O-benzoyl-azauridine is used as a starting material for the preparation of the 4-thio derivative of azacytidine via reaction with phosphorus pentasulfide.
Phyllanthoside is a natural product identified as a novel inhibitor of eukaryotic protein synthesis, specifically interfering with translation elongation and functioning in vivo and in vitro.
norcaesalpinin D is a newly isolated norcaesalpin diterpene from Caesalpinia crista seed kernels with reported antimalarial activity against Plasmodium falciparum FCR-3/A2 in vitro.
(+)-cocaine base can be rapidly and specifically separated from other cocaine stereoisomers using capillary electrophoresis.
Orbiculin D, a sesquiterpene polyester from Celastrus orbiculatus with furoyloxy groups at C-6 and C-9, displays moderate inhibition of NF-jB activation with an IC50 of 36.7 lM.
Paclitaxel levels in suspension cultures of Taxus cuspidata cv Densiformis can range from 0 to 149 µg/L over a 4-week period.
Nicotinoyl azide is a small molecule that, upon light activation, enables measurement of solvent accessibility of purine nucleobases in RNA.
Epoxykinamycin undergoes epoxy ring opening catalyzed by the epoxy hydrolase Alp1U in the kinamycin biosynthetic pathway.
Hispidanin B is an asymmetric dimeric diterpenoid isolated from Isodon hispida rhizomes.

2-[2-Benzylidenehydrazinyl]-N-(5-oxo-4,5-dihydro-1Hpyrazol-3-yl)acetamide is evaluated for antimycobacterial activity against Mycobacterium tuberculosis using the MABA (Marlborough Antibacterial Activity) assay, with MIC determined by the lowest concentration preventing color change from blue to pink after 5 days of incubation at 37°C.
Benzaldehyde thiosemicarbazone coordinates to ruthenium and osmium through the hydrazinic nitrogen and sulfur, forming a four-membered chelate ring with a bite angle of approximately 67 degrees.
Benzaldehyde thiosemicarbazone ligands can form four-membered chelate rings with ruthenium, as observed in structurally characterized complexes.
Benzaldehyde thiosemicarbazone can form coordination complexes with ruthenium and osmium, exhibiting unusual coordination modes depending on experimental conditions and substituent effects.
Benzaldehyde thiosemicarbazone is less effective than 2,3-butanedione oxime thiosemicarbazone against intranasally induced vaccinia infection in mice.
Benzaldehyde-S-benzylisothiosemicarbazones represent a new group of antimycobacterial compounds featuring an alkylsulfanyl functional group as the pharmacophore.

5-(benzylideneamino)-3-(4-methoxyphenylamino)-N-(4-methylphenyl)-1H-pyrazole-4-carboxamide is a Schiff base derivative bearing a pyrazole moiety that displays potent antitumor activity against MCF-7 cancer cell lines.
Analogues of 2-styryl-3,5-dihydro-4H-imidazol-4-one act as reactive oxygen species (ROS) inducers, leading to increased oxidative stress and apoptosis in cancer cells.

2-styryl-3,5-dihydro-4H-imidazol-4-one is a pharmacophore scaffold that can be structurally modified to yield analogues with potent cytotoxic and antiproliferative activity against cancer cell lines, including selective activity at low micromolar to sub-micromolar IC50 values.
N'-(4-methylbenzylidene)-5-phenylisoxazole-3-carbohydrazide is a potent, reversible, and competitive inhibitor of MAO-B, with a docking score of -10.98 kcal/mol for MAO-B binding.

N'-(4-methylbenzylidene)-5-phenylisoxazole-3-carbohydrazide significantly prevents MPTP-induced neurotoxicity in a mouse model of Parkinson's disease, as shown by improved gait behavior in the footprint test and increased grip strength in the horizontal wire test.
The cytotoxic effects of Belinostat correlate with histone H4 acetylation.
Belinostat is a potent histone deacetylase inhibitor (HDACi) with an IC50 of 27 nM.
Belinostat exhibits cytotoxicity against a wide panel of tumor cell lines with IC50 values ranging from 0.2 to 3.4 μM.
4-amino-3-methyl-N-(4-(4-methyl-6-oxo-1,4,5,6-tetrahydropyridazin-3-yl) phenyl)benzamide exhibits cardiotonic activity in Straub's perfusion method compared to levosimendan.
Phenylbutyrylglycine is a pharmacologically active metabolite whose pharmacokinetics (PK) can be measured in subjects administered sodium phenylacetate and phenylbutyric acid.
6-([18F]fluoroacetamido)-1-hexanoicanilide ([18F]FAHA) is rapidly metabolized to [18F]FACE in blood.

6-([18F]fluoroacetamido)-1-hexanoicanilide ([18F]FAHA) specifically interacts with HDAC 4 and 5 in HDAC IIa-rich brain regions such as the nucleus accumbens, amygdala, hippocampus, and periaqueductal grey matter.
6-([18F]fluoroacetamido)-1-hexanoicanilide ([18F]FAHA) is a PET imaging agent and substrate for histone deacetylase (HDAC) used to image whole body HDAC expression and tumor activity.
6-([18F]fluoroacetamido)-1-hexanoicanilide ([18F]FAHA) demonstrates HDAC activity in the rat brain, with peak uptake of 0.44%ID/g.

Bismerthiazol exposure during development is likely a thyroid disruptor in female rats, as evidenced by decreased serum triiodothyronine (T3) and thyroxine (T4) concentrations, increased thyroid stimulating hormone (TSH) concentrations, and thyroid gland hyperplasia.

Triazine thione forms complexes with Ag, Pd, and Pt that can interact with BSA (bovine serum albumin) through electrostatic and hydrophobic interactions, as well as potentially through electrostatic interactions in the case of Pt complexes.
Thiazolo[4,5-d]thiazolidine-2-thione serves as a scaffold for the synthesis of pyrazoline derivatives with demonstrated cytotoxicity and multitargeting kinase inhibitory activity.
5-(pyridine-4-yl)-1,3,4-oxadiazole-2(3H)-thione serves as a core structure for the synthesis of oxadiazole derivatives evaluated as antimycobacterial agents.
5-(pyridin-4-yl)-3H-1,3,4-oxadiazol-2-thione derivatives may interact at the active site of mycobacterial cytochrome P450-dependent sterol 14demethylase in the sterol biosynthesis pathway.
5-(pyridin-4-yl)-3H-1,3,4-oxadiazol-2-thione derivatives exhibit in vitro antimycobacterial activity against M. tuberculosis H 37 Rv strain.
5-(pyridin-4-yl)-3H-1,3,4-oxadiazol-2-thione derivatives have been synthesized and tested for in vitro antimycobacterial activity against M. tuberculosis H37Rv.
2,4-dihydro-3H [1,2,4]triazole-3-thione derivatives with a shorter connecting chain exhibit lower affinity for the 5-HT 1A receptor compared to their structural isomers.
2,4-dihydro-3H-[1,2,4]triazole-3-thione derivatives have been tested for binding affinity and selectivity at the 5-HT 1A receptor and the α1-adrenoceptor.
2,4-dihydro-3H [1,2,4]triazole-3-thione derivatives with a shorter connecting chain show increased affinity for alpha-1 adrenoceptor sites compared to their structural isomers.
3-amino-1H-pyrazolo(3,4-c)pyridazine forms supramolecular hydrogen-bonded 3D networks in the crystal structures of organic compounds.

The pyrazolo[3,4-c]pyridazin-3-amine scaffold is capable of being enclosed and binding within EGFR and CDK-2 receptors through various hydrophilic interactions, as demonstrated by docking simulations.
The pyrazolo[3,4-c]pyridazin-3-amine scaffold can be accommodated by and bind to EGFR and CDK-2 receptors through various hydrophilic interactions, as shown by docking simulations.
Changing the chelating ligand from azopyridine to azopyrazole in chlorido complexes increases chloride lability and decreases arene lability in aqueous solution.

Azopyrazole ligands, when introduced into chlorido Ru(II) arene complexes, result in highly colored complexes with reduced hydrolysis rates, arene loss as a competing reaction, decreased pKa* of coordinated water, and lower DNA affinity compared to diamine chelators such as en.
Despite these changes in ligand properties, azopyrazole-containing Ru(II) arene complexes still exhibit moderate cytotoxicity against A2780 and A549 cancer cells.

Pyrazole-carboxylic-acid hydrazide compounds are potent DNA polymerase IIIC inhibitors with antibacterial activity, lacking heterocyclic nitrogenous bases but mimicking or competing with dNTPs to inhibit DNA pol IIIC.

Ethylenethiourea, a metabolite of mancozeb, induces a 1.8-fold increase in reporter enzyme activity in PC72 worms at 10% of the optimal mancozeb dose (50 lg mL‫מ‬), which is statistically significant (p ‫ס‬ 0.011).
2-pyridylamino,5-pyridylthiazoles are a novel category of ATP-competitive Chk1 inhibitors with outstanding inhibitory potential.
The hydrazone derivative 3c bearing a 2-oxo-5-thiono-1,3-thiazolidine moiety is the most potent compound in the series for leishmanicidal activity.
4-methoxy-5-propylbiphenyl-2-ol is a honokiol analog that functions as a positive allosteric modulator of GABA-A receptors, enhancing GABA-induced currents in a concentration-dependent manner.

4-methoxy-5-propylbiphenyl-2-ol is a potent ago-allosteric modulator of the GABA A receptor (a1·······b2·······c2 isoform) expressed in Xenopus oocytes, potentiating GABA currents by up to 5000% at 10 µM.
5-hexyl-4-methoxybiphenyl-2-ol is a honokiol analog that functions as a positive allosteric modulator of GABA-A receptors, potentiating GABA-induced currents in a concentration-dependent manner.
The number of sorption isotherms for 4-ethyl-2-phenylphenol onto various biochars is generally inversely correlated with the CO2-SA/OC ratio, supporting the role of pore-filling in its adsorption.

The sorption of 4-ethyl-2-phenylphenol onto wood-derived biochars (WDBs) shows a positive correlation between logKoc and the CO2-SA/OC ratio, indicating that pore-filling is a significant mechanism for its adsorption.

4-ethyl-2-phenylphenol acts as an inducer of CYP102 in Bacillus megaterium, and its induction is additive with that of 100 lM E2 at the same concentration and time points, indicating action at a different regulatory site than E2.
5-hydroxymethyl-2-hydroxy-4-methoxy-phenylethanone, isolated from Craterellus odoratus, exhibits inhibitory activity against human 11beta-HSD1 with an IC50 value of 16.4 µg/mL.

5-hydroxymethyl-2-hydroxy-4-methoxy-phenylethanone is a newly isolated compound from Craterellus odoratus that has been evaluated for inhibitory activity against human 11b-hydroxysteroid dehydrogenase (11b-HSD1) and cytotoxic activity on five human cancer cell lines.
LY293111 markedly inhibits pulmonary inflammation caused by A23187 challenge at 1 hour post-exposure in guinea pigs.
LY293111 is a leukotriene B4 receptor antagonist that has recently become available for clinical evaluation.
LY293111 is a potent and selective antagonist of the human neutrophil function in vitro.
LY293111 is a selective leukotriene B4 receptor antagonist with potent pulmonary anti-inflammatory activity.
LY293111 is a potent, orally active agent that antagonizes the pulmonary actions of leukotriene B4.
8-methoxy-3-phenylcoumarin is a highly selective MAO-B inhibitor.
In the extracellular space of rat brain, [11C]-homovanillic acid is the main component and its radioactivity increases with time, indicating rapid formation and slow elimination.
5,7-dimethoxy-4-phenylcoumarin mildly but significantly reduces the formation of TNF-alpha in LPS-induced murine macrophage RAW 264.7 cells.

5,7-dimethoxy-4-phenylcoumarin, isolated from Streptomyces aureofaciens CMUAc130, exhibits antifungal activity with a minimum inhibitory concentration (MIC) of 150 µg/ml against tested phytopathogenic fungi.

5,7-dimethoxy-4-phenylcoumarin produced by Streptomyces aureofaciens CMUAc130 shows in vitro anti-inflammatory activity by modulating iNOS enzyme expression, nitric oxide formation, PGE2 release, and COX-2 expression in LPS-induced murine macrophage RAW 264.7 cells in a concentration-dependent manner.
25I-NBOMe induces a head twitch response in mice that is dose-dependently antagonized by M100,907, with an ID50 of 0.0062 mg/kg (0.0017–0.0222 mg/kg) and complete blockade at 0.1 mg/kg.

3-methoxy-4-hydroxyphenylethylethyleneglycol (MHPG) is a metabolite of norepinephrine, and its brain levels are elevated by aspartame and tyrosine administration in rat brain regions such as the hypothalamus, locus coeruleus, and brain stem.
(2R)-7-(3-[2-chloro-4-(4-fluorophenoxy)phenoxy]propoxy)-2-ethylchromane-2-carboxylic acid is a potent, structurally novel, selective PPARalpha/gamma dual agonist.

(2R)-7-(3-[2-chloro-4-(4-fluorophenoxy)phenoxy]propoxy)-2-ethylchromane-2-carboxylic acid exhibits substantial antihyperglycemic and hypolipidemic activities when orally administered in db/db mouse type 2 diabetes, Syrian hamster lipid, and dog lipid models.
LY293111 markedly inhibits pulmonary inflammation caused by A23187 challenge at 1 hour post-exposure in guinea pigs.
LY293111 is a selective leukotriene B4 receptor antagonist with potent pulmonary anti-inflammatory activity.
LY293111 is a potent, orally active agent that antagonizes the pulmonary actions of leukotriene B4.

CNBCA is an SHP2 inhibitor that suppresses cell proliferation, anchorage independent growth, and cancer stem cell properties (mammosphere formation) in breast cancer cells (BT474 and MDA-MB468) at a concentration of 500 nM.

5-[ (3-Carboxy phenyl) sulfonyl]-2-[ (6-phenylhexyl)oxy]benzenepropanoic Acid is a benzophenone dicarboxylic acid derivative with a lipophilic side chain that inhibits the binding of leukotriene B4 to its receptor on intact human neutrophils.

5-[ (3-Carboxy phenyl) sulfonyl]-2-[ (6-phenylhexyl)oxy]benzenepropanoic Acid derivatives with alkyl chains terminated with an aromatic ring show improved inhibition of [3H]LTB4 binding to the LTB4 receptor on intact human neutrophils.

5-[ (3-Carboxy phenyl) sulfonyl]-2-[ (6-phenylhexyl)oxy]benzenepropanoic Acid is a lipophilic benzophenone dicarboxylic acid derivative that inhibits the binding of leukotriene B4 (LTB4) to its receptor on intact human neutrophils.
4-methoxy-5-propylbiphenyl-2-ol is a honokiol analog that functions as a positive allosteric modulator of GABA-A receptors, enhancing GABA-induced currents in a concentration-dependent manner.

4-methoxy-5-propylbiphenyl-2-ol is a potent ago-allosteric modulator of the GABA A receptor (a1·······b2·······c2 isoform) expressed in Xenopus oocytes, potentiating GABA currents by up to 5000% at 10 µM.

(S)-2-(3-((4-(1-adamantyl)benzamido)methyl)-4-(butoxy)benzyl)butanoic acid binds to hPPARγ LBD in a conformation where its acidic head and hydrophobic tail overlap well, but the central benzene ring is flipped and the butoxy group lies in the opposite direction compared to structurally similar compounds bound to hPPARα LBD and hPPARδ LBD.

5-(3-[ 18 F]-fluoropropyloxy)-L-tryptophan ([ 18 F]-L-FPTP) is a [ 18 F]-labeled 5-hydroxy-L-tryptophan tracer developed for clinical amino acid PET imaging, primarily transported by LAT2 in tumor cells, and shows high tumor uptake in model mice.
7-phenoxy-4-imidazolylmethylcoumarin derivatives exhibit very high selectivity for CYP19 (aromatase) over CYP17 (17β-dehydrogenase).
A validated LC-MS/MS method allows for simultaneous quantification of 5-hydroxy pioglitazone in human plasma over the concentration range of 6.01–1496.28 ng/mL.
5-hydroxy pioglitazone can be quantitatively measured in human plasma using LC-MS/MS with a linearity range of 6.01–1496.28 ng/mL and a mean extraction recovery of 96.13% from spiked plasma.

5-hydroxy pioglitazone is a metabolite of pioglitazone and can be quantitatively determined in human plasma using LC-MS/MS, with method validation parameters such as selectivity, linearity, accuracy, precision, matrix effect, and stability.
N,N-dipropyl-2-[4-methoxy-3-(2-phenylethoxyl)phenyl]ethylamine (NE 100) is a highly selective σ 1 receptor ligand.

2-n-amyloxybenzamide exhibits fungistatic activity and is more active than N-n-butyl-3-phenylsalicylamide, undecylenic acid, salicylanilide, Nystatin, phenylmercuric acetate, sodium ethylmercurithiosalicylate, and 8-hydroxyquinoline.
8-Pentyloxy-5,6-dihydro-4H-[1, 2, 4]triazolo [4,3-a] [1]benzazepin-1-one demonstrates a high anticonvulsant potency relative to carbamazepine, as its PI is much greater than that of carbamazepine.
Benzenesulfonyl-prolyl-phenylalanine (Pro-Phe) is a scaffold incorporated into ␣ 4 ␤ 1 inhibitors for the inhibition of platelet adhesion.
1-(D-3-acetylthio-2-methylpropanoyl)-L-prolyl-L-phenylalanine is a novel orally active angiotensin converting enzyme inhibitor.
1-(D-3-acetylthio-2-methylpropanoyl)-L-prolyl-L-phenylalanine (DU-1219) is a new antihypertensive agent.
Suc-L-(Ala)2-Pro-Phe-pNa is a substrate that is used to demonstrate the predominance of Penaeide chymotrypsin activity in Penaeus vannamei.
N-tetrahydrofuroyl-(L)-phenylalanine is a derivative that acts as a potent VLA-4 antagonist with good oral bioavailability.
Tetrahydrofuroyl-l-phenylalanine derivatives have been studied as VLA-4 antagonists using 3-D-QSAR (CoMFA and CoMSIA) models.

Tetrahydrofuroyl-l-phenylalanine derivatives can be modeled using 3-D-QSAR approaches to predict biological activity, with steric and electrostatic properties influencing activity as indicated by contour plots.
Tetrahydrofuroyl-l-phenylalanine derivatives act as VLA-4 antagonists and can be studied for activity using 3-D-QSAR models such as CoMFA and CoMSIA.

4-TAPAP binds to thrombin in a manner similar to argatroban, DAPA, and RWJ-50215, with the P1 arginine CA displaced from the PPACK-thrombin position by 4.1 Å and forming a three-center hydrogen-bonded ion pair in the SI site involving NH1 interacting with both ODI and OD2 of Asp189.
The memory-enhancing effect of rubiscolin-6 is blocked by the ␦ opioid antagonist naltrindole.
The memory-enhancing effect of rubiscolin-6 is mediated via the opioid receptor, as it is blocked by the opioid antagonist naltrindole.

Rubiscolin-6 is a Rubisco-derived opioid peptide that enhances memory consolidation after intracerebroventricular administration, with the optimal dose for memory consolidation being 3 nmol/mouse and being dose-dependent and bell-shaped.
Rubiscolin-6 enhances memory consolidation in mice as assessed by a one-trial step-through passive avoidance test.

5-benzamido-4-oxo-6-phenylhexanoyl-L-prolyl-L-alanine is a potent synthetic inhibitor of collagenase from Clostridium histolyticum, with a viscometric IC50 of 120 pM and a spectrophotometric IC50 of 16 pM.

5-benzamido-4-oxo-6-phenylhexanoyl-L-prolyl-L-alanine binds to collagenase from Clostridium histolyticum with more than 80-fold greater affinity than its peptide analogue benzoyl-L-phenylalanylglycyl-L-prolyl-L-alanine.
Bz-Gly-His-Pro-Phe is a tripeptidyl substrate that is hydrolyzed by negligible amounts by hog lung and kidney kininase II.

N-(N-hydroxysulfamoyl)phenylalanine is a transition state analogue inhibitor of carboxypeptidase A, binding in a manner reminiscent of a transition state and exhibiting a competitive inhibition with a Ki in the micromolar range.
BSH distributes boron throughout the cell in a nearly homogeneous pattern in glioblastoma cells, with cellular uptake being similar after 1 hour and 6 hours of exposure.
Cellular uptake of boron from BSH in glioblastoma cells occurs via passive diffusion from the nutrient medium into the cell.
Prolyl-phenylalanylamide derivatives with N-methylbenzylamide substitutions retain activity on the rabbit NK 1 receptor.
Avian spermatozoa contain comparatively high concentrations of 22:4n-6 fatty acids within their phospholipids.
18:4, n-3 is produced by ⌬ 6 -desaturation of 18:3, n-3 in yeast.
In yeast expressing borage A6 desaturase, octadecatetraenoic acid (CI8 OTA) is converted to linoleic acid (LA), accounting for over 40% of the exogenously supplied octadecatetraenoic acid.
In transgenic tobacco plants expressing borage ⌬ 6 -desaturase, octadecatetraenoic acid (OTA; 18:4 6,9,12,15) accumulates as a result of the enzyme's activity.
Octadecenoic and octadecadienoic acid exhibit differential expression in the local ablation versus palliative care group in HCC patients.
Octadecenoic and octadecadienoic acid display differential expression in hepatocellular carcinoma (HCC) patients undergoing local ablation versus palliative care.

7-HDPA is a n-3 DPA product that is elevated systemically during acute inflammation in vivo and is predominantly produced via lipoxygenase-mediated oxygenation with S-configuration of the hydroxy group.

7-Hydroxydocosapentaenoic acid is a novel n-3 DPA-derived product whose biosynthesis in vivo results in a ratio of approximately 20% R isomer to 80% S isomer, as determined by chiral metabololipidomic profiling in mouse plasma.
7-HDPA is a novel oxygenated product of n-3 DPA produced during acute inflammation, and its plasma concentration rises following ischemia reperfusion injury in mice.

7-OH-DPA accumulates to significantly greater levels in the fish oil group before and after calcium ionophore stimulation, indicating less efficient further oxidation to the bioactive a,b-unsaturated ketone product compared to 5-OH-EPA and 7-OH-DHA.
A 15-hydroxyeicosatrienoic acid-substituted diacylglycerol (DAG) will inhibit PKC-b.
15-hydroxy-eicosatrienoic acid (15-HETre) is a metabolite of dihomogammalinolenic acid (DGLA) and inhibits the formation of leukotriene B4 and 20-hydroxy-leukotriene B4 in vitro.
15-HETE is generated in cytosol from both transfected and untransfected HEK 293 cells by 8-LOX, and is produced as a racemic mixture.
7,8-dihydroxyeicosapentaenoic acid (7,8-diHETE) is released directly from galactolipids as part of the biosynthetic pathway of defense metabolites in G. chilensis.
Plasma levels of 7,8-DiHDPA decrease significantly during CPB compared to pre-CPB, suggesting reduced production or increased clearance of 7,8-DiHDPA during CPB.
15-HPDA is an aromatic tertiary hydroperoxide that can induce cross-reactive immune responses when coupled to allylic tertiary hydroperoxides like 15-HPA in sensitized animals.
15-Hydroperoxydocosatetraenoic acid can generate alkoxy radicals upon homolytic cleavage, which may react with skin proteins to form antigens.

12R-hydroperoxy-octadecatrienoic acid is a hydroperoxy fatty acid that is a substrate in the jasmonate biosynthetic pathway in plants, being converted to cyclopentenone by allene oxide cyclase (AOC) following enzymatic dehydration by 13S-lipoxygenase (13S-LOX) and P450-type AOS.
17(S)-HPDP is a pentaenoic acid hydroperoxide that generates allene oxides that are enzymatically cyclized by corn or potato AOC to form cyclopentenones.
15-Hydroxyeicosatetraenoic acid acetate is a more potent stimulator of 5-lipoxygenase activity than the free 15-hydroxyl group of 15-HETE.
Vadadustat is a prolyl hydroxylase inhibitor that has demonstrated therapeutic utility in treating anemia associated with chronic kidney disease in a non-dialysis population.
3-chloro-2′,5′dihydroxybiphenyl displays moderate 8-oxodG-forming activity among the para dihydroxy metabolites in a nonenzymatic Cu(II) system.
3-chlorobenzyloxyserine derivatives show high inhibitory potency against MAO B, ranking among the most potent compounds in the series.

Dietary administration of CIPC in rats is associated with reversible changes such as increased liver and kidney weight, minor liver pathology, and reversible splenomegaly and congestion of the red pulp, with pathological changes in the liver and splenic fibrosis resolving within 10 weeks after cessation of administration.
GW427353 (solabegron) is a novel, selective ␤ 3-adrenergic receptor (AR) agonist investigated for its effects on bladder function in dogs.
Solabegron is a β 3-adrenoceptor agonist that is in clinical development for the treatment of voiding disorders.
p-Fluorophenylacetylglycine (M-4), a major metabolite of the butyrophenone chain of timiperone, does not show affinity for dopamine receptors.
Sulphation of N-OH-2ABP is observed in human tissues throughout fetal, neonatal, and adult life, and the resulting sulphate esters are reactive compounds capable of inducing DNA lesions.
Hippuric-histidine-leucine is used as a substrate to evaluate angiotensin-converting enzyme (ACE) activity by measuring its hydrolysis in a modified assay.
2-(3-chloro-4-hydroxyphenyl)-N-phenethylacetamide is among the most active compounds in the series as an inhibitor of 17β-HSD2, sharing a similar interaction pattern with other active analogs.

Thiorphan is a less effective inhibitor of the generation of C-terminal fragments from NOFQ biotransformation by enzyme activity isolated from morphine-naive cells and SH-SY5Y cells compared to more potent inhibitors such as PMSF, PHMB, EDTA, Zn2+, Cu2+, and Hg2+.
Thiorphan is an enkephalin-degrading peptidase inhibitor that produces only modest or no prolongation of reaction latencies in the rat tail-flick test for analgesic action.
5-tert-butyl-6-chloro-N-benzylpyrazine-2-carboxamide inhibits photosynthesis in spinach chloroplasts with an IC50 of 7.4 µmol/L.
5-tert-butyl-6-chloro-N-benzylpyrazine-2-carboxamide shows in vitro antimycobacterial activity against M. tuberculosis with a MIC of 6.25 µg/mL.
3HB-GGG was used as a substrate at 3.4 mg/ml in inhibition assays with ACE and aminoacylase at 0.2 U/ml and 172 kU/ml, respectively, and incubated at 37°C for 30 minutes.

(±)-(R*,R*)- [4-[2-[[2-(3-chlorophenyl) -2hydroxyethyl]amino]propyl]phenoxy] acetic acid sodium hydrate (BRL37344) is a β 3-adrenoceptor agonist that inhibits detrusor smooth muscle myogenic contractions and increases intracellular cAMP levels in native and heterologously expressed detrusor smooth muscle tissues.

The BRL37344-induced hyperpolarization of freshly isolated detrusor smooth muscle cells is eliminated by iberiotoxin or ryanodine, indicating involvement of BK channels and ryanodine receptors in its mechanism of action.
BRL37344 is a β3-adrenergic receptor agonist that induces relaxation of human and rodent detrusor smooth muscle in both spontaneous, nerve-evoked, and pharmacologically-induced contractions.

Chloromethyl nicotinamide is an active metabolite produced from the active principle NA specifically in wounded C. elegans, and it confers better healing ability by inducing new collagen synthesis through inhibition of GSK-3 and activation of the Wnt/β-catenin pathway.
2,3,5,5'-tetrachlorobiphenyl is a dechlorination product of Aroclor 1260 in sediment-free enrichment cultures, with over 50% dechlorination of both stereoisomers observed.
6,7-dimethyl quinoline-5,8-dione is inactive as a bioalkylating agent.
6,7-Dimethylquinoline-5,8-dione, a precursor of 6,7-Bis(bromomethyl)quinoline-5,8-dione, lacks alkylating side chains and is inactive in antitumor activity assays.
DMN serves as an electron acceptor for hydrogenase activity assays in bacterial cell fractions.
The reduction of 2,3-dimethyl-1,4-naphthoquinone by the T. maritima hydrogenase K-subunit requires the presence of a 23-kDa subunit, which serves as a hydrophilic equivalent of menaquinone.

Ruthenium(iii) terpyridine complexes, such as those with 4'-chloro-terpyridine or 4'-chlorophenyl-terpyridine as ligands, exhibit slightly better binding affinity to calf thymus DNA compared to bis-pyrazolylpyridine complexes.
2-methyl-3-pentadecyl-1,4-naphthoquinone is used as an internal standard to improve the accuracy of vitamin K homologue determination.
2-methyl-3-pentadecyl-1,4-naphthoquinone is used as an internal standard at a concentration of 3.8 ng/mL in ethanol for plasma sample preparation.
2-methyl-3-pentadecyl-1,4-naphthoquinone is used as an internal standard in the post-column photoproduct (PO) detection of vitamin K homologues in human plasma.
2-methyl-3-pentadecyl-1,4-naphthoquinone is used as an internal standard in plasma vitamin K determination due to its identical chemical properties with vitamin K homologues.
2-methyl-3-pentadecyl-1,4naphthoquinone is used as an internal standard at a concentration of approximately 5 nM in plasma sample preparation for analytical assays.

2-bromo-3-methylpyridine is used to generate a 3-unsubstituted 2-pyridyl cuprate that successfully participates in conjugate addition reactions with various Michael acceptors, including (E)-methyl crotonate, methyl tiglate, (E)-3-penten-2-one, and (E)-crotononitrile, yielding 70%, 10%, 14%, 85%, and 81% respectively.
Substitution of the 2-OH group of pyrimidine with a 2-methyl group confers resistance to nuclease attack in aptamers.

2-methylpyrimidine derivatives with a 4-halobenzylamino group show stronger PET inhibitory activity than atrazine and 1,3,5-triazines, and their activity is about 10 times stronger than that of unsubstituted benzylaminopyrimidines.
2-methylpyrimidine derivatives show high PET inhibitory activity and high herbicidal activity in both pre-emergence paddy and field tests.
5C-aglycone can be used as a synthetic standard to assess vitamin K moiety recovery during solid-phase extraction (SPE) procedures for urinary metabolite analysis.
Increased excretion of 5C-aglycone after K3 supplementation is likely due to its conversion to MK-4 via the addition of a geranyl-geranyl side chain.
The on-column lower limit of detection for 5C-aglycone in urine by HPLC-ECD is 3.5 fmol (Ͻ 1 pg), defined as 5 ϫ baseline noise.

2-methyl-3-(3 Ј -3 Ј -carboxymethylpropyl)-1,4-naphthoquinone (5C-aglycone) is a major aglycone metabolite of vitamin K1 in humans, with a side chain length of 5 carbon atoms, and is excreted as a water-soluble glucuronide conjugate.

Thioxopyrazolo[3,4-b]pyridine serves as a promising cytotoxic scaffold with significant selectivity indices against normal cells, as demonstrated by analogues showing lower IC50 values in human lung fibroblast (WI-38) cells compared to cancer cells.
The MLCT [Pt3p*(5-mphen)] transition for [Pt(5-mphen)(CN)2] occurs at 347 nm with an extinction maximum of 1.48 × 10^3 mol·1 dm^3·cm⁻¹ in acetonitrile.

2-O-octyl-L-ascorbic acid is a monosubstituted ascorbic acid derivative that shows significant antigen-stimulated degranulation inhibitory activity in RBL-2H3 cells, with its activity depending on alkyl chain length.
6-O-phenylascorbic acid reduces the NO moiety of spin-labeled steroid analogs on the outer leaflet of liposomes with a half-time of approximately 30 seconds.
6-O-phenylascorbic acid can slowly permeate erythrocyte membranes, leading to reduction of spin-labeled phospholipids on both the inner and outer leaflets after prolonged incubation.
6-O-phenylascorbic acid reduces spin-labeled phospholipids in erythrocyte membranes, with approximately 55% of analogs remaining shielded from reduction after 600 s incubation at 4°C.
6-O-phenylascorbic acid permeates cell membranes slowly, resulting in its spin label reduction occurring only on the outer leaflet during membrane experiments.
6-benzyloxysalicylate derivatives are h15-LOX-1 inhibitors with modest potency of 7100 nM.
6-benzyloxysalicylate is a structurally novel, competitive inhibitor of h-15-LOX-1 with a Ki value of 1.7 mM.
6-benzyloxysalicylate acts as a competitive inhibitor of h-15-LOX-1.

Tetraethylene glycol monobenzyl ether can be used as a reactant in the transesterification with polyethylene glycols to produce halo-ester-functionalized poly(ethylene glycol)s under solventless conditions using Candida antarctica lipase B as a biocatalyst.
N,N',N''-tribenzyloxyisocyanuric acid is synthesized from 1-(N-benzyloxycarbamoyl) benzotriazole via thermal decomposition in the presence of a catalytic amount of imidazole.
3-Hydroxybenzyl phenyl ether (3-OHBPA) induces cytochrome c release from Jurkat cells upon UVB irradiation at 100 and 1000 J/cm² for 12 hours at 50 lM concentration.
5-benzyloxyisoflavone analogs exhibit lower inhibitory activity against IL-5 bioactivity compared to their cyclohexylmethoxy analogs.

Benzyl carbonate derivatives (compounds 5a–5c) have been designed as fumagillin analogues to optimize hydrophobic interactions and van der Waals contacts with the hydrophobic valley and pocket of human MetAP-2.
Benzyl carbonate prodrugs of paclitaxel (1a-b and 1d-i) do not exhibit much reduced cytotoxicity under aerobic conditions.
5-benzyloxyisoflavone analogs exhibit lower inhibitory activity against IL-5 bioactivity compared to their cyclohexylmethoxy analogs.
The substitution pattern at the meta or para position of 7-benzyloxychromone derivatives has little effect on their MAO-B inhibitory potency.

7-benzyloxychromone derivatives with meta, para, or trifluoromethyl substitution on the benzyloxy phenyl ring are approximately two- to sixfold more potent MAO-B inhibitors than 7-benzyloxychromone (IC50 values ranging from 0.014 to 0.044 µM).
7-benzyloxychromone is a potent inhibitor of human MAO-B, exhibiting an IC50 value of 0.085 µM.
7-benzyloxychromone derivatives exhibit moderate inhibition of MAO-B, with the IC50 value for MAO-B inhibition being approximately 0.018 µM.

7-benzyloxychromone is a highly potent reversible, competitive inhibitor of human MAO-B, with all tested substitutions and substitution patterns on the benzyloxy ring enhancing its MAO-B inhibition potency.
1-(3-chlorophenyl)piperazine hydrochloride is a preferential 5-HT 2C receptor agonist, but generally lacks selectivity over other 5-HT receptors.
The K i of 1-(3-chlorophenyl)piperazine hydrochloride for 5-HT 2C receptors decreased from 523 Ϯ 81 to 64 Ϯ 12 nM in the presence of PNU-69176E at 10 M, indicating a 7-fold reduction in affinity.

1-(m-chlorophenyl)-piperazine hydrochloride (mCPP) is a 5-HT 2C receptor agonist that reduces locomotor activity in normal animals but enhances locomotor activity and increases weight-supported stepping in rats with neonatal thoracic spinal cord transection at doses higher than those effective in normal animals.
Phenylpiperazine-phenylacetate derivatives are designed as rapid recovery hypnotic agents with high affinity for the GABAA receptor and low affinity for other off-target receptors.
In 4-piperidinylbenzamides, electronic properties primarily determine biological activity, as shown by QSAR studies.
1-piperazinyl-2-fluoro-9H-xanthen-9-one is a xanthone analogue with antibacterial activity, being active against Staphylococcus aureus with a MIC of 50 lg/mL.
7-hydroxy-1-naphthylpiperazine serves as the starting material for the synthesis of compounds 4a-g by alkylation with (chloromethyl)piperazides.
7-hydroxy-1-naphthylpiperazine is a structurally simple arylpiperazine that acts as a high-affinity ligand for the 5-HT 5A receptor, with a K i of 3 nM.
7-isopropoxy-4-(1-piperazinyl)coumarin is a 7-alkoxy-4-(1-piperazinyl)coumarin derivative with significantly higher antiplatelet activity than 4-(1-piperazinyl)coumarin.
TLR7/8 agonist 4 is a dual TLR7/8 agonist that contains an aliphatic primary amine and can be directly coupled with carboxylic acid groups on hemagglutinin (HA) for use as a vaccine adjuvant.
4-(4-(benzylideneamino)phenyl)piperazine analogs of triazole alcohols exhibit moderate to excellent antifungal activity against yeasts, dermatophytes, and important fungal pathogens.
7-[4-((3-hydroxy-4-oxo-4H-pyran-2-yl)methyl)piperazin-1-yl]quinolone derivatives have been synthesized and evaluated for antibacterial activity.
1-PP, a metabolite of buspirone, produces a transient decrease in blood pressure of 17% at 1.0 mg/kg during isovolumic experiments, with recovery within 5 minutes.
True blue is a fluorescent retrograde axonal tracer that acts primarily as a cytoplasmic marker and is used for retrograde labeling of neurons.
True Blue, when used as a retrograde tract tracer in monkeys, shows that CuSO4 only reduces the intensity of its specific labeling, but Sudan Black completely eliminates it.
The benzyl carbamate piperazine derivative (compound 192) is a potent inhibitor of human plasma kallikrein VII (hCA-VII) with a Ki of 11.4 nM.

2-(4-methylpiperazin-1-ylcarbonyl)-5-hydroxychromone is a piperazinylcarbonylchromone derivative designed to synergistically modulate multidrug resistance (MDR) by combining a chromone unit with a piperazine unit.

2-(4-methylpiperazin-1-ylcarbonyl)-5-hydroxychromone is a potent reversal agent of P-glycoprotein-mediated multidrug resistance, more potent than cyclosporin A, and increases intracellular accumulation of rhodamine-123 in resistant K562 cells.
Coumarinyloxyacetamide derivatives are evaluated for their inhibitory activity against acetylcholinesterase and butyrylcholinesterase using an IC50-based assay protocol.

2-nitrofuran-5-carboxamide derivatives containing basic and/or electrophilic (alkylating) side chains have been synthesized and their biological activity, including as radiosensitizers and bioreductively activated cytotoxins, has been evaluated.

Leucine methylphosphonic acid, when combined with a hydroxamic acid group, forms a compound with significant inhibitory activity against Helicoverpa armigera larval growth and digestive aminopeptidase activity.
Leu-Val dipeptides have a digestibility closer to that of Leu-Leu dipeptides than to Val-Leu dipeptides in individual forages.
Valine-leucine, when added at 1 mM, provides an equal mole of branched-chain carbon skeleton to other treatments in in vitro fiber digestion assays.
Pyloricidin B, a peptidic moiety composed of L-valine-L-leucine, exhibits potent and highly selective anti-Helicobacter pylori activity.
Monoacetyl-diaminopimelic acid is used as a substrate in transpeptidation reactions catalyzed by exocellular DD-carboxypeptidases from Streptomyces R61, K11, R39, and S. albris G.

Amino-4,4-dimethylpentanoic acid is an acidic analyte that is retained on quinine-derived chiral stationary phases (CSPs) primarily through anion-exchange retention mechanisms in aqueous mobile phases.
2-amino-4,4-dimethylpentanoic acid is a 7-carbon amino acid isomer detected in Murchison meteorite extracts, with an estimated quantity of approximately 0.5 nmol per gram meteorite.
D-leucine hydroxamic acid inhibits LAP-C and LAP-M metalloenzymes approximately as effectively as L-leucine hydroxamic acid.
D-leucine hydroxamic acid exhibits a Ki value of 4.3 × 10⁻⁶ M for its enzyme target, indicating significant inhibitory activity despite the absence of the α-amino group.
D-leucine hydroxamic acid is a potent aminopeptidase A (AAP) inhibitor with a K I of 2.0 nM.
d-leucine hydroxamic acid inhibits Aeromonas proteolytica aminopeptidase with a K i of 2.0 nM, which is much stronger than its l-leucine hydroxamic acid analog.
D-leucine hydroxamic acid is a competitive inhibitor of cytosolic leucine aminopeptidase and inhibits the enzyme about 10 times more effectively than other hydroxamic acids.
Val-Leu-OMe is a dipeptide methyl ester that produces concentration-dependent depletion of NK function, showing at least some NK toxicity.
Topical aminolevulinic acid hydrochloride is used in photodynamic therapy of actinic keratoses, where it is converted to protoporphyrin IX upon blue light exposure.
Topical Aminolevulinic acid hydrochloride (ALA) accumulates in actinic keratoses and, when activated by blue light, contributes to the eradication of actinic keratoses.
Aminolevulinic acid is used as an active ingredient in a W/O emulsion at 20% concentration for photodynamic therapy, where it is applied to actinic keratosis lesions prior to light-based treatment.

Topical aminolevulinic acid (ALA) combined with blue light photodynamic therapy achieves a 75% or 100% clearance rate in 78% of patients with actinic keratoses on the face and 56% on the scalp at 12 weeks after 1 or 2 treatments.
Alanine-glycine-aspartic acid is a tripeptide identified as the antioxidant peptide present in Hippocampus abdominalis protein hydrolysate.

Aspartic acid-Alanine-Glycine (DAG) is a conserved motif derived from the SCMV Coat Protein (CP) gene and can be used in hairpin RNA constructs to target SCMV CP for gene silencing in transgenic plants.
CHPG is a selective mGluR5 agonist that, at low concentrations, increases quantal release of glutamate from presynaptic terminals in the rat spinal cord sensory region.
2-(3,4-dimethoxyphenyl)-3-(4-hydroxyphenyl)-thiazolidin-4-one displays potent cytotoxic activity against MCF-7 and SKBR3 breast cancer cells.
3-OH aniracetam at 1.5 g does not significantly reduce hyperventilation-induced changes in EEG power density in the 3.0 to 7.1-Hz band compared to placebo (P > 0.10, Kruskal-Wallis one-way ANOVA).
1-[4-(4-nitrophenoxy)phenyl]propane-1-one (NPPP) is a synthetic compound with reported in vitro and in vivo anti-coccidial effects.

Proteomic analysis suggests that 1-[4-(4-nitrophenoxy)phenyl]propane-1-one (NPPP) targets proteins such as a putative vacuolar ATP synthase subunit C and a class I S-adenosylmethionine-dependent methyltransferase in Toxoplasma gondii.
1-[4-(4-nitrophenoxy)phenyl]propane-1-one (NPPP) is a synthetic compound with anti-coccidial activity, showing lower EC50 values for anti-Toxoplasma activity in vitro compared to pyrimethamine.
1-[4-(4-nitrophenoxy) phenyl] propane-1-one (NPPP) is significantly active against Toxoplasma gondii tachyzoites both in vitro and in vivo.
Oral administration of 1-[4-(4-nitrophenoxy)phenyl]propane-1-one (NPPP) at 100 mg/kg for 4 days reduces the number of tachyzoites in Eimeria tenella-infected chickens.
4-nitrophenyl 3-O-acetyl-β-d-xylopyranoside is a preferred substrate for the CE16 T. reesei acetylesterase, which displays identical positional specificity to enzymes from other sources.

Cyclopropyl p-nitrophenyl ketone is used as a substrate in the nucleophilic aromatic substitution synthesis of no-carrier-added fluorine-18-labeled N-methylspiroperidol, providing a 10–15% radiochemical yield (based on [18F-] with mass <2 nmol and specific activity >10 Ci/mumol (EOB)).
The rate of cleavage of p-nitrophenyl trimethylacetate by chimeric enzymes rCE and hCE1 decreases significantly as the distance from the carboxyl group and bulkiness of the substrate increase.

p-nitrophenyl trimethylacetate reacts with hydrogen peroxide in aqueous solution, and the rate constant for this reaction is temperature-dependent and influenced by micelle formation in EPE copolymers.
p-nitrophenyl trimethylacetate is used as an acylating agent to study the kinetics of acylation of bound α-chymotrypsin on nylon fiber at pH 7.0 and 25°C.
Correctors containing the 4-methoxyphenyl cyclopropane carboxamide moiety are inactive for F508del-CFTR correction.
2-methyl-4-(4-nitrophenyl)thiazole undergoes microsomal nitroreduction in the kidney, resulting in the formation of a stable amino derivative as the primary product.
2-methyl-4-(4-nitrophenyl)-thiazole is a thiazole derivative containing a nitro group that can be reduced by mammalian nitroreductase enzyme systems.

8-[3-(2,5-dimethylpyrrol-1-yl)benzoyl]-3-(4-methoxyphenyl)-1-oxa-8-azaspiro [4.5]dec-2-ene (M-5) is a non-competitive inhibitor of ODC with a Ki value between 78 and 371 µM against recombinant L. donovani ODC.
8-[3-(2,5-dimethylpyrrol-1-yl)benzoyl]-3-(4-methoxyphenyl)-1-oxa-8-azaspiro [4.5]dec-2-ene (M-5) inhibits L. donovani promastigotes with an EC50 of 125 µM.
p-nitrophenyl thymidine 5'-carbonate serves as a substrate for phosphorylation by p-nitrophenyl phosphorodichloridate, yielding thymidine 3'-phosphate and p-nitrophenol.

p-nitrophenyl thymidine 5'-carbonate can be phosphorylated at the free hydroxyl position using p-nitrophenyl phosphorodichloridate or a mixture of η-cyanoethyl phosphate and N,N'-dicyclohexylcarbodiimide.
4-(4-nitrophenyl)-5-methoxycarbonyl-6-methyl-3,4-dihydropyridone exhibits potent inhibitory activity against Jack bean urease with an IC50 of 29.12 µM, outperforming hydroxyurea (IC50 = 100.0 µM).
Glipizide is a hypoglycemic agent belonging to the sulfonylurea group.

Iodoglibenclamide is a 125I-labeled derivative of glibenclamide that can be used as a photoaffinity probe to identify sulfonylurea receptors in cell membranes due to its higher specific activity compared to [3H]glibenclamide.
The binding of [125I]iodoglibenclamide to recombinant SUR1 is not systematically affected by nucleotides, as demonstrated by unpublished data.

Glibenclamide is a high-affinity ligand for the sulfonylurea receptor (SUR1), with two major glycosylated forms identified: a 140-kDa species and a 150–170-kDa species, both showing a Kd of approximately 3.5 nM for binding to SUR1.
Iodoglibenclamide is used as a photolabeling agent to study the binding sites for sulfonylureas and glibenclamide on the KATP channel.
Iodoglibenclamide binds to SUR2A with a dissociation constant (K d) of 10 ÌM.

1-[4-(5-chloro-2-methoxybenzamidoethyl)benzene sulfonamido]-1-cyclohexylamino-2-nitroethylene (BM 225) inhibits 86Rb (42K substitute) outflow from rat pancreatic islets at 2.8 mM D-glucose and reduces the frequency of KATP+ channel openings in excised inside-out membrane patches.

1-[4-(5-chloro-2-methoxybenzamidoethyl)benzene sulfonamido]-1-cyclohexylamino-2-nitroethylene increases intracellular Ca2+ concentration in single pancreatic islet cells, an effect counteracted by verapamil, a Ca2+ entry blocker.

1-[4-(5-chloro-2-methoxybenzamidoethyl)benzene sulfonamido]-1-cyclohexylamino-2-nitroethylene (BM 225) is a glibenclamide isostere that increases 45Ca outflow and insulin release in rat pancreatic islets at 10 and 25 µM in the presence of 8.3 mM D-glucose.

1-[4-(5-chloro-2-methoxybenzamidoethyl)benzene sulfonamido]-1-cyclohexylamino-2-nitroethylene (BM 225) lowers plasma glucose in fed rats after intraperitoneal injection in a dose-dependent manner, with greater potency and faster action than BM 208.
At 2.8 mM glucose and extracellular Ca2+, 1-[4-(5-chloro-2-methoxybenzamidoethyl)benzene sulfonamido]-1-cyclohexylamino-2-nitroethylene stimulates insulin output in rat pancreatic islets.

1-(3-(triethoxysilyl) propyl)-3-tosylurea is a first generation sulfonylurea anti-diabetes agent with silyl (isosteric with carbon) groups, which can activate non-bonding electrons of nitrogen and enhance potency.

N-(4-chlorophenyl)aminocarbonyl-4-methylbenzene sulfonamide (LY181984) acts as an uncoupler of mitochondrial oxidative phosphorylation, with a pKa value between 6.0 and 6.2 and hydrophobic properties.
N-(4-Methylphenylsulfonyl)-N'-(4-chlorophenyl)urea (MPCU) accumulates inside mitochondria as a function of the intracellular transmembrane pH gradient.
N-(4-chlorophenyl)aminocarbonyl-4-methylbenzene sulfonamide (LY181984) is a diarylsulfonylurea with antitumor activity and has been shown to accumulate in mitochondria.

The accumulation of N-(4-Methylphenylsulfonyl)-N'-(4-chlorophenyl)urea in mitochondria is inhibited by 90% in the presence of nigericin, which increases mitochondrial membrane potential and collapses the pH gradient, indicating the pH gradient plays a role in MPCU sequestration.
N-(4-methylphenylsulfonyl)-N'-(4-chlorophenyl)urea (LY181984) is an antitumor sulfonylurea that acts as a competitive inhibitor of NADH oxidase activity from HeLa cell plasma membranes.
N-(2-aminoethyl)-5-iodo-2-pyridinecarboxamide can be radiolabelled with 123I and 125I to produce radioligands for in vitro and in vivo studies, including SPECT imaging in humans.
Ro 43-0463 shows brain uptake in both human and rat brain stem, specifically in the ependyma, as detected by SPET.
In rats, N-(2-aminoethyl)-5-iodo-2-pyridinecarboxamide shows peak brain uptake of 0.50% of the injected dose at 30 minutes, with slow washout over 60 minutes.

7-O-acetyl-19,20-epoxycytochalasin D is a 19,20-epoxycytochalasan isolated from Xylaria cf. curta, an endophyte associated with Solanum tuberosum, and shows cytotoxicity against HL-60 and MCF-7 cancer cell lines with IC50s in the range of 13.31–37.16 µM, while remaining inactive against A-549, SMMC-7721, and SW480 cell lines.
Beauvericin A is an antimycobacterial and antiplasmodial cyclodepsipeptide isolated from Paecilomyces sp.

In l-Phe-l-Val peptide structures, the phenyl group of l-Phe is involved in intermolecular interactions, including aromatic stacking and hydrogen bonding, to a higher degree than typically observed in peptide structures.

L-Val-L-Phe is a fully 13C/15N labeled dipeptide whose 13C and 15N NMR linewidths under high-power XiX decoupling range from 75 to 120 Hz full width at half height (FWHH) using a 500 MHz spectrometer and a 1.8 mm o.d. double-resonance MAS probe at 50 kHz.
Octaminomycin A is a cyclic octadepsipeptide with a planar structure containing an N-acetyl or N-propionyl threonine residue, isolated from a fraction library by NPPlot screening.
Octaminomycin A exhibits in vitro antimalarial activity against both chloroquine-sensitive 3D7 and chloroquine-resistant Dd2 and K1 strains of Plasmodium, with no cytotoxicity up to 30 μM.
Teixobactin is an antibiotic produced by the Gram-negative β-proteobacterium Eleftheria terrae and features a chemical scaffold distinct from existing antibiotics.

Teixobactin is an antibiotic that targets lipid molecules essential to cell-wall synthesis, making the development of teixobactin-resistant bacteria less likely compared to antibiotics targeting proteins.
Lyngbyastatin 4 is a serine protease inhibitor with a selectivity profile distinct from that of other Ahp-containing cyclodepsipeptides.
Lyngbyastatin 4 is a cyclic peptide protease inhibitor structurally related to dolastatin 13, symplostatin 2, and somamides A and B, and is closely related to marine cyanobacterial metabolites.

Lyngbyastatin 4 selectively inhibits elastase and chymotrypsin activity with IC50 values of 0.03 µM and 0.30 µM, respectively, in vitro, showing over 1000-fold and 100-fold selectivity for elastase and chymotrypsin, respectively, compared to other serine proteases.
15G256γ is a linear hexapeptide with a delta-hydroxymyristic acid amide substituted N-terminus, isolated from Pochonia bulbinosa fermentation and exhibiting potent cell wall activity.
Octaminomycin B is a cyclic octadepsipeptide with a planar structure containing an N-acetyl or N-propionyl threonine residue, isolated from a fraction library by NPPlot screening.
Octaminomycin B exhibits in vitro antimalarial activity against chloroquine-sensitive 3D7 and chloroquine-resistant Dd2 and K1 strains of Plasmodium, with no cytotoxicity up to 30 μM.
Hydrogen bond patterns in 5-benzyl-2-thiohydantoin and its acetylated derivative have been investigated using FT-IR and FT-Raman spectroscopy.
5-benzyl-2-thiohydantoin exhibits reduced antithyroid activity compared to other 5-substituted 2-thiohydantoins.
5-benzyl 2-thiohydantoin is less active than 2-thiohydantoin as a single-dose antithyroid agent in experimental animals.
Pseudoxylallemycin C is a newly identified cyclic peptide produced by Pseudoxylaria, a fungus associated with termite-inhabited fungus combs.
Pseudoxylallemycin C is a cyclotetrapeptide with a rare allenyl (C-4 fragment) modification and demonstrates promising antimicrobial, antiproliferative, and cytotoxic effects.
PheMeAc, a lipophilic derivative of phenylalanine, reacts with enzymatically generated .CCl3 in the presence of NADPH to produce benzene as a volatile reaction product.
(E)-3-(2,4-dihydroxyphenyl)-1-(thiophen-2-yl)prop-2-en-1-one is a potential therapeutic agent for diseases associated with hyperpigment disorders due to its strong tyrosinase inhibitory activity.
(E)-3-(2,4-dihydroxyphenyl)-1-(thiophen-2-yl)prop-2-en-1-one inhibits melanin content and intracellular tyrosinase in a dose-dependent manner in alpha-MSH and IBMX-induced B16F10 melanoma cells.

(E)-3-(2,4-dihydroxyphenyl)-1-(thiophen-2-yl)prop-2-en-1-one is a potent competitive inhibitor of mushroom tyrosinase, exhibiting IC50 values of 0.013 µM for tyrosine hydroxylase and 0.93 µM for dopa oxidase.
