4-hydroxy-2-chlorocinnamic acid is a novel compound produced by cinnamate 4-hydroxylase (C4H) from a substrate analogue with a 2-chloro aromatic side chain.
4-hydroxychalcone exhibits anti-aromatase activity, and its inhibitory effect is reduced when hydroxylated at position 2Ј, resulting in 2Ј,4-dihydroxychalcone.
4-hydroxychalcone is the most potent inhibitor of 17␤-hydroxysteroid dehydrogenase among the compounds studied.
4-hydroxychalcone is the most potent inhibitor of 17␤-hydroxysteroid dehydrogenase among the compounds studied.
4-hydroxychalcone is a potent inhibitor of 17beta-hydroxysteroid dehydrogenase activity in human placental microsomes, with an IC50 value of 16 µM.

Copper(II) 2-thiopheneglyoxylic acid chloride forms a metal halide complex whose vibrational frequencies and assignments have been characterized using DFT/B3LYP calculations with the LANL2DZ basis set.
3-methoxy-2',4,4'-trihydroxy-2-prenylchalcone is a chalcone isolated from Shuteria sinensis that exhibits effects on glucose consumption in insulin-resistant HepG2 cells.
Licochalcone G is a chalcone derivative isolated from the acetone extract of Glycyrrhiza inflata and identified as an active principle with inhibitory activity against influenza neuraminidase (NA).

Thiophene-2-carbonyl-guanidine is an NHE-1 inhibitor with an IC50 of 2.0 µM and exhibits potent cardioprotective effects by inhibiting intracellular Ca2+ overload and mitochondrial cell death pathways.
Methylhydroxychalcone at a dose of 1 mg/disc significantly inhibits vascularization in the chick embryo chorioallantoic membrane, causing distortion of the normal vascular architecture.
2'-hydroxy-4'-methoxychalcone potently reduces iNOS-catalyzed nitric oxide production.
2'-hydroxy-4'-methoxychalcone inhibits LPS-induced production of nitrite and TNF-α in RAW 264.7 macrophages by preventing LPS-induced degradation of IkB-α, thereby blocking NF-κB activation.
2'-hydroxy-4'-methoxychalcone suppresses LPS-induced production of nitrite and TNF-α in RAW 264.7 murine macrophage cells by blocking LPS-induced activation of NF-κB and AP-1 signaling.

3-phenylquinolinylchalcone derivatives exhibit antiproliferative activity against breast cancer cell lines MCF-7, MDA-MB-231, and SKBR-3, with IC50 values in the range of 0.70–0.86 mM for MDA-MB-231 and SKBR-3, and 4.04 mM for MCF-7 in the XTT cell proliferation assay.

3-phenylquinolinylchalcone derivatives are evaluated for anticancer activity in vitro against six cancer cell lines, including non-small cell lung cancer (H1299, H460, A549), breast cancer (MCF-7, MDA-MB-231, SKBR-3), and a normal mammary epithelial cell (H184B5F5/M10), using the XTT cell viability assay to determine IC50 values.
The (S)-suprofen thioester undergoes a 34-fold higher racemization rate compared to the (S)-naproxen thioester when using lipase and trioctylamine as catalysts in isooctane.

(S)-suprofen can be obtained from (R,S)-suprofen 2,2,2-trifluoroethyl thioester with 95% enantiomeric excess (ee) via lipase-catalyzed enantioselective hydrolysis under continuous in situ racemization conditions.
Nitrobenzamide derivatives, such as CB 1954, are substrates for DT diaphorase, and all tested co-factors for menadione reduction are equally effective co-factors for their reduction.

Nitrobenzamide CB1954 is a cytotoxic compound that interacts with DT-diaphorase, with its aziridinyl group pointing toward Tyr155 and its amide group near a hydrophobic pocket next to Tyr128 in the active site.
Nitrobenzamide derivatives function as photonucleases that can be conjugated to acridine derivatives to enhance DNA interaction and activity.

M-1(b) is a metabolite of aranidipine that appreciably inhibits Ca2+-induced contraction in isolated rabbit arteries, but is several fold less potent than aranidipine due to lower affinity for the dihydropyridine binding site.

5-(2-hydroxybenzoyl)-3-methyl-1-(2-pyridinyl)pyrazol-4-carboxylic acid methyl ester forms Ni(II) complexes that exhibit relatively high cytotoxic activity towards HL-60 and NALM-6 leukaemia cells and WM-115 melanoma cells.
2-nitrobenzamidoacridine exhibits lower DNA photocleaving efficiency compared to 4-nitrobenzamidoacridine and 3-nitrobenzamidoacridine.
The endo beta-(1→5)-galactofuranase from Penicillium oxalicum does not hydrolyze o-nitrophenyl galactose.
Methyl 2-fluorobenzoate undergoes enzymatic dihydroxylation by E. coli JM109 (pDTG601A) to yield one diol regioselectively.
Methyl 2-ethylbenzoate serves as a substrate for P450 BM3-catalyzed biotransformation, yielding optically active 3methylphthalides with 7% yield and 31% enantiomeric excess.
Methyl 2-ethylbenzoate serves as a substrate for P450 BM3-catalyzed C-H activation, resulting in the formation of isocoumarin as a by-product due to ·hydroxylation at the ethyl moiety.

Methyl 2-ethylbenzoate can undergo enantioselective benzylic hydroxylation by P450 BM3, yielding products with enantiomeric excesses (ee) ranging from 16% to 76% depending on the enzyme variant and reaction conditions.
Methyl 2-ethylbenzoate can be oxidized by P450 BM3 variants through protein engineering to produce 3-methylisobenzofuran-1(3H)-one.
Methyl 2-(trifluoromethyl)benzoate serves as a substrate for fungal enzyme-catalyzed formation of arene oxide and oxepine metabolites during aromatic hydroxylation.
2,4-Dinitrophenyl 4-methylbenzoate exhibits a second-order rate constant (k) of 39.2 (0.27) M⁻¹ s⁻¹ in 80 mol % H2O-20 mol % DMSO at 25.0 °C for reactions with OH-, CN-, and N3- substituents.
Epoxymollugin is a newly identified compound isolated from the roots of Rubia cordifolia L., and its structure was elucidated using chemical and spectroscopic evidence.
Epoxymollugin was identified as a compound isolated from the methylene chloride fraction of Rubia cordifolia L. roots and marked its first isolation from a natural source.
4-nitropyrene 9,10-quinone is mutagenic in the bacterial strain TA98, both with and without S9 metabolic activation, but is much less mutagenic in TA98NR and TA98/1,8-DNP6 strains.
Nitrobenzoylcoumarin derivatives can lower parasitaemia in mice infected with the Y strain of T. cruzi.
Modification of the allyl group on the C6 carbon of nitrobenzoylcoumarin to an n-propyl chain increases its activity against T. cruzi epimastigote, making it as active as benznidazole.
Nitrobenzoylcoumarin derivatives exhibit low toxicity and are effective against T. cruzi amastigote forms.
EB-PSMA-617 is an albumin binding radioligand that shows higher tumor uptake than PSMA-617 and longer blood circulation (12.2 ± 0.8 %ID/g at 1 hour post-injection) in PET studies using Y-86.
EB-PSMA-617 enhances cellular uptake, internalization, and retention of radioactivity, resulting in improved therapeutic effect compared to other PSMA-617 variants.
PSMA I&T is a PSMA inhibitor that has been described as a theranostic agent for PET imaging and is under investigation for its biodistribution and radiation dosimetry.
PSMA I&T is a radiopharmaceutical that targets PSMA and has demonstrated comparable PSMA affinity and in vivo uptake and kinetics to 68 Ga-PSMA HBED-CC in LNCaP-tumor xenograft models.
177 Lu-PSMA I&T is a radiolabeled compound based on the PSMA-binding inhibitor PSMA I&T, developed for imaging and therapy targeting prostate cancer.

dansyl-Pro-Gln-Gly-Ile-Ala-Gly-D-Arg is a fluorescent peptide substrate specifically cleaved by vertebrate collagenases at the Gly-Ile bond, making it suitable for sensitive detection of collagenase activity.

TRag is a thrombin receptor agonist that acts as a PAR-1 agonist and induces a significant rise in intracellular calcium concentration in OLN-93 oligodendroglial cells after stimulation with 5 µM for 60 seconds.
Antarelix, a GnRH antagonist, induces a 2.5-fold increase in GnRH-R mRNA levels in the rat testis 8 hours after administration, without affecting GnRH mRNA.

E[c(RGDyK)]2 is a dimeric RGD peptide that can be radiolabeled with [F-18]AMFB via selective conjugation of its free thiol groups, resulting in high yields (85 ± 5 % non-decay-corrected) and high specific activity for PET imaging of integrin expression.

Multimeric cyclic RGD peptides containing c(RGDyK), such as E[c(RGDyK)]2 and its octamer E[E[E[c(RGDyK)]2]2]2, have been labeled with 64Cu and 18F for PET tumor imaging and show improved tumor uptake and retention compared to dimeric analogs.
E[c(RGDyK)]2 is a dimeric RGD peptide that can be labeled with F-18 for use in quantitative PET imaging of tumor integrin v3 expression.
E[c(RGDyK)]2 is a dimeric RGD peptide that, when coupled with DOTA and labeled with 64 Cu, is used for breast cancer targeting in imaging studies.
Argenine vasopressin (ADH) administration in rats significantly increases haematocrit in all renal zones, with the most pronounced increase in the inner medulla.

Argenine vasopressin (ADH) may increase haemodynamic resistance in the rat kidney by acting as a vasoconstrictor and by indirectly increasing blood viscosity in the medulla through increased tissue osmolality.

KP-102 (D-alanyl-3-(2-naphthyl)-Dalanyl-L-alanyl-L-tryptophyl-D-phenylalanyl-L-lysineamide dihydrochloride) is a hexapeptide that potently promotes growth hormone (GH) release by acting at both hypothalamic and pituitary sites.
KP-102 dose-dependently increases GH release in conscious rats and dogs, with effects stronger than those of GHRH.
Glu-Glu-Glu-Glu-Tyr-Pro-Met-Glu (MT peptide) inhibits chemotaxis of rabbit peritoneal leucocytes stimulated by the synthetic chemoattractant fMet-Leu-Phe.

Glu-Glu-Glu-Glu-Tyr-Pro-Met-Glu (MT peptide) inhibits chemotaxis of rabbit peritoneal leucocytes stimulated by fMet-Leu-Phe and blocks the release of arachidonic acid from phospholipids in neutrophils, indicating inhibition distal to chemotactic receptors.
Obestatin is a peptide that binds specifically to tissue homogenates of jejunum, pituitary, stomach, and ileum, with minimal binding in the liver and kidney.
Obestatin binding to crude plasma membrane fractions of white adipose tissue and jejunum is specific, as shown by minimal binding in liver and kidney.

AMT (2-amino-dihydro-6-methyl-4H-1,3-thiazine) is a nitric oxide synthase II (NOS II) inhibitor that reduces plasma nitric oxide levels and attenuates the post-suspension reduction in mean arterial pressure in rats.
Amino-thiochroman derivatives are used as P2 ligands in the design of HIV-1 protease inhibitors to promote effective hydrogen bonding interactions with backbone atoms in the S2 subsite.
Aminothiochromane-based carboxamide ligands are designed to promote hydrogen bonding and van der Waals interactions in the active site of HIV-1 protease.
3-aminoharman is a novel modifier of spontaneous chromosomal aberrations (SCE) induction and is present in pyrolysates of tryptophan and casein.

3-Aminoharman suppresses the induction of sister chromatid exchange (SCE) by aromatic hydrocarbons and aromatic amine pyrolysis products, including Trp-P-2, indicating a role as a chemical inducer of SCE.

3-aminoharman, though a weak SCE inducer on its own, can abolish or partially abolish the SCE-inducing abilities of certain polycyclic mutagens such as Trp-P-2, aflatoxin B1, and benzo[a]pyrene when included in culture media with these mutagens.

Topical administration of 2-amino-5,6-dihydro-6-methyl-1,3-thiazine (AMT), a nitric oxide synthase (NOS) inhibitor, increases intraocular pressure (IOP) in rabbits by 394 ± 202 arbitrary units under the described experimental conditions.
4-methylthiazole increases rGSTA2/3/5 mRNAs in starving rats, but rGSTM1/2 mRNAs are not further increased.
4-methylthiazole induces rGSTA1/2, rGSTA3/5, and rGSTM1 proteins by 2.6-, 2.1-, and 2.1-fold, respectively, at 3 days in the rat liver.
4-MT (methylthiazole) at a dose of 0.35 mmol/kg increases hepatic rGSTA2, rGSTA3, and rGSTA5 mRNA levels in starving animals.

Macrocyclic analogs of 2-aminopiperazine have been developed as ALK inhibitors with improved potency and selectivity, showing up to a three- to fourfold greater cellular activity (IC50 = 1 nM vs. 13.38 nM for wt EML4-ALK and L1196M mutants) compared to related 2-aminopyridine analogs.

2-(2,6-diethylphenyl)imidazolidine acts as a full agonist at the octopaminergic receptor in the cockroach thoracic nervous system, with activity similar to octopamine in stimulating adenylate cyclase.
Dimeric amino(methyl)thiazole derivatives exhibit increased high-affinity selectivity for the human histamine H2 receptor compared to monomeric analogues.
3',5'-diamino-3',5'-dideoxythymidine undergoes regioselective acylation by Candida antarctica lipase B, favoring the 5'-position over the 3'-position by up to 80-fold.

Medium hydro-Candida antarctica lipase B (CAL-B) catalyzes the regioselective acylation of 3',5'-diamino-3',5'-dideoxythymidine with nonactivated esters, favoring the 5'-position over the 3'-position by up to 80-fold.
DL-homocysteine thiolactone hydrochloride supports the resumption of growth in methionine-deficient rats, though at a slower rate than methionine supplementation.

β-hydroxyectoine is a potent protein stabilizer that increases the melting temperature of bovine ribonuclease A by 4.1 K/mol β-hydroxyectoine and provides up to 13.5 kJ/mol of stabilization at 330 K with a 3 M concentration.
DMCM, a ␤-carboline, acts as a negative allosteric modulator and inhibits GABA-induced currents at the benzodiazepine site of GABA A receptors in cultured auditory neurons.
tetrahydropyrido [4,3-d]pyrimidine-2,4-dione is a small molecule scaffold evaluated as a potential GnRH receptor antagonist based on computational and synthetic criteria.

Tetrahydropyrido [4,3-d]pyrimidine-2,4-dione serves as a core scaffold for small molecule antagonists targeting the human GnRH receptor, with analogs designed around this core achieving binding affinity below 10 nM in vitro.
The release of acetic and succinic acid from HPMCAS during hot melt extrusion is directly proportional to the processing speed at lower operating temperatures.
The release of acetic and succinic acid from HPMCAS during hot melt extrusion is directly proportional to the processing speed at lower operating temperatures.

The presence of succinate acetate in vivo proton MR spectroscopy indicates the presence of degenerating cysticerci in the brain, distinguishing it from anaerobic brain abscesses where acetate is present alone or at higher concentrations than succinate.

Topical treatment with an acetic and boric acid solution administered three times daily for 14 days successfully resolved the scaly crusts and ear exudate associated with Corynebacterium kroppenstedtii infection in a lovebird.
Acetic acid butyrate is a short-chain fatty acid with potential as a new drug for chemotherapy or chemoprevention of head and neck squamous cell carcinoma (HNSCC).
Ammonium acetate-acetic acid acts as a catalyst for the condensation of ketones with ethyl cyanoacetate to produce ethyl alkylidene alpha cyanoacetate under ultrasound irradiation.
Polyoxyethylene mono-p-nonylphenyl ether is used in a turbidimetric immunoassay to accelerate and enhance the immunoprecipitation reaction for quantifying serum antistreptolysin-O.
CBMA is a zwitterionic polymer that, when incorporated into low-concentration fluoride varnish (LCFV), contributes to biofilm inhibition and reduces enamel demineralization.

Potassium acetylcholine-dependent inward rectifier (I KACh) channels are activated by Diadenosine pentaphosphate in guinea pig atrial myocytes, and this activation is abolished by P1 receptor antagonists or GIRK channel blockers.
5-hydroxyl-5-methyl-2-hexenoic acid is an antifungal compound isolated from the mycelium of Actinoplanes sp. HBDN08.

5-hydroxyl-5-methyl-2-hexenoic acid exhibits strong in vitro antifungal activity against Botrytis cinerea, Cladosporium cucumerinum, and Corynespora cassiicola with IC50 values of 32.45, 27.17, and 30.66 mg/L, respectively.

TAMM (tetrakis(acetoxymethyl)mercury) is a heavy-atom compound that can be solubilized by excess glycylglycine and, when incorporated into nucleosome core particle crystals, produces a derivative with a single major site as revealed by X-ray crystallography.

Glycidol acetate can be resolved by interfacial esterases from marine invertebrates, achieving an enantiomeric ratio of 2.67 (S-selective) when using a biocatalyst derived from Stichodactyla helianthus.
n-propyl fluoroformate undergoes solvolysis in a wide range of hydroxylic solvents via an addition-elimination pathway.
n-propyl fluoroformate solvolyzes via an addition-elimination pathway, with the addition step being rate-determining.

Mycophenolic acid potentiates the anti-HBV activity of guanine- and diaminopurine-based nucleoside analogues by depleting intracellular dGTP pools, thereby reducing competition with 5'-triphosphorylated metabolites in DNA polymerization.
n-propyl fluoroformate undergoes solvolysis in a wide range of hydroxylic solvents via an addition-elimination pathway.
n-propyl fluoroformate solvolyzes via an addition-elimination pathway, with the addition step being rate-determining.
15-MPA is a pinusolide derivative isolated from Biota orientalis leaves that exhibits neuroprotective activity against glutamate-induced toxicity in primary cultured rat cortical cells.
4-OI attenuates periodontal inflammatory damage and oxidative bone resorption by activating Nrf2-mediated antioxidant defense mechanisms.
Estra-4,9(10)-dien-3-one reacts with peracids to yield monoepoxides in high yield.

Estra-4,9-dien-3-one is a glucocorticoid receptor antagonist (GRA) and progesterone receptor antagonist (PRA) that reduces weight gain and decreases elevated insulin levels and weight gain in olanzapine-treated rats.

Estra-4,9-dien-3-one (mifepristone) is a glucocorticoid receptor antagonist (GRA) and progesterone receptor antagonist (PRA) that reduces weight gain observed during olanzapine or risperidone treatment.
17α-methyl-19-nortestosterone (normethandrone) is a potent inhibitor of glucagon-induced hyperglycemia.
10b,17b-dihydroxyestra-1,4dien-3-one (DHED) acts as a substrate for a short-chain NADPH-dependent dehydrogenase/reductase selectively expressed in the brain and linked to neuroprotection.
10β,17β-dihydroxy-1,4-estradiene-3-one (E2-quinol) is a metabolite of estradiol formed via metabolic oxidation catalyzed by cytochrome P450 isoenzymes in rat liver microsomal systems.
10b,17b-dihydroxyestra-1,4dien-3-one (DHED) is a bioprecursor prodrug that is selectively converted to E2 only in the brain after systemic administration.
Androsta-1,4-diene-3,17-dione (androstadienedione) is produced from progesterone by certain Fusarium solani strains, particularly non-germinating spores.

17 alpha-ethynyl-17-hydroxy-7 alpha-methylestr-4-en-3-one (U13851) has high affinity for both progesterone and androgen receptors and is a potent competitor for cytosol [3H]-R1881 binding sites and nuclear accumulation of label in Sertoli cells.
17 alpha-ethynyl-17-hydroxy-7 alpha-methylestr-4-en-3-one (U13851) increases Sertoli cell nuclear RNA polymerase II activity.
For 19-hydroxyandrosta-4,6-diene-3,17-dione, MM2 molecular mechanics calculations indicate that both 19µ- and 19–5-conformations are nearly equally relevant to enzymatic out-of-ring hydroxylations.

19-hydroxyandrosta-4,6-diene-3,17-dione (19OHAD) is a substrate for aromatase whose stereospecificity of oxidation matches that of 19-hydroxyandrost-4-ene-3,17-dione (19OHA), indicating that conformational constraints in 19OHA are not major determinants of aromatase substrate specificity.
16α-hydroxytestosterone is a main metabolite of testosterone produced by rat hepatocytes.
In Mycobacterium smegmatis, 9OHAD (9-hydroxydehydrocholesterol) accumulates when cholesterol but not AD is used as a substrate in the cholesterol degradation process.
In Mycobacterium smegmatis, AD (dehydrocholesterol) accumulates during cholesterol degradation when the ksdD-1 gene is disrupted.
9-hydroxyandrost-1,4-dien-3-one (9OHAD) is a steroid intermediate that can be degraded by R. erythropolis without the need for the kshA2 gene, supporting its preferential use in steroid catabolism.
In Mycobacterium smegmatis, the double disruption of ksdD-1 and ksdD-2 genes leads to accumulation of AD in the cholesterol degradation process performed in rich medium.
20-hydroxymethyl-1,4-pregnadien-3-one increases the 11ss-hydroxylase activity of Cochliobolus lunatus mycelia by about 100-fold when used as a pretreatment for 4–5 hours.
6-methylenandrost-4-ene-3,17-dione is not a time-dependent inactivator of aromatase.
6-methylene-4-androstene-3,17-dione does not yield a 1-dehydrogenation product under the DDQ and tert-butyldimethylchlorosilane catalysis conditions.
Trimethyloxosulfonium hydroxide serves as an effective methylating agent for the regioselective methylation of inosine to produce 7-methylinosine under aqueous conditions.
Oxidovanadium(IV) hydroxide (VO(OH)2) is a sparingly soluble form generated under non-oxic conditions and can be transported in water as colloids or solubilized by complexing ligands.
Vanadyl (IV) VO2+ exists in nanomolar concentrations in the human body due to its precipitation as oxidovanadium(IV)-hydroxide.
The rate constant for the conversion of a sulfide to a sulfoxide (thiophene oxide) increases when a more electron-donating substituent is introduced into the thiophene ring.
Thiophene-1-oxide has been incorporated into π-conjugated systems to improve electron mobility in OLED materials.
Thiophene-S-oxide is an unstable and reactive metabolite of thiophenes formed via P450-catalyzed oxidation.
Diastereoisomeric thiophene-S-oxide dimers have been detected in in vitro and in vivo studies, providing evidence for the formation of thiophene-S-oxide.
A thiophene S-oxide intermediate is implicated as a possible species involved in the inactivation of CYP 2C19 and CYP 2C9 by ticlopidine and tienilic acid, respectively.
Mes-NaOH buffer at pH 7.0 is used as the solvent for NMR-based enzyme kinetic assays involving FUT8.
La(TTHA)3- is a diamagnetic analog of Dy(TTHA)3- and provides conditions similar to SR spectroscopy without the broadening effects of the paramagnetic Dy(111).
La( TTHA)-3 is used as a diamagnetic shift reagent in NMR spectroscopy of heart tissue during perfusion and reperfusion.
Phosphonous pyruvate, produced from phosphonous alanine analogs by pyridoxal 5'-phosphate-dependent alanine aminotransferase, acts as an irreversible nanomolar inhibitor of pyruvate dehydrogenase.
Oxidovanadium(IV) chloride exhibits only slight inhibitory effects on osteoblast-like MG-63 cells at 100 μM, with an IC50 greater than or equal to 100 μM.

Unstable binding of [14C]acetaldehyde to plasma proteins is significantly correlated with the half-life of acetaldehyde in elimination tests (p = 0.74, P < 0.005) in patients with atherosclerotic disease.
The transfer of [14C]acetaldehyde from acetaldehyde-protein complexes to K562 cells is reduced by treatment with thioctic acid or dihydrolipoic acid.
[14C]acetaldehyde is rapidly oxidized by red blood cell aldehyde dehydrogenase, with less than 4% of initial [14C]acetaldehyde remaining after 3 hours of exposure in untreated cells.
SDS-PAGE analysis shows that [14C]acetaldehyde binds to many platelet proteins at neutral pH.

[14C]acetaldehyde forms stable adducts with hemoglobin in red blood cells, with the molar ratio of acetaldehyde to hemoglobin increasing from 0.00069 after 3 hours at 50 nmol/ml to 0.039 after 48 hours at 200 nmol/ml in untreated cells.
Oxidovanadium(IV) chloride exhibits only slight inhibitory effects on osteoblast-like MG-63 cells at 100 μM, with an IC50 greater than or equal to 100 μM.
mBBr binds to protein thiols that are necessary for sperm motility, and its effect on sperm motility can be blocked by pre-incubation with DTT.
5-hydroxyl-5-methyl-2-hexenoic acid is an antifungal compound isolated from the mycelium of Actinoplanes sp. HBDN08.

5-hydroxyl-5-methyl-2-hexenoic acid exhibits strong in vitro antifungal activity against Botrytis cinerea, Cladosporium cucumerinum, and Corynespora cassiicola with IC50 values of 32.45, 27.17, and 30.66 mg/L, respectively.
15-MPA is a pinusolide derivative isolated from Biota orientalis leaves that exhibits neuroprotective activity against glutamate-induced toxicity in primary cultured rat cortical cells.
(E)-14,14-dibromotetradeca-2,13-dienoic acid is a ω-brominated lipid present in motualevic acids A and E, and its presence is required for low micromolar antimicrobial activity.
18R-HEPE is produced from EPA by a biosynthetic pathway in human cells, including in vascular endothelial cells treated with aspirin, and is released to be further converted by activated human PMNs.
Fumarate nitrate regulation, mediated by the Fnr protein, is involved in the iron-dependent regulation of the MnSOD gene (sodA) in Escherichia coli.
13-hydroperoxydocosadienoic acid can act as an oxidant in EPR investigations of the iron center in 5-lipoxygenase, enabling complete oxidation of the active site iron.
10R-HOTrE is produced from α-linolenic acid by whole recombinant cells expressing PpoC from A. nidulans, with optimal production at 3 g/l substrate concentration for 60 minutes.

10R-HOTrE can be produced from α-linolenic acid by whole recombinant cells expressing PpoC from A. nidulans under optimized conditions (pH 8.0, 30°C, 250 rpm, 60 g/l cells, 5% DMSO) achieving a conversion yield of 72% (w/w) and a productivity of 4.3 g/l·h from 3 g/l α-linolenic acid in 30 minutes.
Sorbic acid nitrite, given at 7.5–1 mg/kg orally for 30 days, increases mitotic index in mouse bone marrow, indicating an effect on spindle activity.
Sorbic acid nitrite treatment may increase the mitotic index by affecting the spindle apparatus.
2-C-methyl-D-erythronic acid is an oviposition stimulant isolated from the acidic fraction of Toddalia asiatica foliage and, alone, does not elicit positive responses from Papilio polytes females.

2-C-methyl-D-erythronic acid acts as a synergistic stimulant involved in host recognition and/or preference in Papilio polytes, enhancing the response when combined with trans-4-hydroxy-N-methyl-L-proline.

In aqueous phase, 2,3-dihydroxy-2-methylpropanal undergoes radical-driven oxidation with rate constants of k(OH + DHMP) = (1.2 ± 0.1) × 10^9 L mol^-1 s^-1, k(NO3 + DHMP) = (7.9 ± 0.7) × 10^6 L mol^-1 s^-1, and k(SO4- + DHMP) = (3.3 ± 0.2) × 10^7 L mol^-1 s^-1 at 298 K.

4,4-dimethyl-2-oxovaleric acid as a substrate in reductive amination with [Rh[(R,R)-Deguphos](COD)]BF 4 yields N-benzylated amino acids in good to excellent yields and high enantiomeric excess within 2–3 hours.

Alpha-methyl aminoisobutyric acid (MeAIB) is an inhibitor of Na(+)-dependent L-alanine transport in the isolated duodenal mucosa of Gallotia galloti, with its transport completely inhibited by L-alanine.
15-Hydroxyeicosatetraenoic acid acetate is a more potent stimulator of 5-lipoxygenase activity than the free 15-hydroxyl group of 15-HETE.
15-Hydroxyeicosatetraenoic acid acetate is a potent stimulator of 5-lipoxygenase in PT-18 cells.

(5S,12S)-dihydroxy-6,8,10,14-eicosatetraenoic acid inhibits leukotriene B4-induced degranulation and superoxide anion generation in human leukocytes in a dose-dependent manner without affecting cyclic AMP levels.
(5S,12S)-dihydroxy-6,8,10,14-eicosatetraenoic acid modulates the action of leukotriene B4 in human leukocytes.

N-(1H-indazol-5-yl)acetamide (compound 5) is a paracetamol analogue that shows no significant activity at CB1, CB2 cannabinoid receptors, or FAAH, but displays analgesic activity in the mouse formalin test comparable to paracetamol (200 mg/kg).

2-(1H-indazol-6-yl)-1H-benzo[d]imidazol-5-yl benzamide derivatives act as potent FLT3 inhibitors, with the most potent compound (8r) showing nanomolar IC50 and high selectivity over 42 protein kinases.

N-(2-(1H-indazol-6-yl)-1H-benzo[d]imidazol-5-yl)benzamide derivatives can be structurally optimized as potent FLT3 inhibitors, with compound 8r showing strong inhibition of FLT3 and FLT3 mutants relevant to AML.
3-acetamido-5-[I-123]iodo-6-aminoacridine is taken up more avidly by human cancer cells (PC-3) than by normal hamster lung fibroblasts.
3-acetamido-5-[I-123]iodo-6-aminoacridine is an iodinated intercalating agent that interacts with isolated DNA and stains the nuclei of live cultured cells.
4-(1H-indazol-5-yl)-6-phenylpyrimidin-2(1H)one analogs exhibit strong affinity for CDC7 in vitro.
4-(1H-indazol-5-yl)-6-phenylpyrimidin-2(1H)-one is a novel series of compounds that are potent inhibitors of the kinase CDC7.
2-[125I]iodo-5-methoxycarbonylamino-N-acetyltryptamine (2-[125I]MCA-NAT) is a radioligand with higher affinity and selectivity for the melatonin ML 2 receptor site compared to 2-[125I]iodomelatonin.
6-(pyrimidin-4-yl)-1H-indazole derivatives are a series of small molecule compounds with antiproliferative activity against human nasopharyngeal carcinoma cell lines.

6-bromo-N-(1H-indazol-6-yl)quinolin-4-amine is a potent and selective cell-active chemical probe for cyclin G-associated kinase (GAK), with an IC50 of 1.4 µM in cells and improved metabolic stability in liver microsomes.
4-bromopyridyl acetamide at the C7 position of the chromone scaffold is optimal for activity in a series of potent flavone-based inhibitors targeting ABL and ABL T315I.

N-(6-oxo-5,6-dihydrophenanthridin-2-yl)(N,N-dimethylamino)acetamide hydrochloride (PJ34) is an inhibitor of the coronavirus N protein that interferes with both the RNA-binding activity of the N protein and viral replication.

N-(6-oxo-5,6-dihydrophenanthridin-2-yl)-(N,N-dimethylamino)acetamide hydrochloride is a potent PARP inhibitor that, when administered from postnatal day 30 to 50 in Ndufs4 knockout mice, reduces neurological impairment, increases exploratory activity and motor skills, and significantly reduces basal poly(ADP-ribosyl)ation in brain, skeletal muscle, liver, pancreas, and spleen.

N-(6-oxo-5,6-dihydrophenanthridin-2-yl)(N,N-dimethylamino)acetamide hydrochloride (PJ34) is a coronavirus N protein inhibitor that targets the ribonucleotide-binding pocket of the N-terminal domain of the coronavirus N protein, reducing the N protein's RNA-binding affinity and hindering viral replication.
N-(3-iodo-4-methylphenyl)pyrazine-2-carboxamide exhibits antimycobacterial activity with MIC ≤ 2 mg/L against M. tuberculosis.
N-(3-iodo-4-methylphenyl)pyrazine-2-carboxamide is a substituted pyrazinecarboxylic acid amide investigated as an abiotic elicitor of flavonolignan production in callus culture of S. marianum.
N-(3-iodo-4-methyl-phenyl)pyrazine-2-carboxamide exhibits potent in vitro antimycobacterial activity against M. tuberculosis with MIC < 2.0 µmol/L.
N-acetylprocainamide ethobromide is an organic cation whose hepatic handling and biliary excretion kinetics have been investigated in the context of acute renal failure (ARF) in rats.

Hepatic uptake and biliary excretion of the organic cation N-acetylprocainamide ethobromide are not altered in acute renal failure (ARF), suggesting its excretion pathway is resistant to ARF-related dysfunction.
4-pyridinylcarbamate derivatives of alpha-GalCer have been developed to improve the Th1-polarized antigenic profile when the 6'-OH is solvent-exposed.

CEOOH (cholesterol oxidation oxidation product) accumulates in LDL during and after ultracentrifugation-based isolation methods, with levels as low as pmol/mg Ubiquinol-10 Vitamin E LDL protein after centrifugation.
CEOOH isomers are involved in the initiation of lipid peroxidation, which is a key step leading to the formation of oxidized LDL and foam cells associated with atherosclerosis.

CEOOH isomers are formed in oxidized LDL and their distribution can be evaluated using the DPPP-TLC blot/GC-EI-MS method, which aids in understanding the mechanisms contributing to atherogenic oxidized LDL formation in vivo.
Significant accumulation of CEOOH in LDL (up to nmol/mg LDL protein) is associated with a linear decrease in LDL vitamin E content, with up to a 70% loss in some samples.
Removal of EDTA by dialysis leads to rapid accumulation of CEOOH in LDL, even when precautions are taken to minimize oxidation.
1,4-dioxaspiro[2.3]hexane is a versatile building block used in the synthesis of cell signaling and immunomodulatory molecules such as dihydrosphingosines, sphingosines, and phytosphingosines.
CHMDP produces a large decrease in the calcium absorption capacity of the duodenum.
Glycine-C l4 is produced from threonine-1,2-Cl4 by rat liver particles, though less rapidly than by whole liver homogenates.
3-chloro-3-pyridinol is used as a reagent for esterification with carboxylic acids such as methyl-1H-indole-5-carboxylate to synthesize 3-chloropyridinyl ester-derived SARS-CoV-2 3CLpro inhibitors.
TRN undergoes photoisomerization upon exposure to radiation at 420 nm, making it susceptible to photodegradation.
Tetrachloro-2,3,5,6-tetrahydroxybenzene (TPAA) efficiently regenerates LEG4+ dye by electron transfer from TPAA˙+.
Tetrachloro-2,3,5,6-tetrahydroxybenzene (TPAA) forms a TPAA˙+ ion in electrolyte solution, which is associated with a characteristic positive absorption peak at 720 nm.

Glycidol acetate can be resolved by interfacial esterases from marine invertebrates, achieving an enantiomeric ratio of 2.67 (S-selective) when using a biocatalyst derived from Stichodactyla helianthus.
PDC is used as a buffer component at a concentration of 20 mM and pH 4.5 in the buffer solution for electrochemical detection of anions.
PDCl can be quantified in urine samples using the standard additions method or calibration graph method, with prepared solutions ranging in concentration from 5.0 × 10^-6 to 1.0 × 10^-5 M.
DCP can be completely removed from 100 mg/l solution in 60 minutes using 75 mg/l H2O2 and 10 mg/l Fe(II) in the Photo-Fenton process.
1,4-dichlorophenol is used as a terminal electron acceptor in a coupled assay to monitor the activity of succinate dehydrogenase, where its reduction is measured at 578 nm.
1,4-dichlorophenol can be degraded by photocatalysis using TiO2, and its degradation is improved when tungsten is incorporated into TiO2 (W5þ-TiO2).
(2R,4S)-2,4-dihydroxy-2-methyldihydro-furan-3-one (R-DHMF) is a conformation of autoinducer-2 (AI-2) that predominates at higher hydration conditions in solution.
(2R,4S)-2,4-dihydroxy-2-methyldihydrofuran-3-one (R-DHMF) is an epimeric furanone formed by spontaneous cyclization of 4,5-dihydroxy-2,3-pentanedione (DPD) in the AI-2 biosynthetic pathway.
